1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: NEAR HAS COME the reckoning for men, but they turn away in remissness.

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: The people’s reckoning is near, whereas they are in neglect, turned away!

1.author: Arberry
1.text: Nigh unto men has drawn their reckoning, while they in heedlessness are yet turning away;

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: There hath approached unto mankind their reckoning, while they in neglect are turning away.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Draws near for mankind their reckoning, while they turn away in heedlessness.

1.author: Itani
1.text: Mankind’s reckoning has drawn near, but they turn away heedlessly.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: The time of people's reckoning has drawn near, and yet they turn aside in heedlessness.

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: Draws near for mankind their reckoning, while they turn away in heedlessness.

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: Their reckoning draweth nigh for mankind, while they turn away in heedlessness.

1.author: Qarai
1.text: Mankind’s reckoning has drawn near to them, yet they are disregardful in [their] obliviousness.

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: The Reckoning for mankind is drawing near, yet they are heedless and turn away.

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: [The time of] their account has approached for the people, while they are in heedlessness turning away.

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: The people's day of reckoning is drawing closer, yet they are heedlessly neglectful.

1.author: Shakir
1.text: Their reckoning has drawn near to men, and in heedlessness are they turning aside.

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: The time of reckoning has drawn near for mankind, yet they are heedless and turn away.

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: Closer and closer to mankind comes their Reckoning: yet they heed not and they turn away.

1.author: Transliteration
1.text: Iqtaraba lilnnasi hisabuhum wahum fee ghaflatin muAAridoona

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: Never does a new reminder come to them from their Lord but they listen to it with dalliance.

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: Whenever a new advice comes to them from their Lord, they do not listen to it except while playing.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: no Remembrance from their Lord comes to them lately renewed, but they listen to it yet playing,

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: There cometh not unto them a fresh admonition from their Lord but they listen to it while they are playing.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: Comes not unto them an admonition (a chapter of the Quran) from their Lord as a recent revelation but they listen to it while they play,

2.author: Itani
2.text: No fresh reminder comes to them from their Lord, but they listen to it playfully.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: Whenever any fresh admonition comes to them from their Lord they barely heed it and remain immersed in play,

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: Comes not unto them an admonition from their Lord as a recent revelation but they listen to it while they play.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: Never cometh there unto them a new reminder from their Lord but they listen to it while they play,

2.author: Qarai
2.text: There does not come to them any new reminder from their Lord but they listen to it as they play around,

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: No recent revival of the remembrance comes to them from their Lord, except that they play with it as they listen

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: No mention comes to them anew from their Lord except that they listen to it while they are at play

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: Whenever a new revelation comes to them from their Lord, they listen to it in a playful manner,

2.author: Shakir
2.text: There comes not to them a new reminder from their Lord but they hear it while they sport,

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: Whenever any fresh admonition comes to them from their Lord, they listen to it, but do not take it seriously;

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: Never comes (aught) to them of a renewed Message from their Lord, but they listen to it as in jest,-

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Ma yateehim min thikrin min rabbihim muhdathin illa istamaAAoohu wahum yalAAaboona

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: Their minds are lost in frivolous pastimes; and the evil-doers discuss secretly: "Is he not but only a man like you? Then why are you taken in by magic seeingly?"

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Their hearts are involved in play; and the unjust secretly conferred, “What is he, except another a human like you?! So do you follow magic although you have perceived?”

3.author: Arberry
3.text: diverted their hearts. The evildoers whisper one to another, 'Is this aught but a mortal like to yourselves? What, will you take to sorcery with your eyes open?'

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: In sport being their hearts. And those who do wrong keep secret their private discourse this is but a human being like unto yourselves; will ye then betake yourselves to magic while ye know?

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: With their hearts occupied (with evil things) those who do wrong, conceal their private counsels, (saying): "Is this (Muhammad SAW) more than a human being like you? Will you submit to magic while you see it?"

3.author: Itani
3.text: Their hearts distracted, the wrongdoers confer secretly, “Is this anything but a mortal like you? Will you take to sorcery, with open-eyes?”

3.author: Maududi
3.text: their hearts being set on other concerns. The wrong-doers whisper to one another: "This person is no more than a mortal like yourselves. Will you, then, be enchanted by sorcery while you see?"

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: With their hearts occupied. Those who do wrong, conceal their private counsels, (saying): "Is this more than a human being like you Will you submit to magic while you see it"

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: With hearts preoccupied. And they confer in secret. The wrong-doers say: Is this other than a mortal like you? Will ye then succumb to magic when ye see (it)?

3.author: Qarai
3.text: their hearts set on diversions. The wrongdoers secretly whisper together, [saying], ‘Is this [man] not a human being like yourselves? Will you give in to magic with open eyes?’

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: their hearts are diverted. The harmdoers mutter to one another: 'Is this anything else but a human like yourself? What, will you follow sorcery with your eyes open'

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: With their hearts distracted. And those who do wrong conceal their private conversation, [saying], "Is this [Prophet] except a human being like you? So would you approach magic while you are aware [of it]?"

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: and their hearts are preoccupied with trivial matters. The unjust ones whisper to each other and say, "Is he (Muhammad) more than a mere mortal like you? How can you follow that which you know is only a magic?"

3.author: Shakir
3.text: Their hearts trifling; and those who are unjust counsel together in secret: He is nothing but a mortal like yourselves; what! will you then yield to enchantment while you see?

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: their hearts are distracted and forgetful. The wrongdoers confer together secretly, saying, "Is not this man a mortal like you? Will you succumb to magic with your eyes open?"

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: Their hearts toying as with trifles. The wrong-doers conceal their private counsels, (saying), "Is this (one) more than a man like yourselves? Will ye go to witchcraft with your eyes open?"

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Lahiyatan quloobuhum waasarroo alnnajwa allatheena thalamoo hal hatha illa basharun mithlukum afatatoona alssihra waantum tubsiroona

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: He said: "My Lord knows whatever is spoken in the heavens and the earth. He hears all and knows everything."

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: And the Prophet said, “My Lord knows all that is spoken in the heavens and in the earth; and He only is the All Hearing, the All Knowing."

4.author: Arberry
4.text: He says: 'My Lord knows what is said in the heavens and the earth, and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.'

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: The Prophet said: my Lord knoweth the word in the heavens and the earth, and He is the Hearer, the Knower.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: He (Muhammad SAW) said: "My Lord knows (every) word (spoken) in the heavens and on earth. And He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower."

4.author: Itani
4.text: He said, “My Lord knows what is said in the heaven and the earth; and He is the Hearer, the Knower.”

4.author: Maududi
4.text: He said: "My Lord knows well all that is spoken in the heavens and the earth. He is All-Hearing, All- Knowing."

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: He said: "My Lord knows what is said in the heavens and on earth. And He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower."

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: He saith: My Lord knoweth what is spoken in the heaven and the earth. He is the Hearer, the Knower.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: He said, ‘My Lord knows every word [spoken] in the sky and on the earth, and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.’

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: He said: 'My Lord has knowledge of what is said in the heavens and on earth. He is the Hearer, the Knower'

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: The Prophet said, "My Lord knows whatever is said throughout the heaven and earth, and He is the Hearing, the Knowing."

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: The Lord said, "Tell them (Muhammad), 'My Lord knows all that is said in the heavens and the earth. He is All-hearing and All-knowing ".

4.author: Shakir
4.text: He said: My Lord knows what is spoken in the heaven and the earth, and He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: Say, "My Lord knows every word spoken in the heavens and on the earth. He is All Hearing, All Knowing."

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: Say: "My Lord knoweth (every) word (spoken) in the heavens and on earth: He is the One that heareth and knoweth (all things)."

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Qala rabbee yaAAlamu alqawla fee alssamai waalardi wahuwa alssameeAAu alAAaleemu

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: Yet they say: "These are only confused dreams," or rather: "He has invented them;" or: "He is only a poet. Let him therefore bring a miracle to us as the earlier (apostles) were sent with."

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: Rather they said, “These are confused dreams, but in fact he has fabricated it – but in fact he is a poet; so he must bring us some sign, like those who were sent before.”

5.author: Arberry
5.text: Nay, but they say: 'A hotchpotch of nightmares! Nay, he has forged it; nay, he is a poet! Now therefore let him bring us a sign, even as the ancient ones were sent as Messengers.'

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Aye! they say: a medley of dreams; aye! he hath fabricated it; aye! he is a poet; so let him bring us a sign even as the ancients were sent with.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: Nay, they say:"These (revelations of the Quran which are inspired to Muhammad SAW) are mixed up false dreams! Nay, he has invented it! Nay, he is a poet! Let him then bring us an Ayah (sign as a proof) like the ones (Prophets) that were sent before (with signs)!"

5.author: Itani
5.text: And they said, “A jumble of dreams,” and, “He made it up,” and, “He is a poet,” “let him bring us a sign, like those sent to the ancients.”

5.author: Maududi
5.text: They say: "Nay, these are confused dreams; nay, he has forged it; nay, he is a poet. So let him bring us a sign, even as the Messengers of the past were sent with signs."

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: Nay, they say: "These are mixed up false dreams! Nay, he has invented it! -- Nay, he is a poet! Let him then bring us an Ayah like the ones that were sent before!"

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Nay, say they, (these are but) muddled dreams; nay, he hath but invented it; nay, he is but a poet. Let him bring us a portent even as those of old (who were Allah's messengers) were sent (with portents).

5.author: Qarai
5.text: But they said, ‘[They are] muddled dreams!’ ‘Indeed, he has fabricated it!’ ‘Indeed, he is a poet!’ ‘Let him bring us a sign, like those sent to the former generations.’

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: Some say: 'No, it is only mixed dreams' 'No, he has forged it himself', or, 'no, he is a poet! Let him bring us a sign, just as the ancient ones were sent asMessengers'

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: But they say, "[The revelation is but] a mixture of false dreams; rather, he has invented it; rather, he is a poet. So let him bring us a sign just as the previous [messengers] were sent [with miracles]."

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: They have said, "It, (the Quran), is only the result of some confused dreams. He is only a poet. He should show us some miracles, as the ancient Prophets had done."

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Nay! say they: Medleys of dreams; nay! he has forged it; nay! he is a poet; so let him bring to us a sign as the former (prophets) were sent (with).

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: Some say, "These are his confused dreams." Others say, "He has invented it himself," and yet others say, "He is a poet. Let him bring us a sign as previous messengers did."

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: "Nay," they say, "(these are) medleys of dream! - Nay, He forged it! - Nay, He is (but) a poet! Let him then bring us a Sign like the ones that were sent to (Prophets) of old!"

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Bal qaloo adghathu ahlamin bali iftarahu bal huwa shaAAirun falyatina biayatin kama orsila alawwaloona

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: Not one habitation that We destroyed before them had believed. So how can they believe?

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: There is not a township before them which did not believe which We have not destroyed; so will they believe?

6.author: Arberry
6.text: Not one city that We destroyed before them believed; what then, will they not believe?

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: Not a city before them which We destroyed believed; will they then believe?

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: Not one of the towns (populations), of those which We destroyed, believed before them (though We sent them signs), will they then believe?

6.author: Itani
6.text: None of the towns We destroyed before them had believed. Will they, then, believe?

6.author: Maududi
6.text: Not one township that We destroyed before them believed. Would they, then, believe?

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: Not one of the towns of those which We destroyed, believed before them; will they then believe.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: Not a township believed of those which We destroyed before them (though We sent them portents): would they then believe?

6.author: Qarai
6.text: No town that We destroyed before them believed. Will these then have faith [if they are sent signs]?

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: Of the villages We destroyed, there was not one that believed. What, will they not believe?

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: Not a [single] city which We destroyed believed before them, so will they believe?

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: The people of the town whom We had destroyed also had no faith. Will these people then believe in God?

6.author: Shakir
6.text: There did not believe before them any town which We destroyed, will they then believe?

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: Before them, not one of the communities which We destroyed believed either. Will these believe?

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: (As to those) before them, not one of the populations which We destroyed believed: will these believe?

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Ma amanat qablahum min qaryatin ahlaknaha afahum yuminoona

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: Never did We send a message before you but through a man, whom We inspired. If you do not know, then ask the keepers of the oracles of God.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: And We did not send (Prophets) before you except men, to whom We sent divine revelations – therefore, O people, ask the people of knowledge if you do not know.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: And We sent none before thee, but men to whom We made revelation -- question the People of the Remembrance, if you do not know --

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: And We sent not before thee but men unto whom We revealed; so ask the people of the Admonition if ye know not.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: And We sent not before you (O Muhammad SAW) but men to whom We inspired, so ask the people of the Reminder [Scriptures - the Taurat (Torah), the Injeel (Gospel)] if you do not know.

7.author: Itani
7.text: We did not send before you except men, whom We inspired. Ask the people of knowledge, if you do not know.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: (O Muhammad), even before you We never sent any other than human beings as Messengers, and to them We sent revelation. Ask the People of the Book if you do not know.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: And We sent not before you but men to whom We revealed. So ask the people of the Reminder if you do not know.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: And We sent not (as Our messengers) before thee other than men, whom We inspired. Ask the followers of the Reminder if ye know not?

7.author: Qarai
7.text: We did not send [any apostles] before you except as men to whom We revealed. Ask the People of the Reminder if you do not know.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: We never sent (anyone) before you except men to whom We revealed to them. 'Ask the people of the Remembrance if you do not know'

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: And We sent not before you, [O Muhammad], except men to whom We revealed [the message], so ask the people of the message if you do not know.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: The messengers that We had sent before you were only men to whom We had given revelation. Ask the People of the Book if you do not know.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: And We did not send before you any but men to whom We sent revelation, so ask the followers of the reminder if you do not

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: The messengers We sent before you were but men whom We had sent revelations. Ask the People of the Book if you do not know this --

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: Before thee, also, the messengers We sent were but men, to whom We granted inspiration: If ye realise this not, ask of those who possess the Message.

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Wama arsalna qablaka illa rijalan noohee ilayhim faisaloo ahla alththikri in kuntum la taAAlamoona

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: We did not make their bodies immune to hunger, nor were they immortal.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: And We did not create them without bodies so they would not eat food – nor that they abide on earth forever.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: nor did We fashion them as bodies that ate not food, neither were they immortal;

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: And We made them not bodies not eating food; nor were they to be abiders.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: And We did not create them (the Messengers, with) bodies that ate not food, nor were they immortals,

8.author: Itani
8.text: We did not make them mere bodies that ate no food, nor were they immortal.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: We did not endow the Messengers with bodies that would need no food; nor were they immortals.

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: And We did not place them in bodies that did not eat food, nor were they immortals.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: We gave them not bodies that would not eat food, nor were they immortals.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: We did not make them bodies that did not eat food, nor were they immortal.

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: Nor did We make them bodies that ate no food, nor were they immortal.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: And We did not make the prophets forms not eating food, nor were they immortal [on earth].

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: We had not made them such bodies that would not eat any food nor were they immortal.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: And We did not make them bodies not eating the food, and they were not to abide (forever).

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: We did not give them bodies that needed no food, nor were they to live forever.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: Nor did We give them bodies that ate no food, nor were they exempt from death.

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Wama jaAAalnahum jasadan la yakuloona alttaAAama wama kanoo khalideena

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: Then We made Our promise good to them and delivered whomsoever We pleased, and destroyed the transgressors.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: We then fulfilled the promise to them, therefore rescued them and whomever We willed, and destroyed the transgressors.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: then We made true the promise We gave them and We delivered them, and whomsoever We would; and We destroyed the prodigal.

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: Then We made good unto them the premise; so We delivered them and those whom We willed, and We destroyed the extravagant.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: Then We fulfilled to them the promise, and We saved them and those whom We willed, but We destroyed Al-Musrifun (i.e. extravagants in oppression, polytheism and in sin).

9.author: Itani
9.text: Then We fulfilled Our promise to them, and We saved them together with whomever We willed, and We destroyed the extravagant.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: Then We fulfilled the promise We had made to them: We rescued them and those whom We wished, and We destroyed those who exceeded all bounds.

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: Then We fulfilled to them the promise. So We saved them and those whom We willed, but We destroyed Al-Musrifin.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: Then we fulfilled the promise unto them. So we delivered them and whom We would, and We destroyed the prodigals.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: Then We fulfilled Our promise to them, and We delivered them and whomever We wished, and We destroyed the transgressors.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: Then We were true to the promise, We saved them together with those whom We willed, and destroyed the transgressors.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: Then We fulfilled for them the promise, and We saved them and whom We willed and destroyed the transgressors.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: Our promise to them came true and We saved them and those whom We wanted, but destroyed the unjust people.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: Then We made Our promise good to them, so We delivered them and those whom We pleased, and We destroyed the extravagant.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: Then We fulfilled Our promise to them and We saved them and those with them whom We pleased, and We destroyed those who exceeded all bounds.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: In the end We fulfilled to them Our Promise, and We saved them and those whom We pleased, but We destroyed those who transgressed beyond bounds.

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Thumma sadaqnahumu alwaAAda faanjaynahum waman nashao waahlakna almusrifeena

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: We have sent down to you a Book which has a reminder for you. Do you not understand?

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: We have indeed sent down towards you a Book, in which is your repute; so do you not have sense?

10.author: Arberry
10.text: Now We have sent down to you a Book wherein is your Remembrance; will you not understand?

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And assuredly We have sent down unto you a Book wherein is admonition for you; will ye not then reflect?

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: Indeed, We have sent down for you (O mankind) a Book, (the Quran) in which there is Dhikrukum, (your Reminder or an honour for you i.e. honour for the one who follows the teaching of the Quran and acts on its orders). Will you not then understand?

10.author: Itani
10.text: We have sent down to you a Book, containing your message. Do you not understand?

10.author: Maududi
10.text: We have bestowed upon you a Book that mentions you. Do you not understand?

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: Indeed, We have sent down for you a Book in which there is Dhikrukum. Will you not then understand

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Now We have revealed unto you a Scripture wherein is your Reminder. Have ye then no sense?

10.author: Qarai
10.text: Certainly We have sent down to you a Book in which there is an admonition for you. Do you not exercise your reason?

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: Now, We have sent down to you a Book in which is your Remembrance. Will you not understand?

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: We have certainly sent down to you a Book in which is your mention. Then will you not reason?

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: We have sent a Book, (the Quran), which is an honor for you. Will you then not understand?

10.author: Shakir
10.text: Certainly We have revealed to you a Book in which is your good remembrance; what! do you not then understand?

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: We have revealed a Book to you which is admonition for you. Will you not then understand?

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: We have revealed for you (O men!) a book in which is a Message for you: will ye not then understand?

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Laqad anzalna ilaykum kitaban feehi thikrukum afala taAAqiloona

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: How many habitations that were sinful have We demolished utterly, and raised other people after them.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: And many a township did We destroy, for they were unjust, and We created other nations after them.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: How many a city that was evildoing We have shattered, and set up after it another people!

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: And how many a city have We overthrown which were doing wrong, and We caused to grow up thereafter anot her nation!

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: How many a town (community), that were wrong-doers, have We destroyed, and raised up after them another people!

11.author: Itani
11.text: How many a guilty town have We crushed, and established thereafter another people?

11.author: Maududi
11.text: How many a wrong-doing town did We shatter and then raise up another people.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: How many a town given to wrongdoing, have We destroyed, and raised up after them another people!

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: How many a community that dealt unjustly have We shattered, and raised up after them another folk!

11.author: Qarai
11.text: How many a town We have smashed that had been wrongdoing, and We brought forth another people after it.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: How many harmdoing villages have We shattered and replaced them with another nation.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: And how many a city which was unjust have We shattered and produced after it another people.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: How many unjust towns did We destroy and replace them with other nations?

11.author: Shakir
11.text: And how many a town which was iniquitous did We demolish, and We raised up after it another people!

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: How many communities of evil-doers We have destroyed, raising up other people after them.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: How many were the populations We utterly destroyed because of their iniquities, setting up in their places other peoples?

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Wakam qasamna min qaryatin kanat thalimatan waanshana baAAdaha qawman akhareena

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: Whensoever they sensed Our punishment they fled from them.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: And when they tasted Our punishment, they immediately started fleeing from it.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: Then, when they perceived Our might, behold, they ran headlong out of it.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: Then when they perceived Our violence, lo! they were from it fleeing.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: Then, when they perceived (saw) Our Torment (coming), behold, they (tried to) flee from it.

12.author: Itani
12.text: Then, when they sensed Our might, they started running away from it.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: Then, when they perceived Our chastisement they took to their heels and fled.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: Then, when they sensed Our torment, behold, they (tried to) flee from it.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: And, when they felt Our might, behold them fleeing from it!

12.author: Qarai
12.text: So when they sighted Our punishment, behold, they ran away from it.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: And when they felt Our Might they fled from it.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And when its inhabitants perceived Our punishment, at once they fled from it.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: When they found Our torment approaching them they started to run away from the town.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: So when they felt Our punishment, lo! they began to fly

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: When they felt Our punishment coming upon them, they began to flee from it.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: Yet, when they felt Our Punishment (coming), behold, they (tried to) flee from it.

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Falamma ahassoo basana itha hum minha yarkudoona

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: "Do not flee; go back to your halls of pleasure and your habitations, so that you may be interrogated."

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: The angels said to them, “Do not flee and return to the comforts that were given to you and to your homes, perhaps you will be questioned.”

13.author: Arberry
13.text: 'Run not! Return you unto the luxury that you exulted in, and your dwelling-places; haply you shall be questioned.'

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: Flee not, and return unto that wherein ye luxuriated and your habitations, haply ye will be questioned.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: Flee not, but return to that wherein you lived a luxurious life, and to your homes, in order that you may be questioned.

13.author: Itani
13.text: Do not run, but come back to your luxuries, and to your homes, that you may be questioned.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: (They were told): "Flee not, but return to your comforts and to your dwellings. You are likely to be questioned."

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: Flee not, but return to that wherein you lived a luxurious life, and to your homes, in order that you may be questioned.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: (But it was said unto them): Flee not, but return to that (existence) which emasculated you and to your dwellings, that ye may be questioned.

13.author: Qarai
13.text: ‘Do not run away! Return to the opulence you were given to enjoy and to your dwellings so that you may be questioned!’

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: 'Do not run away. Return to your luxury that you rejoiced in, and your homes in order that you be questioned'

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: [Some angels said], "Do not flee but return to where you were given luxury and to your homes - perhaps you will be questioned."

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: We told them, "Do not run away. Come back to your luxuries and your houses so that you can be questioned".

13.author: Shakir
13.text: Do not fly (now) and come back to what you were made to lead easy lives in and to your dwellings, haply you will be questioned.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: They were told, "Do not try to flee, but return to the comfort and luxuries in which you exulted and to the places where you lived, so that you may be questioned."

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: Flee not, but return to the good things of this life which were given you, and to your homes in order that ye may be called to account.

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: La tarkudoo wairjiAAoo ila ma otriftum feehi wamasakinikum laAAallakum tusaloona

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: "Woe, alas," they said, "we were really sinful."

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: They cried, “Woe to us, we were indeed unjust!”

14.author: Arberry
14.text: They said, 'Alas for us! We have been evildoers.'

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: They said: woe unto us! verily we have been wrong-doers.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: They cried: "Woe to us! Certainly! We have been Zalimun (polytheists, wrong-doers and disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah, etc.)."

14.author: Itani
14.text: They said, “Woe to us; we were unfair.”

14.author: Maududi
14.text: They said: "Woe to us; surely we were wrong-doers."

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: They cried: "Woe to us! Certainly we have been wrongdoers."

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: They cried: Alas for us! we were wrong-doers.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: They said, ‘Woe to us! We have indeed been wrongdoers!’

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: They said: 'Alas for us we were harmdoers'

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: They said, "O woe to us! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: They said, "Woe to us! We have been unjust".

14.author: Shakir
14.text: They said: O woe to us! surely we were unjust.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: They said, "Woe to us! We were indeed wrongdoers,"

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: They said: "Ah! woe to us! We were indeed wrong-doers!"

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Qaloo ya waylana inna kunna thalimeena

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: And this remained their lament till We mowed them down and made them extinct.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: So they kept saying this until We made them cut off, extinguished.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: So they ceased not to cry, until We made them stubble, silent and still.

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: And this ceased not to be their cry until We made them a harvestreaped, extinguished.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: And that cry of theirs ceased not, till We made them as a field that is reaped, extinct (dead).

15.author: Itani
15.text: This continued to be their cry, until We made them silent ashes.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: They did not cease to cry this until We reduced them to stubble, still and silent.

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: And that cry of theirs ceased not, till We made them as a field that is reaped, extinct.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: And this their crying ceased not till We made them as reaped corn, extinct.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: That remained their cry until We turned them into a mown field, stilled [like burnt ashes].

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: And this they did not stop crying out until We made them stubble, silent, and still.

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: And that declaration of theirs did not cease until We made them [as] a harvest [mowed down], extinguished [like a fire].

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: Such was what they continued to say until We mowed them down and made them completely extinct.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: And this ceased not to be their cry till We made them cut off, extinct.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: and this they kept repeating until We caused them to become like a field mowed down, and reduced to ashes.

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: And that cry of theirs ceased not, till We made them as a field that is mown, as ashes silent and quenched.

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Fama zalat tilka daAAwahum hatta jaAAalnahum haseedan khamideena

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: We have not created the heavens and the earth, and all that lies between them, out of fun.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And We have not created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, unnecessarily.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: We created not the heaven and the earth, and whatsoever between them is, as playing;

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And We created not the heaven and the earth and that which is in between in play.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: We created not the heavens and the earth and all that is between them for a (mere) play.

16.author: Itani
16.text: We did not create the sky and the earth and what is between them for amusement.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: We did not create in sport the heavens and the earth and all that lies between the two.

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: We created not the heavens and the earth and all that is between them for play.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: We created not the heaven and the earth and all that is between them in play.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: We did not create the sky and the earth and whatever is between them for play.

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: It was not in play that We created the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them.

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And We did not create the heaven and earth and that between them in play.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: We did not create the heavens and the earth just for fun.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And We did not create the heaven and the earth and what is between them for sport.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: It was not in play that We created the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them.

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: Not for (idle) sport did We create the heavens and the earth and all that is between!

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Wama khalaqna alssamaa waalarda wama baynahuma laAAibeena

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: If We had pleased to make a plaything We could have made it Ourself, if We had cared to do so.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: If We willed to choose a pastime, We could have chosen it from Ourselves – if We wanted to.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: had We desired to take to Us a diversion We would have taken it to Us from Ourselves, had We done aught.

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Had We intended that We should choose a sport, surely We would choose it from before Us--if We were ever going to do that.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: Had We intended to take a pastime (i.e. a wife or a son, etc.), We could surely have taken it from Us, if We were going to do (that).

17.author: Itani
17.text: If We wanted amusement, We could have found it within Us, were We to do so.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: Had it been Our will to find a pastime, We would have found one near at hand; if at all We were inclined to do so.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: Had We intended to take a pastime, We could surely have taken it from Us, if We were going to do (that).

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: If We had wished to find a pastime, We could have found it in Our presence - if We ever did.

17.author: Qarai
17.text: Had We desired to take up some diversion We would have taken it up with Ourselves, were We to do [so].

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: Had We wished to take to Us an amusement We would have taken it to Us from Ours had We done so.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: Had We intended to take a diversion, We could have taken it from [what is] with Us - if [indeed] We were to do so.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: Had We wanted to play games, We could have certainly done so with things at hand.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Had We wished to make a diversion, We would have made it from before Ourselves: by no means would We do (it).

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: Had We wished to find a pastime, We would surely have found it in that which is with Us, if such had been Our will.

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: If it had been Our wish to take (just) a pastime, We should surely have taken it from the things nearest to Us, if We would do (such a thing)!

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Law aradna an nattakhitha lahwan laittakhathnahu min ladunna in kunna faAAileena

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: In fact We strike the truth against the false, which shatters it, and it disappears. Woe to you for what you attribute (to Him)!

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: But in fact We hurl the truth upon falsehood, so it scatters its brains – thereupon it vanishes; and for you is the ruin due to the matters you fabricate.

18.author: Arberry
18.text: Nay, but We hurl the truth against falsehood and it prevails over it, and behold, falsehood vanishes away. Then woe to you for that you describe!

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: Aye! We hurl truth against false hood, so that it braineth it, and lo! it vanisheth; and to you be the woe for that which ye utter!

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: Nay, We fling (send down) the truth (this Quran) against the falsehood (disbelief), so it destroys it, and behold, it (falsehood) is vanished. And woe to you for that (lie) which you ascribe (to Us) (against Allah by uttering that Allah has a wife and a son).

18.author: Itani
18.text: In fact, We hurl the truth against falsehood, and it crushes it, so it vanishes. Woe unto you, for what you describe.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: Nay, We hurl the Truth at falsehood so that the Truth crushes falsehood, and lo! it vanishes. Woe to you for what you utter!

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: Nay, We fling the truth against the falsehood, so it destroys it, and behold, it disappears. And woe to you for that which you ascribe.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Nay, but We hurl the true against the false, and it doth break its head and lo! it vanisheth. And yours will be woe for that which ye ascribe (unto Him).

18.author: Qarai
18.text: Indeed, We hurl the truth against falsehood, and it crushes its head, and behold, falsehood vanishes! And woe to you for what you allege [about Allah].

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: No, We hurl truth at falsehood, and it shall conquer it, and see, falsehood vanishes. Woe to you for all you have described.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: Rather, We dash the truth upon falsehood, and it destroys it, and thereupon it departs. And for you is destruction from that which you describe.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: We bring forward the Truth to crush and destroy falsehood; it is doomed to be banished. Woe to you for your way of thinking about God!

18.author: Shakir
18.text: Nay! We cast the truth against the falsehood, so that it breaks its head, and lo! it vanishes; and woe to you for what you describe;

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: We will hurl the truth at falsehood, the falsehood shall be crushed and will disappear. Woe to you for what you utter.

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: Nay, We hurl the Truth against falsehood, and it knocks out its brain, and behold, falsehood doth perish! Ah! woe be to you for the (false) things ye ascribe (to Us).

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Bal naqthifu bialhaqqi AAala albatili fayadmaghuhu faitha huwa zahiqun walakumu alwaylu mimma tasifoona

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: Whosoever is in the heavens and the earth belongs to Him; and those who are near Him do not disdain to worship Him or weary (of His service),

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: And to Him only belong all those who are in the heavens and in the earth; and those with Him are not conceited towards worshipping Him, nor do they tire.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: To Him belongs whosoever is in the heavens and the earth; and those who are with Him wax not too proud to do Him service neither grow weary,

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: And His is whosoever is in the heavens and the earth; and those nigh unto Him are not too stiff-necked for His worship nor are they weary.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: To Him belongs whosoever is in the heavens and on earth. And those who are near Him (i.e. the angels) are not too proud to worship Him, nor are they weary (of His worship).

19.author: Itani
19.text: To Him belongs everyone in the heavens and the earth. Those near Him are not too proud to worship Him, nor do they waver.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: To Him belongs whosoever dwells in the heavens and on earth. Those (angels) that are with Him neither disdain to serve Him out of pride, nor do they weary of it.

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: To Him belongs whosoever is in the heavens and on earth. And those who are near Him are not too proud to worship Him, nor are they weary.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: Unto Him belongeth whosoever is in the heavens and the earth. And those who dwell in His presence are not too proud to worship Him, nor do they weary;

19.author: Qarai
19.text: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth, and those who are near Him do not disdain to worship Him, nor do they become weary.

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: To Him belongs whosoever is in the heavens and the earth. Those who are with Him are not too proud to worship Him, nor are they ever wearied.

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: To Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. And those near Him are not prevented by arrogance from His worship, nor do they tire.

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: To Him belongs all those who are in the heavens and the earth. Those who are closer to Him are not too proud to worship Him, nor do they get tired of worshipping.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And whoever is in the heavens and the earth is His; and those who are with Him are not proud to serve Him, nor do they grow weary.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: To Him belongs whosoever is in the heavens and on the earth and those that are with Him are never too proud to worship Him, nor do they grow weary;

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: To Him belong all (creatures) in the heavens and on earth: Even those who are in His (very) Presence are not too proud to serve Him, nor are they (ever) weary (of His service):

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Walahu man fee alssamawati waalardi waman AAindahu la yastakbiroona AAan AAibadatihi wala yastahsiroona

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: Nor cease to endeavour praising Him night and day.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: They say His Purity night and day, and do not slacken.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: glorifying Him by night and in the daytime and never failing.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: And they hallow Him night and day, and they flag not.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: They (i.e. the angels) glorify His Praises night and day, (and) they never slacken (to do so).

20.author: Itani
20.text: They praise night and day, without ever tiring.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: They glorify Him night and day, without flagging.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: They glorify His praises night and day, they never slacken.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: They glorify (Him) night and day; they flag not.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: They glorify [Him] night and day, and they do not flag.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: They never fail to exalt Him either at night or in the day.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: They exalt [Him] night and day [and] do not slacken.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: They glorify Him day and night without fail.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: They glorify (Him) by night and day; they are never languid.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: they glorify Him night and day without tiring.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: They celebrate His praises night and day, nor do they ever flag or intermit.

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Or have they taken gods from the earth who can raise the dead?

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: Have they appointed from the earth, Gods that create something?

21.author: Arberry
21.text: Or have they taken gods out of the earth who raise the dead?

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: Have they taken gods from the earth who raise the deed?

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: Or have they taken (for worship) aliha (gods) from the earth who raise the dead?

21.author: Itani
21.text: Or have they taken to themselves gods from the earth who resurrect?

21.author: Maududi
21.text: Have they taken earthly gods who are such that they raise up the dead to life?

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: Or have they taken (for worship) gods from the earth who raise the dead

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: Or have they chosen gods from the earth who raise the dead?

21.author: Qarai
21.text: Have they taken gods from the earth who raise [the dead]?

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: Or, have they taken earthly gods who revive the dead?

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: Or have men taken for themselves gods from the earth who resurrect [the dead]?

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: Have they chosen deities from earth? Can such deities give life to anyone?

21.author: Shakir
21.text: Or have they taken gods from the earth who raise (the dead).

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: Have they taken deities from the earth who can bring the dead to life?

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: Or have they taken (for worship) gods from the earth who can raise (the dead)?

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: Ami ittakhathoo alihatan mina alardi hum yunshiroona

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: Had there been gods apart from God, both (the heavens and the earth) would have been despoiled. Much too glorious is God, the Lord of the mighty throne, for things they assert!

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: If other than Allah, there were Gods* in the heavens and the earth, they would be destroyed; therefore Purity is to Allah, Owner of the Throne, from the matters that they fabricate. (* Which is not possible. ** The heavens and the earth.)

22.author: Arberry
22.text: Why, were there gods in earth and heaven other than God, they would surely go to ruin; so glory be to God, the Lord of the Throne, above that they describe!

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: Had there been in between the twain gods except Allah surely the twain would have gone to ruin. Hallowed be Allah, the Lord of the Throne, from that which they utter!

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: Had there been therein (in the heavens and the earth) gods besides Allah, then verily both would have been ruined. Glorified be Allah, the Lord of the Throne, (High is He) above what they attribute to Him!

22.author: Itani
22.text: If there were in them gods other than God, they would have gone to ruin. So glory be to God, Lord of the Throne, beyond what they allege.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: Had there been any gods in the heavens and the earth apart from Allah, the order of both the heavens and the earth would have gone to ruins. Glory be to Allah, the Lord of the Throne, Who is far above their false descriptions of Him.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: Had there been therein (in the heavens and the earth) gods besides Allah, then verily, both would have been ruined. Glorified be Allah, the Lord of the Throne, (High is He) above all that (evil) they associate with Him!

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: If there were therein gods beside Allah, then verily both (the heavens and the earth) had been disordered. Glorified be Allah, the Lord of the Throne, from all that they ascribe (unto Him).

22.author: Qarai
22.text: Had there been any gods in them other than Allah, they would surely have fallen apart. Clear is Allah, the Lord of the Throne, of what they allege [concerning Him].

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: Had there been gods in heaven or earth, other than Allah, both would indeed have been ruined. Exalted be Allah, Lord of the Throne, above that they describe.

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: Had there been within the heavens and earth gods besides Allah, they both would have been ruined. So exalted is Allah, Lord of the Throne, above what they describe.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: Had there been other deities in the heavens and the earth besides God, both the heavens and the earth would have been destroyed. God, the Lord of the Throne, is too Glorious to be as they think He is.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: If there had been in them any gods except Allah, they would both have certainly been in a state of disorder; therefore glory be to Allah, the Lord of the dominion, above what they attribute (to Him).

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: If there had been in the heavens and on the earth, other deities besides God, both the heavens and earth would be ruined. God, Lord of the throne, is far above that which they ascribe to Him.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: If there were, in the heavens and the earth, other gods besides Allah, there would have been confusion in both! but glory to Allah, the Lord of the Throne: (High is He) above what they attribute to Him!

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Law kana feehima alihatun illa Allahu lafasadata fasubhana Allahi rabbi alAAarshi AAamma yasifoona

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: He cannot be questioned about what He does, but they will be questioned.

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: He is not questioned whatever He does, whereas they will all be questioned.

23.author: Arberry
23.text: He shall not be questioned as to what He does, but they shall he questioned.

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: He shall be questioned not as to that which He doth, while they shall be questioned.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: He cannot be questioned as to what He does, while they will be questioned.

23.author: Itani
23.text: He will not be questioned about what He does, but they will be questioned.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: None shall question Him about what He does, but they shall be questioned.

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: He cannot be questioned as to what He does, while they will be questioned.

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: He will not be questioned as to that which He doeth, but they will be questioned.

23.author: Qarai
23.text: He is not questioned concerning what He does, but they are questioned.

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: He is not to be questioned about what He does, but they shall be questioned.

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: He is not questioned about what He does, but they will be questioned.

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: He will not be questioned about anything He does, but all people will be questioned about their deeds.

23.author: Shakir
23.text: He cannot be questioned concerning what He does and they shall be questioned.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: None shall question Him about His works, but they shall be questioned.

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: He cannot be questioned for His acts, but they will be questioned (for theirs).

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: La yusalu AAamma yafAAalu wahum yusaloona

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: Have they taken gods besides God? Say: "Then bring your proof. Here is the Book of those who are with me, and the Book of those who have gone before me." But most men do not know the truth and turn away.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: Or have they set up other Gods besides Allah? Say, “Bring your proof; this is the remembrance of those with me and those before me”; but in fact most of them do not know the Truth, so they turn away.

24.author: Arberry
24.text: Or have they taken gods apart from Him? Say: 'Bring your proof! This is the Remembrance of him who is with me, and the Remembrance of those before me. Nay, but the most part of them know not the truth, so therefore they are turning away.

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Have they taken gods beside Him? Say thou: forth with your proof This is an admonition unto those with me and an admonition unto those before me. But most of them knew not the truth, and so they are averters.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: Or have they taken for worship (other) aliha (gods) besides Him? Say: "Bring your proof:" This (the Quran) is the Reminder for those with me and the Reminder for those before me. But most of them know not the Truth, so they are averse.

24.author: Itani
24.text: Or have they taken, besides Him, other gods? Say, “Bring your proof. This is a message for those with me, and a message of those before me.” But most of them do not know the truth, so they turn away.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: Have they taken gods other than Him? Say, (O Muhammad): "Bring forth your proof! Here is the Book with admonition for those of my time and there are also scriptures with admonition for people before me." But most people do not know the Truth, and have, therefore, turned away from it.

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: Or have they taken for worship gods besides Him Say: "Bring your proof. This is the Reminder for those with me and the Reminder for those before me." But most of them know not the Truth, so they are averse.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: Or have they chosen other gods beside Him? say: Bring your proof (of their godhead). This is the Reminder of those with me and those before me, but most of them know not the Truth and so they are averse.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: Have they taken gods besides Him? Say, ‘Produce your evidence! This is a precept of those who are with me, and a precept of those [who went] before me.’ But most of them do not know the truth, and so they are disregardful.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: Have they taken gods, other than Him? Say: 'Bring us your proof! Here is the Remembrance of he who is with me and the Remembrance of those before me' But no, most of them do not know the truth, therefore they turn away.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: Or have they taken gods besides Him? Say, [O Muhammad], "Produce your proof. This [Qur'an] is the message for those with me and the message of those before me." But most of them do not know the truth, so they are turning away.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: Have they chosen other gods besides God? (Muhammad), ask them, "Show the proof (in support of such belief). This is (the Quran) which tells us about the (beliefs of the people) in my time and those who lived before me." Most of them do not know. Moreover, the truth is that they neglect (the question of belief altogether).

24.author: Shakir
24.text: Or, have they taken gods besides Him? Say: Bring your proof; this is the reminder of those with me and the reminder of those before me. Nay! most of them do not know the truth, so they turn aside.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: Have they taken other deities besides Him? Say to them, "Bring your proofs. This is the reminder of those who are with me and the reminder of those who were before me." But most of them do not know the truth, and so they turn away from it.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: Or have they taken for worship (other) gods besides him? Say, "Bring your convincing proof: this is the Message of those with me and the Message of those before me." But most of them know not the Truth, and so turn away.

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Ami ittakhathoo min doonihi alihatan qul hatoo burhanakum hatha thikru man maAAiya wathikru man qablee bal aktharuhum la yaAAlamoona alhaqqa fahum muAAridoona

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: We have not sent an apostle before you without instructing him that there is no god but I, so worship Me.

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: And We did not send any Noble Messenger before you, but We divinely revealed to him that, “There is no God except I (Allah), therefore worship Me alone.”

25.author: Arberry
25.text: And We sent never a Messenger before thee except that We revealed to him, saying, 'There is no god but I; so serve Me.'

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: And We sent not before thee an apostle but We revealed unto him that there is no god but I, so worship Me.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: And We did not send any Messenger before you (O Muhammad SAW) but We inspired him (saying): La ilaha illa Ana [none has the right to be worshipped but I (Allah)], so worship Me (Alone and none else)."

25.author: Itani
25.text: We never sent a messenger before you without inspiring him that: “There is no god but I, so worship Me.”

25.author: Maududi
25.text: Never did We send any Messenger before you to whom We did not reveal: "There is no god but Me. So serve Me alone."

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: And We did not send any Messenger before you but We revealed to him (saying): "There is no God but I, so worship Me."

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: And We sent no messenger before thee but We inspired him, (saying): There is no Allah save Me (Allah), so worship Me.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: We did not send any apostle before you but that We revealed to him that ‘There is no god except Me; so worship Me.’

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: We never sent a Messenger before you except that We revealed to him saying: 'There is no god except Me, therefore, worship Me'

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: And We sent not before you any messenger except that We revealed to him that, "There is no deity except Me, so worship Me."

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: To all the Messengers that were sent before you We revealed that I am the only God to be worshipped.

25.author: Shakir
25.text: And We did not send before you any apostle but We revealed to him that there is no god but Me, therefore serve Me.

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: We sent all messengers before you with this revelation: "There is no deity save Me, so worship Me alone."

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: Not a messenger did We send before thee without this inspiration sent by Us to him: that there is no god but I; therefore worship and serve Me.

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Wama arsalna min qablika min rasoolin illa noohee ilayhi annahu la ilaha illa ana faoAAbudooni

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: And yet they say: "Ar-Rahman has begotten a son." Too exalted is He! In fact, those (they call His sons) were His honoured votaries.

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: And they said, “The Most Gracious has chosen a son – Purity is to Him! In fact they are honourable bondmen.”

26.author: Arberry
26.text: They say:' 'The All-merciful has taken to Him a son.' Glory be to Him! Nay, but they are honoured servants

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: And they say; the Compaionate hath taken a son. Hallowed be He! aye! they are bondmen honoured.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: And they say: "The Most Beneficent (Allah) has begotten a son (or children)." Glory to Him! They [those whom they call children of Allah i.e. the angels, 'Iesa (Jesus) son of Maryam (Mary), 'Uzair (Ezra), etc.], are but honoured slaves.

26.author: Itani
26.text: And they say, “The Most Merciful has taken to himself a son.” Be He glorified; they are but honored servants.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: They say: "The Most Compassionate Lord has taken to Himself a son." Glory be to Him! Those whom they so designate are only His honoured servants.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: And they say: "The Most Gracious has begotten children." Glory to Him! They are but honored servants.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: And they say: The Beneficent hath taken unto Himself a son. Be He Glorified! Nay, but (those whom they call sons) are honoured slaves;

26.author: Qarai
26.text: They say, ‘The All-beneficent has taken offsprings.’ Immaculate is He! Indeed, they are [His] honoured servants.

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: They say 'The Merciful has taken a son' Exaltations to Him! No, they are only His honored worshipers,

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: And they say, "The Most Merciful has taken a son." Exalted is He! Rather, they are [but] honored servants.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: They said, "The Beneficent God has given birth to a son. He is too Exalted to give birth to a son." (Those whom they think are God's sons) are only His honorable servants.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: And they say: The Beneficent Allah has taken to Himself a son. Glory be to Him. Nay! they are honored servants

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: They say, "The All Merciful has taken a son!" Glory be to Him! They are only His honoured servants:

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: And they say: "(Allah) Most Gracious has begotten offspring." Glory to Him! they are (but) servants raised to honour.

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Waqaloo ittakhatha alrrahmanu waladan subhanahu bal AAibadun mukramoona

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: They did not precede Him in their speech, and acted on His command.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: They do not speak before He has, and they act only at His command.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: that outstrip Him not in speech, and perform as He commands.

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: They precede Him not in words, and by His command they work.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: They speak not until He has spoken, and they act on His Command.

27.author: Itani
27.text: They never speak before He has spoken, and they only act on His command.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: They do not outstrip Him in speech and only act as He commands.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: They speak not until He has spoken, and they act on His command.

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: They speak not until He hath spoken, and they act by His command.

27.author: Qarai
27.text: They do not venture to speak ahead of Him, and they act by His command.

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: who do not surpass Him in speech, and do as He commands.

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: They cannot precede Him in word, and they act by His command.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: These servants do not speak before He speaks. They simply act according to His orders.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: They do not precede Him in speech and (only) according to His commandment do they act.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: they do not try to speak ahead of Him, and they act at His command.

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: They speak not before He speaks, and they act (in all things) by His Command.

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: La yasbiqoonahu bialqawli wahum biamrihi yaAAmaloona

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: He knows what was there before them and what came after them; and they did not intercede for any one but whom He willed, and they were filled with awe of Him.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: He knows what is before them and what is behind them, and they do not intercede except for him whom He likes, and they fear with awe of Him. (The Holy Prophets and virtuous people will be given the permission to intercede. Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him – will be the first to intercede.)

28.author: Arberry
28.text: He knows what is before them and behind them, and they intercede not save for him with whom He is well-pleased, and they tremble in awe of Him.

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: He knoweth whatsoever is before them and whatsoever is behind them; and they intercede not except for him whom He approveth, and in awe of Him they are fearful.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: He knows what is before them, and what is behind them, and they cannot intercede except for him with whom He is pleased. And they stand in awe for fear of Him.

28.author: Itani
28.text: He knows what is before them, and what is behind them; and they do not intercede except for him whom He approves; and they tremble in awe of Him.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: He knows whatever is before them and whatsoever is remote from them and they do not intercede except for him, intercession on whose behalf pleases Him, and they stand constantly in awe of Him.

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: He knows what is before them, and what is behind them, and they cannot intercede except for him with whom He is pleased. And they stand in awe for fear of Him.

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: He knoweth what is before them and what is behind them, and they cannot intercede except for him whom He accepteth, and they quake for awe of Him.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: He knows that which is before them and that which is behind them, and they do not intercede except for someone He approves of, and they are apprehensive for the fear of Him.

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: He knows what is before them and what is behind them. They intercede for none except for him whom He is well pleased, and they tremble in awe of Him.

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: He knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, and they cannot intercede except on behalf of one whom He approves. And they, from fear of Him, are apprehensive.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: He knows all that is in front of them and all that is behind them. (These servants of God) will not intercede with Him for anyone without His permission and they tremble in awe (before His greatness).

28.author: Shakir
28.text: He knows what is before them and what is behind them, and they do not intercede except for him whom He approves and for fear of Him they tremble.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: He knows what is before them and what is behind them, and they cannot intercede without His permission. Indeed they themselves stand in awe of Him.

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: He knows what is before them, and what is behind them, and they offer no intercession except for those who are acceptable, and they stand in awe and reverence of His (Glory).

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: YaAAlamu ma bayna aydeehim wama khalfahum wala yashfaAAoona illa limani irtada wahum min khashyatihi mushfiqoona

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: If any one of them said: "I am God besides Him," We should award him Hell; for this is how We requite the evil-doers.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: And the one among them who says, “I am a God beside Allah” – We shall reward him with hell; this is how We punish the unjust.

29.author: Arberry
29.text: If any of them says, 'I am a god apart from Him', such a one We recompense with Gehenna; even so We recompense the evildoers.

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: And whosoever of them should say: verily I am a god beside Him, such a one We shall requite with Hell; Thus We requite the wrong- doers.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: And if any of them should say: "Verily, I am an ilah (a god) besides Him (Allah)," such a one We should recompense with Hell. Thus We recompense the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc.).

29.author: Itani
29.text: And whoever of them says, “I am a god besides Him,” We will reward him with Hell. Thus We reward the wrongdoers.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: And if anyone of them were to claim: "Indeed I am a god apart from Him," We shall recompense both with Hell. Thus do We recompense the wrong-doers.

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: And if any of them should say: "Verily, I am a god besides Him," such We should recompense with Hell. Thus We recompense the wrongdoers.

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: And one of them who should say: Lo! I am a god beside Him, that one We should repay with hell. Thus We Repay wrong-doers.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: Should any of them say, ‘I am a god besides Him,’ We will requite him with hell. Thus do We requite the wrongdoers.

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: If any one of them says: 'I am a god other than Him' We will recompense him with Gehenna (Hell). As such We recompense the harmdoers.

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: And whoever of them should say, "Indeed, I am a god besides Him"- that one We would recompense with Hell. Thus do We recompense the wrongdoers.

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: The recompense of those of them who say that they are the Lord instead of God will be hell; thus, do We recompense the unjust ones.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: And whoever of them should say: Surely I am a god besides Him, such a one do We recompense with hell; thus do, We recompense the unjust.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: Whoever of them should say, "I am a deity besides Him," shall be requited with Hell. Thus do We reward the wrongdoers.

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: If any of them should say, "I am a god besides Him", such a one We should reward with Hell: thus do We reward those who do wrong.

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Waman yaqul minhum innee ilahun min doonihi fathalika najzeehi jahannama kathalika najzee alththalimeena

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: Do not these unbelievers see that the heavens and the earth were an integrated mass, then We split them and made every living thing from water? Will they not believe even then?

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: Did not the disbelievers observe that the heavens and the earth were together, so We parted them, and we made every living thing from water? So will they not accept faith?

30.author: Arberry
30.text: Have not the unbelievers then beheld that the heavens and the earth were a mass all sewn up, and then We unstitched them and of water fashioned every living thing? Will they not believe?

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: Have not these who disbelieve considered that the heavens and the earth were closed up, then We rent them? And We have made of water everything living; will they not then believe?

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: Have not those who disbelieve known that the heavens and the earth were joined together as one united piece, then We parted them? And We have made from water every living thing. Will they not then believe?

30.author: Itani
30.text: Do the disbelievers not see that the heavens and the earth were one mass, and We tore them apart? And We made from water every living thing. Will they not believe?

30.author: Maududi
30.text: Did the unbelievers (who do not accept the teaching of the Prophet) not realize that the heavens and the earth were one solid mass, then We tore them apart, and We made every living being out of water? Will they, then, not believe (that We created all this)?

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: Have not those who disbelieve known that the heavens and the earth were joined together as one united piece, then We parted them And We have made from water every living thing. Will they not then believe

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: Have not those who disbelieve known that the heavens and the earth were of one piece, then We parted them, and we made every living thing of water? Will they not then believe?

30.author: Qarai
30.text: Have the faithless not regarded that the heavens and the earth were interwoven and We unravelled them, and We made every living thing out of water? Will they not then have faith?

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: Have the unbelievers not seen that the heavens and the earth were sewn up as one (solid) mass, then We unstitched them, and that We made every living thing of water? Will they not believe!

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: Have those who disbelieved not considered that the heavens and the earth were a joined entity, and We separated them and made from water every living thing? Then will they not believe?

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: Have the unbelievers not ever considered that the heavens and the earth were one piece and that We tore them apart from one another. From water We have created all living things. Will they then have no faith?

30.author: Shakir
30.text: Do not those who disbelieve see that the heavens and the earth were closed up, but We have opened them; and We have made of water everything living, will they not then believe?

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: Do not those who deny the truth see that the heavens and the earth were joined together and that We then split them asunder? And that We have made every living thing out of water? Will they still not believe?

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: Do not the Unbelievers see that the heavens and the earth were joined together (as one unit of creation), before we clove them asunder? We made from water every living thing. Will they not then believe?

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Awalam yara allatheena kafaroo anna alssamawati waalarda kanata ratqan fafataqnahuma wajaAAalna mina almai kulla shayin hayyin afala yuminoona

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: We placed stabilisers in the earth so that as it revolved with them you lived undisturbed; and We provided passageways between them so that men may find their way;

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: And We have placed mountains as anchors in the earth so that it may not shake with them; and We kept wide roads in it, so that they may find guidance.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: And We set in the earth firm mountains lest it should shake with them, and We set in it ravines to serve as ways, that haply so they may be guided;

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And We have placed in the earth firm mountains lest it should move away with them, and We placed therein Passages for paths, that haply they may be guided.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: And We have placed on the earth firm mountains, lest it should shake with them, and We placed therein broad highways for them to pass through, that they may be guided.

31.author: Itani
31.text: And We placed on earth stabilizers, lest it sways with them, and We placed therein signposts and passages, that they may be guided.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: And We placed firm mountains on earth lest it should sway with them, and We made wide paths in them that they may find their way;

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: And We have placed on the earth firm mountains, lest it should shake with them, and We placed therein broad highways for them to pass through, that they may be guided.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: And We have placed in the earth firm hills lest it quake with them, and We have placed therein ravines as roads that haply they may find their way.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: We set firm mountains in the earth lest it should shake with them, and We made broad ways in them so that they may be guided [to their destinations].

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: We set firm mountains upon the earth lest it should shake with them, and We placed therein ravines to be paths so that they might be guided.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: And We placed within the earth firmly set mountains, lest it should shift with them, and We made therein [mountain] passes [as] roads that they might be guided.

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: We placed firm mountains on earth lest it would shake them away. We made wide roads for them so that they might have the right guidance.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: And We have made great mountains in the earth lest it might be convulsed with them, and We have made in it wide ways that they may follow a right direction.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: We set firm mountains upon the earth lest it should sway under them, and We placed therein passages for paths so that they might find their way.

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: And We have set on the earth mountains standing firm, lest it should shake with them, and We have made therein broad highways (between mountains) for them to pass through: that they may receive Guidance.

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: WajaAAalna fee alardi rawasiya an tameeda bihim wajaAAalna feeha fijajan subulan laAAallahum yahtadoona

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: And We made the sky a well-protected roof. Still they turn away from His signs!

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: And We have made the sky a roof, protected; and they turn away from its signs.

32.author: Arberry
32.text: and We set up the heaven as a roof well-protected; yet still from Our signs they are turning away.

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: And We have made the heaven a roof, safe, and from the signs there of they are averters.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: And We have made the heaven a roof, safe and well guarded. Yet they turn away from its signs (i.e. sun, moon, winds, clouds, etc.).

32.author: Itani
32.text: And We made the sky a protected ceiling; yet they turn away from its wonders.

32.author: Maududi
32.text: and We made the sky a secure canopy; and yet they turn away from these Signs.

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: And We have made the heaven a roof, safe and well-guarded. Yet they turn away from its signs.

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And we have made the sky a roof withheld (from them). Yet they turn away from its portents.

32.author: Qarai
32.text: We made the sky a preserved roof and yet they are disregardful of its signs.

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
