32.text: We established the heaven as a well protected roof, yet they still turn away from its signs.

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: And We made the sky a protected ceiling, but they, from its signs, are turning away.

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: We made the sky above them as a well-guarded ceiling, but they have neglected the evidence (of Our existence) therein.

32.author: Shakir
32.text: And We have made the heaven a guarded canopy and (yet) they turn aside from its signs.

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: We have made the heaven a well-secured canopy; yet still they turn away from Our signs.

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: And We have made the heavens as a canopy well guarded: yet do they turn away from the Signs which these things (point to)!

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: WajaAAalna alssamaa saqfan mahfoothan wahum AAan ayatiha muAAridoona

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: It is He who created night and day, the sun and the moon, revolving on its orbit.

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: And it is He Who created the night and the day, and the sun and the moon; each one floats in an orbit.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: It is He who created the night and the day, the sun and the moon, each swimming in a sky.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: And He it is Who hath created the night and the day, and the sun and the moon, each in an orb floating.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: And He it is Who has created the night and the day, and the sun and the moon, each in an orbit floating.

33.author: Itani
33.text: It is He who created the night and the day, and the sun and the moon; each floating in an orbit.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: It is He Who created the night and the day, and the sun and the moon. Each of them is floating in its orbit.

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: And He it is Who has created the night and the day, and the sun and the moon, each in an orbit floating.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: And He it is Who created the night and the day, and the sun and the moon. They float, each in an orbit.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: It is He who created the night and the day, the sun and the moon, each swimming in an orbit.

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: It is He who created the night and the day, and the sun and the moon; each floating in an orbit.

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: And it is He who created the night and the day and the sun and the moon; all [heavenly bodies] in an orbit are swimming.

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: It is God who has created the night, the day, the Sun, and Moon and has made them swim in a certain orbit.

33.author: Shakir
33.text: And He it is Who created the night and the day and the sun and the moon; all (orbs) travel along swiftly in their celestial spheres.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: It is He who created the night and the day, and the sun and the moon, each gliding in its orbit.

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: It is He Who created the Night and the Day, and the sun and the moon: all (the celestial bodies) swim along, each in its rounded course.

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Wahuwa allathee khalaqa allayla waalnnahara waalshshamsa waalqamara kullun fee falakin yasbahoona

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: We have given no man everlastingness before you. So then if you die, will they live ever after?

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: And before you, We did not appoint on earth a never-ending life for any human; will they, if you depart, become immortal?

34.author: Arberry
34.text: We have not assigned to any mortal before thee to live forever; therefore, if thou diest, will they live forever?

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: And We appointed not for any human being before thee immortality; if thou then diest, are they to be immortals?

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: And We granted not to any human being immortality before you (O Muhammad SAW), then if you die, would they live forever?

34.author: Itani
34.text: We did not grant immortality to any human being before you. Should you die, are they then the immortal?

34.author: Maududi
34.text: (O Muhammad), We did not grant everlasting life to any human being even before you. If you were to die, will they live for ever?

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: And We granted not to any human being immortality before you; then if you die, would they live forever

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: We appointed immortality for no mortal before thee. What! if thou diest, can they be immortal!

34.author: Qarai
34.text: We did not give immortality to any human before you. If you are fated to die, will they live on forever?

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: We have never assigned immortality to a human before you, therefore, if you yourself die, will they live for ever?

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: And We did not grant to any man before you eternity [on earth]; so if you die - would they be eternal?

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: We made no mortal before you immortal. Will they become immortal after you die?

34.author: Shakir
34.text: And We did not ordain abiding for any mortal before you. What! Then if you die, will they abide?

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: We have not granted everlasting life to any human being before you; then if you [Muhammad] should die, will they live forever?

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: We granted not to any man before thee permanent life (here): if then thou shouldst die, would they live permanently?

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Wama jaAAalna libasharin min qablika alkhulda afain mitta fahumu alkhalidoona

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: Every soul will know the taste of death. We tempt you with evil and with good as a trial; and to Us you will return.

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: Every living being must taste death; and We test you with harm and with good – a trial; and to Us only you have to return.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: Every soul shall taste of death; and We try you with evil and good for a testing, then unto Us you shall be returned.

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: Every soul is going to taste of death, and We shall prove you with evil and good as a temptation; and unto Us ye shall be returned.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: Everyone is going to taste death, and We shall make a trial of you with evil and with good, and to Us you will be returned.

35.author: Itani
35.text: Every soul will taste death. We burden you with adversity and prosperity—a test. And to Us you will be returned.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: Every living being shall taste death and We shall subject you to ill and good by way of trial, and to Us shall all of you be eventually sent back.

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: Everyone is going to taste death, and We shall test you with evil and with good by way of trial. And to Us you will be returned.

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: Every soul must taste of death, and We try you with evil and with good, for ordeal. And unto Us ye will be returned.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: Every soul shall taste death, and We will test you with good and ill by way of test, and to Us you will be brought back.

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: Every soul shall taste death. We will try you with a trial of evil and good. Then, to Us you shall be returned.

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: Every soul will taste death. And We test you with evil and with good as trial; and to Us you will be returned.

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: Every soul has to experience the taste of death. We test you with both hardships and blessings. In the end you will all return to Us.

35.author: Shakir
35.text: Every soul must taste of death and We try you by evil and good by way of probation; and to Us you shall be brought back.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: Every soul shall taste death; We test you with both good and evil [circumstances] as a trial. To Us you shall return.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: Every soul shall have a taste of death: and We test you by evil and by good by way of trial. to Us must ye return.

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Kullu nafsin thaiqatu almawti wanablookum bialshsharri waalkhayri fitnatan wailayna turjaAAoona

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: But when the unbelievers see you they make fun of you (and say): "Is this the one who mentions your gods (deridingly)?" Yet in Ar-Rahman they disbelieve!

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: And when the disbelievers see you, they do not appoint you except as an object of mockery; “Is he the one who speaks ill of your Gods?”; whereas they deny the remembrance of the Most Gracious Himself!

36.author: Arberry
36.text: When the unbelievers behold thee, they take thee only for mockery: 'Ha, is this the one who makes mention of your gods?' Yet they in the Remembrance of the All-merciful are unbelievers.

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: And when those who disbelieve behold thee they only take thee up for mockery: is this he who mentioneth your gods? while in the mention of the compassionate they are themselves blasphemers.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: And when those who disbelieve (in the Oneness of Allah) see you (O Muhammad SAW), they take you not except for mockery (saying): "Is this the one who talks (badly) about your gods?" While they disbelieve at the mention of the Most Beneficent (Allah). [Tafsir. Al-Qurtubi].

36.author: Itani
36.text: When those who disbelieve see you, they treat you only with ridicule: “Is this the one who mentions your gods?” And they reject the mention of the Merciful.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: When the unbelievers see you they make you an object of fun, saying: "Is he the one who mentions your gods?" They say so, and yet they reject with disdain the very mention of the Most Compassionate Lord.

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: And when those who disbelieved see you, they take you not except for mockery (saying): "Is this the one who talks about your gods" While they disbelieve at the mention of the Most Gracious.

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: And when those who disbelieve behold thee, they but choose thee out for mockery, (saying): Is this he who maketh mention of your gods? And they would deny all mention of the Beneficent.

36.author: Qarai
36.text: Whenever the faithless see you they only take you in derision: ‘Is this the one who remembers your gods?’ while they remain defiant towards the remembrance of the All-beneficent.

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: When the unbelievers see you, they take you only for mockery, saying: 'Is this he who talks about your gods' While they are unbelievers in the Remembrance of the Merciful.

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: And when those who disbelieve see you, [O Muhammad], they take you not except in ridicule, [saying], "Is this the one who insults your gods?" And they are, at the mention of the Most Merciful, disbelievers.

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: (Muhammad), whenever the unbelievers see you, they think that you deserve nothing more than to be mocked. They say, "Is it he, (Muhammad), who speaks against your gods?" But they themselves have no faith at all in the Beneficent God.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: And when those who disbelieve see you, they do not take you but for one to be scoffed at: Is this he who speaks of your gods? And they are deniers at the mention of the Beneficent Allah.

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: When those who deny the truth see you, they laugh at you, saying, "Is this the one who talks of your deities?" Yet it is they who deny all mention of the Gracious One.

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: When the Unbelievers see thee, they treat thee not except with ridicule. "Is this," (they say), "the one who talks of your gods?" and they blaspheme at the mention of (Allah) Most Gracious!

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Waitha raaka allatheena kafaroo in yattakhithoonaka illa huzuwan ahatha allathee yathkuru alihatakum wahum bithikri alrrahmani hum kafiroona

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: Man is made of inordinate haste. We will show you Our signs, then you will not desire to hasten (the punishment).

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: Man has been created hasty; very soon I shall show you My signs, do not be impatient.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: Man was created of haste. Assuredly I shall show you My signs; so demand not that I make haste.

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: Man was created of hast, surely I shall show you MY signs, so ask Me not to hasten.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: Man is created of haste, I will show you My Ayat (torments, proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.). So ask Me not to hasten (them).

37.author: Itani
37.text: The human being was created of haste. I will show you My signs, so do not seek to rush Me.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: Man is hasty by nature. I shall certainly show you My Signs. Do not ask Me to be hasty.

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: Man is created of haste. I will show you My Ayat. So ask Me not to hasten (them).

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: Man is made of haste. I shall show you My portents, but ask Me not to hasten.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: Man is a creature of haste. Soon I will show you My signs. So do not ask Me to hasten.

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: The human was created of haste. Indeed, I will show you My signs; so do not ask Me to hasten them.

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: Man was created of haste. I will show you My signs, so do not impatiently urge Me.

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: The human being is created hasty. Tell them, "Do not be hasty, for God will soon show you the evidence of His existence".

37.author: Shakir
37.text: Man is created of haste; now will I show to you My signs, therefore do not ask Me to hasten (them) on.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: Man is a creature of haste. Soon I will show you My signs, but do not ask Me to hasten them.

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: Man is a creature of haste: soon (enough) will I show you My Signs; then ye will not ask Me to hasten them!

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Khuliqa alinsanu min AAajalin saoreekum ayatee fala tastaAAjiloona

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: Yet they say: "When will the promised threat come to pass, if you speak the truth?"

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: And they say, “When will this promise occur, if you are truthful?

38.author: Arberry
38.text: They say, 'And when shall the promise come to pass, if you speak truly?'

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: And they say: when cometh this torment if ye are truth- tellers!

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: And they say: "When will this promise (come to pass), if you are truthful."

38.author: Itani
38.text: And they say, “When will this promise come true, if you are truthful?”

38.author: Maududi
38.text: They say: "(Tell us) when will the threat of punishment come to pass if you are truthful?"

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: And they say: "When will this promise (come to pass), if you are truthful."

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: And they say: When will this promise (be fulfilled), if ye are truthful?

38.author: Qarai
38.text: And they say, ‘When will this promise be fulfilled, if you are truthful?’

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: They say: 'If you are truthful, when will this promise come'

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: And they say, "When is this promise, if you should be truthful?"

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: They say, "When shall the Day of Judgment come to pass if you are true in your claim?"

38.author: Shakir
38.text: And they say: When will this threat come to pass if you are truthful?

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: They ask, "When will this promise be fulfilled, if what you say be true?"

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: They say: "When will this promise come to pass, if ye are telling the truth?"

38.author: Transliteration
38.text: Wayaqooloona mata hatha alwaAAdu in kuntum sadiqeena

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: If only the unbelievers could apprehend the moment when they would neither be able to ward off the fire from their faces and their backs, nor help reach them!

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: If only the disbelievers realised the time when they will not be able to stop the fire from their faces and from their backs – and nor are they to be helped.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: If the unbelievers but knew when that they shall not ward off the Fire from their faces nor from their backs, neither shall they be helped!

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: If only those who disbelieve have known of the time when they shall not be able to ward off from their faces the Fire nor from their backs, nor shall they be succoured!

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: If only those who disbelieved knew (the time) when they will not be able to ward off the Fire from their faces, nor from their backs; and they will not be helped.

39.author: Itani
39.text: If those who disbelieve only knew, when they cannot keep the fire off their faces and off their backs, and they will not be helped.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: If only the unbelievers knew of the Hour when they shall not be able to keep off the Fire from their faces, nor from their backs; nor shall they be helped.

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: If only those who disbelieved knew (the time) when they will not be able to ward off the Fire from their faces, nor from their backs, and they will not be helped.

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: If those who disbelieved but knew the time when they will not be able to drive off the fire from their faces and from their backs, and they will not be helped!

39.author: Qarai
39.text: If only the faithless knew about the time when they will not be able to keep the Fire off their faces and their backs, nor will they be helped!

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: If the unbelievers only knew when they will be unable to shield either their faces or their backs from the Fire; when they will not be helped!

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: If those who disbelieved but knew the time when they will not avert the Fire from their faces or from their backs and they will not be aided...

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: Would that the unbelievers knew that no one would protect their faces and backs against the fire, nor they would be helped.

39.author: Shakir
39.text: Had those who disbelieve but known (of the time) when they shall not be able to ward off the fire from their faces nor from their backs, nor shall they be helped.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: If only those who deny the truth knew the time when they would not be able to ward off the fire neither from their faces nor from their backs. They will not be helped!

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: If only the Unbelievers knew (the time) when they will not be able to ward off the fire from their faces, nor yet from their backs, and (when) no help can reach them!

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Law yaAAlamu allatheena kafaroo heena la yakuffoona AAan wujoohihimu alnnara wala AAan thuhoorihim wala hum yunsaroona

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: It will come upon them unawares confounding them, and they will not be able to keep it back, nor will they be given respite.

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: In fact it will suddenly come upon them, therefore shocking them, and they will not be able to repel it nor will they be given respite.

40.author: Arberry
40.text: Nay, but it shall come upon them suddenly, dumbfounding them, and they shall not be able to repel it, nor shall they be respited.

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: Aye! it would come upon them on a sudden and shall dumbfound them; they shall not be able to avert it, nor shall they be respited.

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: Nay, it (the Fire or the Day of Resurrection) will come upon them all of a sudden and will perplex them, and they will have no power to avert it, nor will they get respite.

40.author: Itani
40.text: In fact, it will come upon them suddenly, and bewilder them. They will not be able to repel it, and they will not be reprieved.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: That will suddenly come upon them and stupefy them. They shall not be able to ward it off, nor shall they be granted any respite.

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: Nay, it will come upon them all of a sudden and will perplex them, and they will have no power to avert it nor will they get respite.

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: Nay, but it will come upon them unawares so that it will stupefy them, and they will be unable to repel it, neither will they be reprieved.

40.author: Qarai
40.text: Indeed, it will overtake them suddenly, dumbfounding them. So they will neither be able to avert it, nor will they be granted any respite.

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: It will overtake them suddenly, dumbfounding them. They shall be unable to ward it off, and they shall not be respited.

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: Rather, it will come to them unexpectedly and bewilder them, and they will not be able to repel it, nor will they be reprieved.

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: The fire will suddenly strike and confound them. They will not be able to repel it, nor will they be given any respite.

40.author: Shakir
40.text: Nay, it shall come on them all of a sudden and cause them to become confounded, so they shall not have the power to avert it, nor shall they be respited.

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: Indeed, it will come upon them suddenly and confound them; and they will not be able to ward it off, nor shall they be reprieved.

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: Nay, it may come to them all of a sudden and confound them: no power will they have then to avert it, nor will they (then) get respite.

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Bal tateehim baghtatan fatabhatuhum fala yastateeAAoona raddaha wala hum yuntharoona

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: Many apostles have been scoffed before you; but they who scoffed were themselves caught by what they had ridiculed.

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: And indeed the Noble Messengers before you were mocked at, but their mockery ruined the mockers themselves.

41.author: Arberry
41.text: Messengers indeed were mocked at before thee, but those that scoffed at them were encompassed by that they mocked at.

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: And assuredly mocked were the apostles before thee, then there surrounded those of them Who scoffed that whereat they had been mocking!

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: Indeed (many) Messengers were mocked before you (O Muhammad SAW), but the scoffers were surrounded by that, whereat they used to mock.

41.author: Itani
41.text: Messengers before you were also ridiculed, but those who jeered were surrounded by what they had ridiculed.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: Other Messengers before you were also mocked, but those who scoffed at the Messengers were overtaken by the same scourge that they had scoffed at.

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: Indeed (many) Messengers were mocked before you, but the scoffers were surrounded by what they used to mock.

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: Messengers before thee, indeed, were mocked, but that whereat they mocked surrounded those who scoffed at them.

41.author: Qarai
41.text: Apostles were certainly derided before you; but those who ridiculed them were besieged by what they had been deriding.

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: Before you, other Messengers were mocked, but the mockers were encompassed by the very thing they mocked.

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: And already were messengers ridiculed before you, but those who mocked them were enveloped by what they used to ridicule.

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: They mocked the Messengers who were sent before you; thus, the torment which they had ridiculed encompassed them all.

41.author: Shakir
41.text: And certainly apostles before you were scoffed at, then there befell those of them who scoffed that at which they had scoffed.

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: Other messengers have been mocked before you, but those who scoffed were overwhelmed by the very thing they had mocked.

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: Mocked were (many) messenger before thee; But their scoffers were hemmed in by the thing that they mocked.

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: Walaqadi istuhzia birusulin min qablika fahaqa biallatheena sakhiroo minhum ma kanoo bihi yastahzioona

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: Say: "Who guards you from Ar-Rahman by night and by day?" Yet from a mention of their Lord they turn away.

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: Proclaim, “Who guards you night and day from the Most Gracious?” In fact they have turned away from the remembrance of their Lord.

42.author: Arberry
42.text: Say: 'Who shall guard you by night and in the daytime from the All-merciful?' Nay, but from the Remembrance of their Lord they are turning away.

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: Say thou: who guardeth you by night and by day from the Compassionate! O Yet from the remembrance of their Lord they avert themselves!

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: Say: "Who can guard and protect you in the night or in the day from the (punishment of the) Most Beneficent (Allah)?" Nay, but they turn away from the remembrance of their Lord.

42.author: Itani
42.text: Say, “Who guards you against the Merciful by night and by day?” But they turn away from the mention of their Lord.

42.author: Maududi
42.text: Tell them, (O Muhammad): "Who protects you, during the night or the day from the Most Compassionate Lord?" And yet they turn away from the Admonition of their Lord.

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: Say: "Who can guard and protect you in the night or in the day from the Most Gracious" Nay, but they turn away from the remembrance of their Lord.

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: Say: Who guardeth you in the night or in the day from the Beneficent? Nay, but they turn away from mention of their Lord!

42.author: Qarai
42.text: Say, ‘Who can guard you, day and night, against [the punishment of] the All-beneficent [should He want to punish you]?’ Indeed, they are disregardful of their Lord’s remembrance.

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: Say: 'Who will guard you, by night and by day from the Merciful' But no, they turn away from their Lord's remembrance.

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: Say, "Who can protect you at night or by day from the Most Merciful?" But they are, from the remembrance of their Lord, turning away.

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: Ask them, "Who can protect them from (the wrath of) the Beneficent God during the night and day?" Yet they are neglectful about their Lord.

42.author: Shakir
42.text: Say: Who guards you by night and by day from the Beneficent Allah? Nay, they turn aside at the mention of their Lord.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: Say, "Who will save you from the wrath of the Most Gracious, by night and by day?" Yet they turn away from the remembrance of their Lord.

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: Say: "Who can keep you safe by night and by day from (the Wrath of) (Allah) Most Gracious?" Yet they turn away from the mention of their Lord.

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Qul man yaklaokum biallayli waalnnahari mina alrrahmani bal hum AAan thikri rabbihim muAAridoona

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: Or do they have lords of their own besides Us who can defend them? But they are not able to help themselves, nor can they find protection against Us.

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: Do they have some Gods who protect them from Us? Neither can they save themselves nor save their friends from Us.

43.author: Arberry
43.text: Or have they gods that shall defend them apart from Us? Why, they are not able to help themselves, nor shall they be guarded in safety from Us.

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: Have they gods who defend them beside Us? They are not able to succour themselves, and against Us they cannot be kept company with.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: Or have they aliha (gods) who can guard them from Us? They have no power to help themselves, nor can they be protected from Us (i.e. from Our Torment).

43.author: Itani
43.text: Or do they have gods who can defend them against Us? They cannot help themselves, nor will they be protected from Us.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: Do they have any gods who would protect them against Us? They have no power even to help themselves; nor do they enjoy Our support.

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: Or have they gods who can guard them from Us They have no power to help themselves, nor can they be protected from Us.

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: Or have they gods who can shield them from Us? They cannot help themselves nor can they be defended from Us.

43.author: Qarai
43.text: Do they have gods besides Us to defend them? Neither can they help themselves, nor can they shield [the idolaters] from Us.

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: Or have they gods to defend them, other than Us? Indeed, they are unable to help themselves, neither shall they be guarded in safety from Us.

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: Or do they have gods to defend them other than Us? They are unable [even] to help themselves, nor can they be protected from Us.

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: Can their gods protect them against Us? Their gods have no power even to help themselves, nor are they safe from Our retribution.

43.author: Shakir
43.text: Or, have they gods who can defend them against Us? They shall not be able to assist themselves, nor shall they be defended from Us.

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: Do they have other deities who can defend them against Us? They cannot even help themselves, neither can they be aided against Us.

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: Or have they gods that can guard them from Us? They have no power to aid themselves, nor can they be defended from Us.

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Am lahum alihatun tamnaAAuhum min doonina la yastateeAAoona nasra anfusihim wala hum minna yushaboona

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: We allowed them and their fathers time to enjoy till the very end of their lives. Do they not see Us advancing into the land, reducing its frontiers? Would they still prevail?

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: But in fact We have given these (disbelievers) and their fathers a benefit to the extent that life became long for them; so do they not see that We are reducing the land from its borders? So will these be victorious?

44.author: Arberry
44.text: Nay, but Ourselves gave these and their fathers enjoyment of days, until their life had lasted long while upon them. What, do they not see how We come to the land, diminishing it in its extremities? Or are they the victors?

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: Aye! We let these people and their fathers enjoy until there grew long upon them the life. Behold they not that We come unto the land diminishing it by the borders thereof? Shall they then be the victors?

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: Nay, We gave the luxuries of this life to these men and their fathers until the period grew long for them. See they not that We gradually reduce the land (in their control) from its outlying borders? Is it then they who will overcome.

44.author: Itani
44.text: We have given these enjoyments, and their ancestors, until time grew long upon them. Do they not see how We gradually reduce the land from its extremities? Are they then the victors?

44.author: Maududi
44.text: Nay, We generously provided them and their fathers, and they enjoyed Our provision for long. Do they not see that We are now advancing into their territory, diminishing it from different sides? Is it they, then, who will triumph?"

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: Nay, We gave the luxuries of this life to these men and their fathers until the period grew long for them. See they not that We gradually reduce the land from its outlying borders Is it then they who will overcome

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: Nay, but We gave these and their fathers ease until life grew long for them. See they not how we aim to the land, reducing it of its outlying parts? Can they then be the victors?

44.author: Qarai
44.text: Indeed, We have provided for them and their fathers until they lived on for long years. Do they not see how We visit the land diminishing it at its edges? Are they the ones who will prevail?

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: We gave days of enjoyment to them and their fathers until their life became long. Are they unable to see how We come to their land and reduce its borders. Or, is it they who are the victors?

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: But, [on the contrary], We have provided good things for these [disbelievers] and their fathers until life was prolonged for them. Then do they not see that We set upon the land, reducing it from its borders? So it is they who will overcome?

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: We have been providing these men and their fathers with the means of enjoyment for a long time. Have they not ever considered that We populated the earth and then caused many of the inhabitants to pass away? Can they have any success (in their wickedness)?

44.author: Shakir
44.text: Nay, We gave provision to these and their fathers until life was prolonged to them. Do they not then see that We are visiting the land, curtailing it of its sides? Shall they then prevail?

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: Yet We bestowed the good things [of life] upon their fathers for a great length of time. But do they not see how We are shrinking their borders? Is it they who will prevail?

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: Nay, We gave the good things of this life to these men and their fathers until the period grew long for them; See they not that We gradually reduce the land (in their control) from its outlying borders? Is it then they who will win?

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Bal mattaAAna haolai waabaahum hatta tala AAalayhimu alAAumuru afala yarawna anna natee alarda nanqusuha min atrafiha afahumu alghaliboona

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: Say: "I am warning you by God's command." But the deaf do not hear the call when they are warned.

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) “I warn you only with the divine revelation; and the deaf do not hear the call when warned.”

45.author: Arberry
45.text: Say: 'I warn you only by the Revelation'; but they that are deaf do not hear the call when they are warned.

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: Say thou: I only warn you by the revelation; and the deaf hearken not unto the call when they are warned.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "I warn you only by the revelation (from Allah and not by the opinion of the religious scholars and others). But the deaf (who follow the religious scholars and others blindly) will not hear the call, (even) when they are warned [(i.e. one should follow only the Quran and the Sunnah (legal ways, orders, acts of worship, statements of Prophet Muhammad SAW, as the Companions of the Prophet SAW did)].

45.author: Itani
45.text: Say, “I am warning you through inspiration.” But the deaf cannot hear the call when they are being warned.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: Tell them: "I only warn you by the Revelation." But the deaf do not hear the cry when they are warned.

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: Say: "I warn you only by the revelation. "But the deaf will not hear the call, (even) when they are warned.

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: Say (O Muhammad, unto mankind): I warn you only by the Inspiration. But the deaf hear not the call when they are warned.

45.author: Qarai
45.text: Say, ‘I indeed warn you by the means of revelation.’ But the deaf do not hear the call when they are warned.

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: Say: 'I warn you only by the Revelation' But the deaf hear nothing when they are warned.

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: Say, "I only warn you by revelation." But the deaf do not hear the call when they are warned.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: (Muhammad), tell them, "I am warning you by revelation alone." The deaf do not hear any call when they are warned.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: Say: I warn you only by revelation; and the deaf do not hear the call whenever they are warned.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: Say, "I warn you only through the Revelation." But the deaf can hear nothing when they are warned,

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: Say, "I do but warn you according to revelation": But the deaf will not hear the call, (even) when they are warned!

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Qul innama onthirukum bialwahyi wala yasmaAAu alssummu aldduAAaa itha ma yuntharoona

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: Even if a whiff of the Lord's chastisement were to touch them they would surely say: "Ah woe, alas, we were surely sinful."

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: And if a whiff of your Lord’s punishment were to touch them, they would certainly say, “Woe to us – we were indeed unjust!”

46.author: Arberry
46.text: If but a breath of thy Lord's chastisement touched them, they would surely say, 'Alas for us! We were evildoers.'

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: And if a breath of the torment of thy Lord were to touch them, they will say: woe unto us! we have been wrong-doers.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: And if a breath (minor calamity) of the Torment of your Lord touches them, they will surely cry: "Woe unto us! Indeed we have been Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc.).

46.author: Itani
46.text: And when a breath of your Lord’s punishment touches them, they say, “Woe to us, we were truly wicked.”

46.author: Maududi
46.text: Were only the slightest whiff from your Lord's punishment to touch them, they would cry out: "Woe to us; we were indeed wrong-doers."

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: And if a breath of the torment of your Lord touches them, they will surely cry: "Woe unto us! Indeed we have been wrongdoers."

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: And if a breath of thy Lord's punishment were to touch them, they assuredly would say: Alas for us! Lo! we were wrong-doers.

46.author: Qarai
46.text: Should a whiff of your Lord’s punishment touch them, they will surely say, ‘Woe to us! We have indeed been wrongdoers!’

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: But if just a breath of your Lord's punishment were to touch them, they would say: 'Woe for us, we were harmdoers'

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: And if [as much as] a whiff of the punishment of your Lord should touch them, they would surely say, "O woe to us! Indeed, we have been wrongdoers."

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: If a blast of the torment of your Lord strikes them, they will say, "Woe to us! We have been unjust people."

46.author: Shakir
46.text: And if a blast of the chastisement of your Lord were to touch them, they will certainly say: O woe to us! surely we were unjust.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: yet if even a breath of your Lord's punishment touched them, they would say, "Woe to us! We were indeed wrongdoers."

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: If but a breath of the Wrath of thy Lord do touch them, they will then say, "Woe to us! we did wrong indeed!"

46.author: Transliteration
46.text: Walain massathum nafhatun min AAathabi rabbika layaqoolunna ya waylana inna kunna thalimeena

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: We shall fix the scales of justice on the Day of Resurrection, so that none will be wronged in the least; and even if it were equal to a mustard seed in weight We shall take it (into account). We are sufficient for computation.

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: And We shall set up the scales of justice on the Day of Resurrection – therefore no soul will be wronged in the least; and if a thing is equal to a grain of mustard seed, We will bring it; and We are Sufficient to (take) account.

47.author: Arberry
47.text: And We shall set up the just balances for the Resurrection Day, so that not one soul shall be wronged anything; even if it be the weight of one grain of mustard-seed We shall produce it, and sufficient are We for reckoners.

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: And We shall set balances of justice for the Day of Judgment; then no soul shall be wronged at all; and if it be but the weight of a grain of mustard-seed We shall bring it; and We suffice as Reckoners.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: And We shall set up balances of justice on the Day of Resurrection, then none will be dealt with unjustly in anything. And if there be the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it. And Sufficient are We as Reckoners.

47.author: Itani
47.text: We will set up the scales of justice for the Day of Resurrection, so that no soul will suffer the least injustice. And even if it be the weight of a mustard-seed, We will bring it up. Sufficient are We as Reckoners.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: We shall set up just scales on the Day of Resurrection so that none will be wronged in the least. (We shall bring forth the acts of everyone), even if it be the weight of a grain of mustard seed. We shall suffice as Reckoners.

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: And We shall set up Balances of justice on the Day of Resurrection, then none will be dealt with unjustly in anything. And if there be the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it. And sufficient are We to take account.

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: And We set a just balance for the Day of Resurrection so that no soul is wronged in aught. Though it be of the weight of a grain of mustard seed, We bring it. And We suffice for reckoners.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: We shall set up just scales on the Day of Resurrection, and no soul will be wronged in the least. Even if it be the weight of a mustard seed We shall produce it and We suffice as reckoners.

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: On the Day of Resurrection We shall set up just scales, so that no soul shall in the least be wronged, even though it be the weight of a grain of mustard seedWe will bring it We Suffice as reckoners.

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: And We place the scales of justice for the Day of Resurrection, so no soul will be treated unjustly at all. And if there is [even] the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it forth. And sufficient are We as accountant.

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: We shall maintain proper justice on the Day of Judgment. No soul will be wronged the least. For a deed even as small as a mustard seed one will duly be recompensed. We are efficient in maintaining the account.

47.author: Shakir
47.text: And We will set up a just balance on the day of resurrection, so no soul shall be dealt with unjustly in the least; and though there be the weight of a grain of mustard seed, (yet) will We bring it, and sufficient are We to take account.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: We shall set up scales of justice on the Day of Resurrection, so that no soul can be in the least wronged. Actions as small as a grain of mustard seed shall be weighed. We are sufficient as a reckoner.

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: We shall set up scales of justice for the Day of Judgment, so that not a soul will be dealt with unjustly in the least, and if there be (no more than) the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it (to account): and enough are We to take account.

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: WanadaAAu almawazeena alqista liyawmi alqiyamati fala tuthlamu nafsun shayan wain kana mithqala habbatin min khardalin atayna biha wakafa bina hasibeena

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: We gave Moses and Aaron the Criterion, and a light and reminder for those who take heed for themselves,

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: And indeed We gave Moosa and Haroon the Judgement* and a light and an advice for the pious. (* The Holy Book Taurat.)

48.author: Arberry
48.text: We gave Moses and Aaron the Salvation and a Radiance, and a Remembrance for the godfearing

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: And assuredly We vcuchsafed unto Musa and Harun the distinction and illumination and an admonition for the God-fearing.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: And indeed We granted to Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron) the criterion (of right and wrong), and a shining light [i.e. the Taurat (Torah)] and a Reminder for Al-Muttaqun (the pious - see V. 2:2).

48.author: Itani
48.text: We gave Moses and Aaron the Criterion, and illumination, and a reminder for the righteous.

48.author: Maududi
48.text: Surely We had granted to Moses and Aaron the Criterion (between right and wrong), and Light and Admonition for the good of the God-fearing,

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: And indeed We granted to Musa and Harun the criterion, and a shining light and a Reminder for those who have Taqwa.

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: And We verily gave Moses and Aaron the Criterion (of right and wrong) and a light and a Reminder for those who keep from evil,

48.author: Qarai
48.text: Certainly We gave Moses and Aaron the Criterion, a light and reminder for the Godwary

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: We gave Moses and Aaron the Criterion, and gave them a light and a Remembrance for the cautious:

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: And We had already given Moses and Aaron the criterion and a light and a reminder for the righteous

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: To Moses and Aaron We granted the criteria of discerning right from wrong, and We gave them the light and a reminder to the pious ones

48.author: Shakir
48.text: And certainly We gave to Musa and Haroun the Furqan and a light and a reminder for those who would guard (against evil).

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: We gave Moses and Aaron the criterion of right and wrong and a light and reminder for the righteous,

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: In the past We granted to Moses and Aaron the criterion (for judgment), and a Light and a Message for those who would do right,-

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: Walaqad atayna moosa waharoona alfurqana wadiyaan wathikran lilmuttaqeena

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: Who are fearful of their Lord inwardly and dread the Hour.

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: Those who fear their Lord without seeing and who apprehend the Last Day.

49.author: Arberry
49.text: such as fear God in the Unseen, trembling because of the Hour.

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: These who fear their Lord unseen, and who are of the Hour fearful.

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: Those who fear their Lord without seeing Him, while they are afraid of the Hour.

49.author: Itani
49.text: Those who fear their Lord in private, and are apprehensive of the Hour.

49.author: Maududi
49.text: for those who fear their Lord without seeing Him and dread the Hour (of Reckoning).

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: Those who fear their Lord in the unseen, and they are afraid of the Hour.

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: Those who fear their Lord in secret and who dread the Hour (of doom).

49.author: Qarai
49.text: —those who fear their Lord in secret, and who are apprehensive of the Hour.

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: those who fear Allah in the Unseen, tremble because of the Hour.

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: Who fear their Lord unseen, while they are of the Hour apprehensive.

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: who fear their unseen Lord and are anxious about the Day of Judgment.

49.author: Shakir
49.text: (For) those who fear their Lord in secret and they are fearful of the hour.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: those who fear their Lord in the unseen, also dread the Hour of Judgement.

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: Those who fear their Lord in their most secret thoughts, and who hold the Hour (of Judgment) in awe.

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Allatheena yakhshawna rabbahum bialghaybi wahum mina alssaAAati mushfiqoona

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: And this is a blessed reminder that We have sent down. Will you then deny it?

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: This is a blessed remembrance, sent down by Us; so do you deny it?

50.author: Arberry
50.text: And this is a blessed Remembrance that We have sent down; so are you now denying it?

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: And this is an admonition blest, which We have sent down! will ye then be the rejectors thereof?

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: And this is a blessed Reminder (the Quran) which We have sent down, will you then (dare to) deny it?

50.author: Itani
50.text: This too is a blessed message that We revealed. Are you going to deny it?

50.author: Maududi
50.text: This is a Blessed Admonition that We have revealed. Are you, then, going to reject it?

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: And this is a blessed Reminder which We have sent down; will you then deny it

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: This is a blessed Reminder that we have revealed: Will ye then reject it?

50.author: Qarai
50.text: This [too] is a blessed reminder, which We have sent down. Will you then deny it?

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: This is a blessed Remembrance which We have sent down. Do you disbelieve it?

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: And this [Qur'an] is a blessed message which We have sent down. Then are you with it unacquainted?

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: This (Quran) which We have revealed is a blessed reminder. Will you then deny it?

50.author: Shakir
50.text: And this is a blessed Reminder which We have revealed; will you then deny it?

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: This is a blessed reminder that We have revealed to you. Will you then reject it?

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: And this is a blessed Message which We have sent down: will ye then reject it?

50.author: Transliteration
50.text: Wahatha thikrun mubarakun anzalnahu afaantum lahu munkiroona

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: We had earlier given Abraham true direction, for We knew him well.

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: And indeed We bestowed Ibrahim with guidance from the beginning, and We were Aware of him.

51.author: Arberry
51.text: We gave Abraham aforetime his rectitude -- for We knew him --

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: And assuredly We vouchsafed un to lbrahim his rectitude aforetime, and him We had ever known.

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: And indeed We bestowed aforetime on Ibrahim (Abraham) his (portion of) guidance, and We were Well-Acquainted with him (as to his Belief in the Oneness of Allah, etc.).

51.author: Itani
51.text: We gave Abraham his integrity formerly, and We knew him well.

51.author: Maududi
51.text: Surely We had bestowed wisdom upon Abraham even earlier, and We knew him well.

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: And indeed We bestowed aforetime on Ibrahim his guidance, and We were Well-Acquainted with him.

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: And We verily gave Abraham of old his proper course, and We were Aware of him,

51.author: Qarai
51.text: Certainly We had given Abraham his probity before, and We knew him

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: Before this We gave Abraham his virtue, for We knew him.

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: And We had certainly given Abraham his sound judgement before, and We were of him well-Knowing

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: To Abraham We gave the right guidance and We knew him very well.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: And certainly We gave to Ibrahim his rectitude before, and We knew him fully well.

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: Before this We gave Abraham his guidance. We knew him well.

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: We bestowed aforetime on Abraham his rectitude of conduct, and well were We acquainted with him.

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Walaqad atayna ibraheema rushdahu min qablu wakunna bihi AAalimeena

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: When he said to his father and his people: "What are these idols to which you cling so passionately?"

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: When he said to his father and his people, “What are these idols before whom you squat (worshipping)?”

52.author: Arberry
52.text: when he said to his father and his people, 'What are these statues unto which you are cleaving?'

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: Recall what time he said unto his father and his people what are these images to which ye are cleaving?

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: When he said to his father and his people: "What are these images, to which you are devoted?"

52.author: Itani
52.text: When he said to his father and his people, “What are these statues to which you are devoted?”

52.author: Maududi
52.text: Recall, when he said to his father and his people: "What are these images to which you are devoutly clinging?"

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: When he said to his father and his people: "What are these images to which you are devoted"

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: When he said unto his father and his folk: What are these images unto which ye pay devotion?

52.author: Qarai
52.text: when he said to his father and his people, ‘What are these images to which you keep on clinging?’

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: He said to his father and to his nation: 'What, are these the statues to which you cling'

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: When he said to his father and his people, "What are these statues to which you are devoted?"

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: Abraham asked his father and his people, "What are these statues which you worship?"

52.author: Shakir
52.text: When he said to his father and his people: What are these images to whose worship you cleave?

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: When he asked his father and his people, "What are these images to which you are so devoted?"

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: Behold! he said to his father and his people, "What are these images, to which ye are (so assiduously) devoted?"

52.author: Transliteration
52.text: Ith qala liabeehi waqawmihi ma hathihi alttamatheelu allatee antum laha AAakifoona

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: They replied: "We found our fathers worshipping them."

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: They said, “We found our forefathers worshipping them.”

53.author: Arberry
53.text: They said, 'We found our fathers serving them.'

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: They said: we found our fathers the worshippers thereof.

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: They said:"We found our fathers worshipping them."

53.author: Itani
53.text: They said, “We found our parents worshiping them.”

53.author: Maududi
53.text: They answered: "We found our fathers worshipping them."

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: They said: "We found our fathers worshipping them."

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: They said: We found our fathers worshippers of them.

53.author: Qarai
53.text: They said, ‘We found our fathers worshipping them.’

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: They replied: 'We found our fathers worshipping them'

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: They said, "We found our fathers worshippers of them."

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: They replied, "We found our fathers worshipping them".

53.author: Shakir
53.text: They said: We found our fathers worshipping them.

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: They replied, "We found our fathers worshipping them."

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: They said, "We found our fathers worshipping them."

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: He said: "You and your fathers were in clear error."

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: He said, “Indeed you all – you and your forefathers – were in open error.”

54.author: Arberry
54.text: He said, 'Then assuredly you and your fathers have been in manifest error.'

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: He said: assuredlY ye, ye and your fathers, have been in error manifest.

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: He said: "Indeed you and your fathers have been in manifest error."

54.author: Itani
54.text: He said, “You and your parents are in evident error.”

54.author: Maududi
54.text: He said: "Certainly you and your fathers have all been in manifest error."

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: He said: "Indeed you and your fathers have been in manifest error."

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: He said: Verily ye and your fathers were in plain error.

54.author: Qarai
54.text: He said, ‘Certainly you and your fathers have been in manifest error.’

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: He said: 'Truly, you and your fathers are in clear error'

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: He said, "You were certainly, you and your fathers, in manifest error."

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: He said, "Both you and your fathers have certainly been in error."

54.author: Shakir
54.text: He said: Certainly you have been, (both) you and your fathers, in manifest error.

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: Abraham said, "Indeed, you and your fathers have been clearly misguided."

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: He said, "Indeed ye have been in manifest error - ye and your fathers."

54.author: Transliteration
54.text: Qala laqad kuntum antum waabaokum fee dalalin mubeenin

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: They said: "Are you speaking in earnest, or only jesting?"

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: They said, “Have you brought the Truth to us, or are you just making fun?”

55.author: Arberry
55.text: They said, 'What, hast thou come to us with the truth, or art thou one of those that play?'

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: They said: hast thou come unto us with the truth, or art thou of those who sport?

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: They said: "Have you brought us the truth, or are you one of those who play about?"

55.author: Itani
55.text: They said, “Are you telling us the truth, or are you just playing?”

55.author: Maududi
55.text: They said: "Are you expressing your true ideas before us or are you jesting?"

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: They said: "Have you brought us the Truth, or are you one of those who play about"

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: They said: Bringest thou unto us the truth, or art thou some jester?

55.author: Qarai
55.text: They said, ‘Are you telling the truth, or are you [just] kidding?’

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: They said: 'Is it the truth you have brought us, or are you one of those that play'

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: They said, "Have you come to us with truth, or are you of those who jest?"

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: They exclaimed, "Have you brought the Truth or are you joking?"

55.author: Shakir
55.text: They said: Have you brought to us the truth, or are you one of the triflers?

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: They said, "Have you brought us the truth or are you jesting?"

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: They said, "Have you brought us the Truth, or are you one of those who jest?"

55.author: Transliteration
55.text: Qaloo ajitana bialhaqqi am anta mina allaAAibeena

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: He said: "In fact it was your Lord, the Lord of the heavens and the earth, who created them; and I bear witness to this.

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: He said, “In fact, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the One Who created them; and I am of those who testify to it.”

56.author: Arberry
56.text: He said, 'Nay, but your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth who originated them, and I am one of those that bear witness thereunto.

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: He said: Aye! your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth Who created them, and as to that I am of the witnesses.

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: He said: "Nay, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who created them and of that I am one of the witnesses.

56.author: Itani
56.text: He said, “Your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the One who created them, and I bear witness to that.

56.author: Maududi
56.text: He said: "Nay, but your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth which He created and to that I bear witness before you.

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: He said: "Nay, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who created them and to that I am one of the witnesses."

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: He said: Nay, but your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who created them; and I am of those who testify unto that.

56.author: Qarai
56.text: He said, ‘Indeed, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, who originated them, and I affirm that.

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: 'No' he answered, 'your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Originator of them, and I am among those bearing witness to it.

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: He said, "[No], rather, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth who created them, and I, to that, am of those who testify.

56.author: Sarwar
56.text: He said, "Your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. It was He who created them and I testify to this fact".

56.author: Shakir
56.text: He said: Nay! your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who brought them into existence, and I am of those who bear witness to this:

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: Abraham replied, "Your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, who created them, and I bear witness to that.

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: He said, "Nay, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, He Who created them (from nothing): and I am a witness to this (Truth).

56.author: Transliteration
56.text: Qala bal rabbukum rabbu alssamawati waalardi allathee fatarahunna waana AAala thalikum mina alshshahideena

57.author: Ahmed Ali
57.text: I swear by God I will do something to your idols when you have turned your backs and gone."

57.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
57.text: “And, by oath of Allah, I shall seek to harm your idols after you have gone away and turned your backs.”

57.author: Arberry
57.text: And, by God, I shall assuredly outwit your idols, after you have gone away turning your backs.'

57.author: Daryabadi
57.text: And by Allah I shall surely devise a plot against your idols after ye turned away backward.

57.author: Hilali & Khan
57.text: "And by Allah, I shall plot a plan (to destroy) your idols after you have gone away and turned your backs."

57.author: Itani
57.text: “By God, I will have a plan for your statues after you have gone away.”

57.author: Maududi
57.text: By Allah, I shall certainly carry out my plan against your gods after you are gone."

57.author: Mubarakpuri
57.text: "And by Allah, I shall plot a plan for your idols after you have gone away and turned your backs."

57.author: Pickthall
57.text: And, by Allah, I shall circumvent your idols after ye have gone away and turned your backs.

57.author: Qarai
57.text: By Allah, I will devise a stratagem against your idols after you have gone away.’

57.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
57.text: By Allah, I will certainly outwit your idols as soon as you have turned your backs and gone'

57.author: Saheeh International
57.text: And [I swear] by Allah, I will surely plan against your idols after you have turned and gone away."

57.author: Sarwar
57.text: Abraham said to himself, "By God! I will devise a plan against their idols when they are away."

57.author: Shakir
57.text: And, by Allah! I will certainly do something against your idols after you go away, turning back.

57.author: Wahiduddin Khan
57.text: By the Lord, I will devise a plan against your deities after you have gone away and turned your backs!"

57.author: Yusuf Ali
57.text: "And by Allah, I have a plan for your idols - after ye go away and turn your backs"..

57.author: Transliteration
57.text: WataAllahi laakeedanna asnamakum baAAda an tuwalloo mudbireena

58.author: Ahmed Ali
58.text: So he smashed them up to pieces with the exception of the biggest, so that they may turn to it.

58.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
58.text: He shattered them all, except the biggest among them, that perhaps they may question it.

58.author: Arberry
58.text: So he broke them into fragments, all but a great one they had, for haply they would return to it.

58.author: Daryabadi
58.text: Then he made them fragments, all except the big one of them, that haply unto it they may return.

58.author: Hilali & Khan
58.text: So he broke them to pieces, (all) except the biggest of them, that they might turn to it.

58.author: Itani
58.text: So he reduced them into pieces, except for their biggest, that they may return to it.

58.author: Maududi
58.text: Then he broke them all into pieces, sparing only the supreme one among them that they may possibly return to him.

58.author: Mubarakpuri
58.text: So he broke them to pieces, except the biggest of them, that they might turn to it.

58.author: Pickthall
58.text: Then he reduced them to fragments, all save the chief of them, that haply they might have recourse to it.

58.author: Qarai
58.text: So he broke them into pieces—all except the biggest of them—so that they might come back to it.

58.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
58.text: He broke them all into pieces, except their great one so that they might return to it.

58.author: Saheeh International
58.text: So he made them into fragments, except a large one among them, that they might return to it [and question].

58.author: Sarwar
58.text: He broke all the idols into pieces, except the biggest among them so that perhaps people would refer to it.

58.author: Shakir
58.text: So he broke them into pieces, except the chief of them, that haply they may return to it.

58.author: Wahiduddin Khan
58.text: He broke them all into pieces, except for the biggest one of them, so that they might return to it [for enquiry].

58.author: Yusuf Ali
58.text: So he broke them to pieces, (all) but the biggest of them, that they might turn (and address themselves) to it.

58.author: Transliteration
58.text: FajaAAalahum juthathan illa kabeeran lahum laAAallahum ilayhi yarjiAAoona

59.author: Ahmed Ali
59.text: They asked (on return): "Who has done this to our gods? He is surely a mischief-monger."

59.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
59.text: They said, “Who has done this to our Gods? He is indeed cruel!”

59.author: Arberry
59.text: They said, 'Who has. done this with our gods? Surely he is one of the evildoers.'

59.author: Daryabadi
59.text: They said: who hath wrought this to our gods? verily he is of the wrong-doers.

59.author: Hilali & Khan
59.text: They said: "Who has done this to our aliha (gods)? He must indeed be one of the wrong-doers."

59.author: Itani
59.text: They said, “Who did this to our gods? He is certainly one of the wrongdoers.”

59.author: Maududi
59.text: (When they saw the idols in this state) they said: "Who has done this to our gods? Surely he is one of the wrong-doers."

59.author: Mubarakpuri
59.text: They said: "Who has done this to our gods He must indeed be one of the wrongdoers."

59.author: Pickthall
59.text: They said: Who hath done this to our gods? Surely it must be some evil-doer.

59.author: Qarai
59.text: They said, ‘Whoever has done this to Our gods?! He is indeed a wrongdoer!’

59.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
59.text: 'Who has done this to our gods' they exclaimed. 'He must surely be a harmdoer'

59.author: Saheeh International
59.text: They said, "Who has done this to our gods? Indeed, he is of the wrongdoers."

59.author: Sarwar
59.text: (When the people came to the temple and saw the broken idols) they asked each other, "Who has done this to our gods? He certainly is an unjust person".

59.author: Shakir
59.text: They said: Who has done this to our gods? Most surely he is one of the unjust.

59.author: Wahiduddin Khan
59.text: "Who has done this to our deities? He must be a wrongdoer."

59.author: Yusuf Ali
59.text: They said, "Who has done this to our gods? He must indeed be some man of impiety!"

59.author: Transliteration
59.text: Qaloo man faAAala hatha bialihatina innahu lamina alththalimeena

60.author: Ahmed Ali
60.text: They said: "We heard a youth talk about them. He is called Abraham."

60.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
60.text: Some among them said, “We heard a youth speak ill of them – the one called Ibrahim.”

60.author: Arberry
60.text: They said, 'We heard a young man making mention of them, and he was called Abraham.'

60.author: Daryabadi
60.text: They said: We heard a youth speak of them, who is called Ibrahim.

60.author: Hilali & Khan
60.text: They said: "We heard a young man talking (against) them who is called Ibrahim (Abraham)."

60.author: Itani
60.text: They said, “We heard a youth mentioning them. He is called Abraham.”

60.author: Maududi
60.text: Some of them said: "We heard a youth called Abraham speak (ill) of them."

60.author: Mubarakpuri
60.text: They said: "We heard a young man talking against them, who is called Ibrahim."

60.author: Pickthall
60.text: They said: We heard a youth make mention of them, who is called Abraham.

60.author: Qarai
60.text: They said, ‘We heard a young man mentioning them. He is called ‘‘Abraham.’’ ’

60.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
60.text: 'We have heard a young man called Abraham mention them' they replied.

60.author: Saheeh International
60.text: They said, "We heard a young man mention them who is called Abraham."

60.author: Sarwar
60.text: Some of them said, "We heard a youth called Abraham speaking against the idols".

60.author: Shakir
60.text: They said: We heard a youth called Ibrahim speak of them.

60.author: Wahiduddin Khan
60.text: Some said, "We heard a young man, called Abraham, talking about them."

60.author: Yusuf Ali
60.text: They said, "We heard a youth talk of them: He is called Abraham."

60.author: Transliteration
60.text: Qaloo samiAAna fatan yathkuruhum yuqalu lahu ibraheemu

61.author: Ahmed Ali
61.text: "Bring him before the people," they said, "that they may bear witness."

61.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
61.text: They said, “Therefore bring him in front of the people, perhaps they may testify.”

61.author: Arberry
61.text: They said, 'Bring him before the people's eyes; haply they shall bear witness.'

61.author: Daryabadi
61.text: They said: bring him then before the eyes of the people, haply they may bear witness.

61.author: Hilali & Khan
61.text: They said: "Then bring him before the eyes of the people, that they may testify."

61.author: Itani
61.text: They said, “Bring him before the eyes of the people, so that they may witness.”

61.author: Maududi
61.text: The others said: "Bring him, then, before the eyes of the people that they may see (what will be done to him)."

61.author: Mubarakpuri
61.text: They said: "Then bring him before the eyes of the people, that they may testify."

61.author: Pickthall
61.text: They said: Then bring him (hither) before the people's eyes that they may testify.

61.author: Qarai
61.text: They said, ‘Bring him before the people’s eyes so that they may bear witness [against him].’

61.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
61.text: They said: 'Then bring him here so that the people may see, so that they may bear witness'

61.author: Saheeh International
61.text: They said, "Then bring him before the eyes of the people that they may testify."

61.author: Sarwar
61.text: Their chiefs said, "Bring him before the eyes of the people and let them testify that he has spoken against the idols."

61.author: Shakir
61.text: Said they: Then bring him before the eyes of the people, perhaps they may bear witness.

61.author: Wahiduddin Khan
61.text: They said, "Then bring him here in the sight of all the people, so that they may act as witnesses."

61.author: Yusuf Ali
61.text: They said, "Then bring him before the eyes of the people, that they may bear witness."

61.author: Transliteration
61.text: Qaloo fatoo bihi AAala aAAyuni alnnasi laAAallahum yashhadoona

62.author: Ahmed Ali
62.text: "Did you do this to our gods, O Abraham?" they enquired.

62.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
62.text: They said, “Did you do this to our Gods, O Ibrahim?”

62.author: Arberry
62.text: They said, 'So, art thou the man who did this unto our gods, Abraham?'

62.author: Daryabadi
62.text: They said: art thou the one who hath wrought this unto our gods, O Ibrahim?

62.author: Hilali & Khan
62.text: They said: "Are you the one who has done this to our gods, O Ibrahim (Abraham)?"

62.author: Itani
62.text: They said, “Are you the one who did this to our gods, O Abraham?”

62.author: Maududi
62.text: (On Abraham's arrival) they said: "Abraham, are you he who has done this to our gods?"

62.author: Mubarakpuri
62.text: They said: "Are you the one who has done this to our gods, O Ibrahim"

62.author: Pickthall
62.text: They said: Is it thou who hast done this to our gods, O Abraham?

62.author: Qarai
62.text: They said, ‘Was it you who did this to our gods, O Abraham?’

62.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
62.text: 'Abraham' they said, 'was it you who did this to our gods'

62.author: Saheeh International
62.text: They said, "Have you done this to our gods, O Abraham?"

62.author: Sarwar
62.text: They asked, "Abraham, did you do this to our idols?"

62.author: Shakir
62.text: They said: Have you done this to our gods, O Ibrahim?

62.author: Wahiduddin Khan
62.text: They said, "Abraham, was it you who did this to our deities?"

62.author: Yusuf Ali
62.text: They said, "Art thou the one that did this with our gods, O Abraham?"

62.author: Transliteration
62.text: Qaloo aanta faAAalta hatha bialihatina ya ibraheemu

63.author: Ahmed Ali
63.text: "No," he said. "It was done by that chief of theirs. Ask him in case they can speak."

63.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
63.text: Said he, “Rather, their chief may have done it; so question them, if they can speak.”

63.author: Arberry
63.text: He said, 'No; it was this great one of them that did it. Question them; if they are able to speak!'

63.author: Daryabadi
63.text: He said: rather he hath wrought it: this big one of them: so question them, if they ever speak.

63.author: Hilali & Khan
63.text: [Ibrahim (Abraham)] said: "Nay, this one, the biggest of them (idols) did it. Ask them, if they can speak!"

63.author: Itani
63.text: He said, “But it was this biggest of them that did it. Ask them, if they can speak.”

63.author: Maududi
63.text: He answered: "Rather it was this supreme one who has done it. So ask them, if they can speak."

63.author: Mubarakpuri
63.text: He said: "Nay, this one, the biggest of them did it. Ask them, if they can speak!"

63.author: Pickthall
63.text: He said: But this, their chief hath done it. So question them, if they can speak.

63.author: Qarai
63.text: He said, ‘No, it was this biggest one of them who did it! Ask them, if they can speak.’

63.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
63.text: 'No' he replied. 'It was their great one amongst them that did it. Ask them, if they are able to speak'

63.author: Saheeh International
63.text: He said, "Rather, this - the largest of them - did it, so ask them, if they should [be able to] speak."

63.author: Sarwar
63.text: He replied, "I think the biggest among them has broken the smaller ones. Ask them if they are able to speak".

63.author: Shakir
63.text: He said: Surely (some doer) has done it; the chief of them is this, therefore ask them, if they can speak.

63.author: Wahiduddin Khan
63.text: He answered, "Rather this biggest one of them did it. Ask them, if they can speak."

63.author: Yusuf Ali
63.text: He said: "Nay, this was done by - this is their biggest one! ask them, if they can speak intelligently!"

63.author: Transliteration
63.text: Qala bal faAAalahu kabeeruhum hatha faisaloohum in kanoo yantiqoona

64.author: Ahmed Ali
64.text: Then they thought and observed: "Surely you are yourselves unjust."

64.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
64.text: So they turned towards their own selves and (inwardly) said, “Indeed you yourselves are unjust.”

64.author: Arberry
64.text: So they returned one to another, and they said, 'Surely it is you who are the evildoers.'

64.author: Daryabadi
64.text: They then turned to themselves, and said: verily ye it is who are the wrong-doers.

64.author: Hilali & Khan
64.text: So they turned to themselves and said: "Verily, you are the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers)."

64.author: Itani
64.text: Then they turned to one another, and said, “You yourselves are the wrongdoers.”

64.author: Maududi
64.text: Thereupon they turned to their (inner) selves and said (to themselves): "Surely it is you who are the wrong-doers."

64.author: Mubarakpuri
64.text: So they turned to themselves and said: "Verily, you are the wrongdoers."

64.author: Pickthall
64.text: Then gathered they apart and said: Lo! ye yourselves are the wrong-doers.

64.author: Qarai
64.text: Thereat they came to themselves and said [to one another], ‘Indeed it is you who are the wrongdoers!’

64.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
64.text: So they returned one to another saying, 'Surely, it is you who are the harmdoers'

64.author: Saheeh International
64.text: So they returned to [blaming] themselves and said [to each other], "Indeed, you are the wrongdoers."

64.author: Sarwar
64.text: Thereupon they realized their own foolishness and said, "We ourselves are wrong-doers".

64.author: Shakir
64.text: Then they turned to themselves and said: Surely you yourselves are the unjust;

64.author: Wahiduddin Khan
64.text: Then they turned to one another and said, "It is you yourselves who are in the wrong,"

64.author: Yusuf Ali
64.text: So they turned to themselves and said, "Surely ye are the ones in the wrong!"

64.author: Transliteration
64.text: FarajaAAoo ila anfusihim faqaloo innakum antumu alththalimoona

65.author: Ahmed Ali
65.text: Then crestfallen (they confessed): "Truly, as you know, they cannot speak."

65.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
65.text: Again they were inverted upon their heads; saying, “You know well that these do not speak.”

65.author: Arberry
65.text: Then they were utterly put to confusion saying, 'Very well indeed thou knowest these do not speak.'

65.author: Daryabadi
65.text: Thereafter they were made to turn over upon their heads, saying, assuredly thou knowest that they speak not.

65.author: Hilali & Khan
65.text: Then they turned to themselves (their first thought and said): "Indeed you [Ibrahim (Abraham)] know well that these (idols) speak not!"

65.author: Itani
65.text: But they reverted to their old ideas: “You certainly know that these do not speak.”

65.author: Maududi
65.text: Then their minds were turned upside down, and they said: "You know well that they do not speak."

65.author: Mubarakpuri
65.text: Then they turned to themselves: "Indeed you know well that these speak not!"

65.author: Pickthall
65.text: And they were utterly confounded, and they said: Well thou knowest that these speak not.

65.author: Qarai
65.text: Then they hung their heads. [However, they said,] ‘You certainly know that they cannot speak.’

65.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
65.text: But then they reversed their minds: 'You know they do not speak'

65.author: Saheeh International
65.text: Then they reversed themselves, [saying], "You have already known that these do not speak!"

65.author: Sarwar
65.text: With their heads cast down they said, "Abraham, you know that idols do not speak. How then can you ask such a question?"

65.author: Shakir
65.text: Then they were made to hang down their heads: Certainly you know that they do not speak.

65.author: Wahiduddin Khan
65.text: then they hung their heads, and said, "O Abraham! You know they cannot speak."

65.author: Yusuf Ali
65.text: Then were they confounded with shame: (they said), "Thou knowest full well that these (idols) do not speak!"

65.author: Transliteration
65.text: Thumma nukisoo AAala ruoosihim laqad AAalimta ma haolai yantiqoona

66.author: Ahmed Ali
66.text: (So Abraham) said: "Then why do you worship something apart from God that cannot profit you or do you harm?

66.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
66.text: He said, “What! You worship, instead of Allah, one that neither benefits you nor harms you?”

66.author: Arberry
