66.text: He said, 'What, and do you serve, apart from God, that which profits you nothing; neither hurts you?

66.author: Daryabadi
66.text: He said: worship ye, then, beside Allah, that which profiteth you not at all, nor it hurteth you

66.author: Hilali & Khan
66.text: [Ibrahim (Abraham)] said: "Do you then worship besides Allah, things that can neither profit you, nor harm you?

66.author: Itani
66.text: He said, “Do you worship, instead of God, what can neither benefit you in anything, nor harm you?

66.author: Maududi
66.text: Abraham said: "Do you, then, worship beside Allah a thing that can neither benefit you nor hurt you?

66.author: Mubarakpuri
66.text: He said: "Do you then worship besides Allah, things that can neither profit you nor harm you"

66.author: Pickthall
66.text: He said: Worship ye then instead of Allah that which cannot profit you at all, nor harm you?

66.author: Qarai
66.text: He said, ‘Then, do you worship besides Allah that which cannot cause you any benefit or harm?

66.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
66.text: He said: 'Would you then worship that, instead of Allah, which can neither help nor harm you?

66.author: Saheeh International
66.text: He said, "Then do you worship instead of Allah that which does not benefit you at all or harm you?

66.author: Sarwar
66.text: He said, "Do you, instead of God, worship things that can neither harm nor benefit you?"

66.author: Shakir
66.text: He said: What! do you then serve besides Allah what brings you not any benefit at all, nor does it harm you?

66.author: Wahiduddin Khan
66.text: Abraham said, "So, do you worship something instead of God that can neither benefit you nor harm you?

66.author: Yusuf Ali
66.text: (Abraham) said, "Do ye then worship, besides Allah, things that can neither be of any good to you nor do you harm?

66.author: Transliteration
66.text: Qala afataAAbudoona min dooni Allahi ma la yanfaAAukum shayan wala yadurrukum

67.author: Ahmed Ali
67.text: Fie on you and those you worship besides God! Will you not understand?"

67.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
67.text: “Disgrace be upon you and all the idols whom you worship instead of Allah; so do you not have sense?”

67.author: Arberry
67.text: Fie upon you and that you serve apart from God! Do you not understand?'

67.author: Daryabadi
67.text: Fie upon you and upon that which ye worship beside Allah! Will ye not then reflect?

67.author: Hilali & Khan
67.text: "Fie upon you, and upon that which you worship besides Allah! Have you then no sense?"

67.author: Itani
67.text: Fie on you, and on what you worship instead of God. Do you not understand?”

67.author: Maududi
67.text: Fie upon you and upon all that you worship beside Allah. Do you have no sense?"

67.author: Mubarakpuri
67.text: "Fie upon you, and upon that which you worship besides Allah! Have you then no sense"

67.author: Pickthall
67.text: Fie on you and all that ye worship instead of Allah! Have ye then no sense?

67.author: Qarai
67.text: Fie on you and what you worship besides Allah! Do you not exercise your reason?’

67.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
67.text: Shame on you and on that you worship other than Allah! Have you no understanding'

67.author: Saheeh International
67.text: Uff to you and to what you worship instead of Allah. Then will you not use reason?"

67.author: Sarwar
67.text: Woe to you for what you worship instead of God. Have you no understanding?"

67.author: Shakir
67.text: Fie on you and on what you serve besides Allah; what! do you not then understand?

67.author: Wahiduddin Khan
67.text: Shame on you and on whatever you worship instead of God. Can you not understand?"

67.author: Yusuf Ali
67.text: "Fie upon you, and upon the things that ye worship besides Allah! Have ye no sense?"..

67.author: Transliteration
67.text: Offin lakum walima taAAbudoona min dooni Allahi afala taAAqiloona

68.author: Ahmed Ali
68.text: They said: "Burn him, and save your gods, if you are men of action."

68.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
68.text: They said, “Burn him and help your Gods, if you want to.”

68.author: Arberry
68.text: They said, 'Burn him, and help your gods, if you would do aught.'

68.author: Daryabadi
68.text: They said: burn him, and succour your gods, if ye will be doing.

68.author: Hilali & Khan
68.text: They said: "Burn him and help your aliha (gods), if you will be doing."

68.author: Itani
68.text: They said, “Burn him and support your gods, if you are going to act.”

68.author: Maududi
68.text: They said: "Burn him, and come to the support of your gods, if you are going to do anything."

68.author: Mubarakpuri
68.text: They said: "Burn him and help your gods, if you will be doing."

68.author: Pickthall
68.text: They cried: Burn him and stand by your gods, if ye will be doing.

68.author: Qarai
68.text: They said, ‘Burn him, and help your gods, if you are to do anything!’

68.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
68.text: They said: 'Burn him and help your gods, if you are going to do anything'

68.author: Saheeh International
68.text: They said, "Burn him and support your gods - if you are to act."

68.author: Sarwar
68.text: They said, "Burn him to ashes if you want to help your gods".

68.author: Shakir
68.text: They said: Burn him and help your gods, if you are going to do (anything).

68.author: Wahiduddin Khan
68.text: They said, "Burn him and help your deities, if you are resolved to do something."

68.author: Yusuf Ali
68.text: They said, "Burn him and protect your gods, If ye do (anything at all)!"

68.author: Transliteration
68.text: Qaloo harriqoohu waonsuroo alihatakum in kuntum faAAileena

69.author: Ahmed Ali
69.text: "Turn cold, O fire," We said, "and give safety to Abraham."

69.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
69.text: We said, “O fire, become cool and peaceful upon Ibrahim.”

69.author: Arberry
69.text: We said, 'O fire, be coolness and safety for Abraham!'

69.author: Daryabadi
69.text: We said: O fire! be thou cool and peace unto Ibrahim

69.author: Hilali & Khan
69.text: We (Allah) said: "O fire! Be you coolness and safety for Ibrahim (Abraham)!"

69.author: Itani
69.text: We said, “O fire, be coolness and safety upon Abraham.”

69.author: Maududi
69.text: We said: "O fire, become coolness and safety for Abraham."

69.author: Mubarakpuri
69.text: We said: "O fire! Be you cool and safety for Ibrahim!"

69.author: Pickthall
69.text: We said: O fire, be coolness and peace for Abraham,

69.author: Qarai
69.text: We said, ‘O fire! Be cool and safe for Abraham!’

69.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
69.text: 'O Fire' We said, 'be coolness and safety for Abraham'

69.author: Saheeh International
69.text: Allah said, "O fire, be coolness and safety upon Abraham."

69.author: Sarwar
69.text: We said to the fire, "Be cool and peaceful (with Abraham)".

69.author: Shakir
69.text: We said: O fire! be a comfort and peace to Ibrahim;

69.author: Wahiduddin Khan
69.text: But We said, "Fire! Be cool and a means of safety for Abraham."

69.author: Yusuf Ali
69.text: We said, "O Fire! be thou cool, and (a means of) safety for Abraham!"

69.author: Transliteration
69.text: Qulna ya naru koonee bardan wasalaman AAala ibraheema

70.author: Ahmed Ali
70.text: They wished to entrap him, but We made them greater losers.

70.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
70.text: And they wished to cause him harm, so We made them the greatest of losers.

70.author: Arberry
70.text: They desired to outwit him; so We made them the worse losers,

70.author: Daryabadi
70.text: And they intended to do him an evil, but We made them the worst losers.

70.author: Hilali & Khan
70.text: And they wanted to harm him, but We made them the worst losers.

70.author: Itani
70.text: They planned to harm him, but We made them the worst losers.

70.author: Maududi
70.text: They had sought to do evil to him, but We caused them to be the worst losers,

70.author: Mubarakpuri
70.text: And they wanted to harm him, but We made them the worst losers.

70.author: Pickthall
70.text: And they wished to set a snare for him, but We made them the greater losers.

70.author: Qarai
70.text: They plotted to harm him, but We made them the biggest losers.

70.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
70.text: They sought to outwit him, but We made them the worst of losers.

70.author: Saheeh International
70.text: And they intended for him harm, but We made them the greatest losers.

70.author: Sarwar
70.text: They had devised an evil plan (against Abraham), but We turned it into failure."

70.author: Shakir
70.text: And they desired a war on him, but We made them the greatest losers.

70.author: Wahiduddin Khan
70.text: They had sought to do him harm, but We frustrated them.

70.author: Yusuf Ali
70.text: Then they sought a stratagem against him: but We made them the ones that lost most!

71.author: Ahmed Ali
71.text: So We delivered him and Lot, and brought them to the land We had blessed for all the people.

71.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
71.text: And We rescued him and Lut towards the land which We have blessed for the entire world.

71.author: Arberry
71.text: and We delivered him, and Lot, unto the land that We had blessed for all beings.

71.author: Daryabadi
71.text: And We delivered him and Lut to the land wherein We had placed Our blessings for the worlds.

71.author: Hilali & Khan
71.text: And We rescued him and Lout (Lot) to the land which We have blessed for the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns).

71.author: Itani
71.text: And We delivered him, and Lot, to the land that We blessed for all people.

71.author: Maududi
71.text: and We saved him and Lot and brought him to the land upon which We had bestowed Our blessings for all the people of the world.

71.author: Mubarakpuri
71.text: And We rescued him and Lut to the land which We have blessed for the nations.

71.author: Pickthall
71.text: And We rescued him and Lot (and brought them) to the land which We have blessed for (all) peoples.

71.author: Qarai
71.text: We delivered him and Lot toward the land, which We have blessed for all nations.

71.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
71.text: We saved him and Lot, and brought them to the land which We had blessed for all the worlds.

71.author: Saheeh International
71.text: And We delivered him and Lot to the land which We had blessed for the worlds.

71.author: Sarwar
71.text: We took Abraham and Lot safely to the land in which We had sent blessings to the worlds.

71.author: Shakir
71.text: And We delivered him as well as Lut (removing them) to the land which We had blessed for all people.

71.author: Wahiduddin Khan
71.text: And We saved him and Lot [and brought them] to a land which We had blessed for all people,

71.author: Yusuf Ali
71.text: But We delivered him and (his nephew) Lut (and directed them) to the land which We have blessed for the nations.

71.author: Transliteration
71.text: Wanajjaynahu walootan ila alardi allatee barakna feeha lilAAalameena

72.author: Ahmed Ali
72.text: And We bestowed on him Isaac, and Jacob as an additional gift, and made them righteous.

72.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
72.text: And We bestowed him Ishaq, and Yaqub the grandson; and We made all of them worthy of Our proximity.

72.author: Arberry
72.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob in superfluity, and every one made We righteous

72.author: Daryabadi
72.text: And We bestowed upon him ls-haq and Ya qub as a grandson; and each one We made righteous.

72.author: Hilali & Khan
72.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaque (Isaac), and (a grandson) Ya'qub (Jacob). Each one We made righteous.

72.author: Itani
72.text: And We granted him Isaac and Jacob as a gift; and each We made righteous.

72.author: Maududi
72.text: And We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob as an additional gift, making each of them righteous.

72.author: Mubarakpuri
72.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaq, and Ya`qub Nafilatan. Each one We made righteous.

72.author: Pickthall
72.text: And We bestowed upon him Isaac, and Jacob as a grandson. Each of them We made righteous.

72.author: Qarai
72.text: And We gave him Isaac, and Jacob as well for a grandson, and each of them We made righteous.

72.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
72.text: We gave him, in excess, Isaac, and Jacob (for a grandson); and We made each righteous

72.author: Saheeh International
72.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob in addition, and all [of them] We made righteous.

72.author: Sarwar
72.text: We granted him Isaac and Jacob as a gift and helped both of them to become righteous people.

72.author: Shakir
72.text: And We gave him Ishaq and Yaqoub, a son's son, and We made (them) all good.

72.author: Wahiduddin Khan
72.text: We bestowed Isaac and then Jacob on him as an additional boon and We made all of them righteous.

72.author: Yusuf Ali
72.text: And We bestowed on him Isaac and, as an additional gift, (a grandson), Jacob, and We made righteous men of every one (of them).

72.author: Transliteration
72.text: Wawahabna lahu ishaqa wayaAAqooba nafilatan wakullan jaAAalna saliheena

73.author: Ahmed Ali
73.text: And We made them leaders to guide (the people) by Our command; and We inspired them to perform good deeds and observe their moral obligations and pay the zakat; and they obeyed Us.

73.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
73.text: And We made them leaders who guide by Our command, and We sent them the divine revelation to do good deeds and to keep the prayer established and to give charity; and they used to worship Us.

73.author: Arberry
73.text: and appointed them to be leaders guiding by Our command, and We revealed to them the doing of good deeds, and to perform the prayer, and to pay the alms, and Us they served.

73.author: Daryabadi
73.text: And We made them leaders, guiding by Our command; and We revealed unto them the doing of good deeds and the establishment of prayer, and the giving of poor-rate; and of Us they were the worshippers.

73.author: Hilali & Khan
73.text: And We made them leaders, guiding (mankind) by Our Command, and We inspired in them the doing of good deeds, performing Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat), and the giving of Zakat and of Us (Alone) they were worshippers.

73.author: Itani
73.text: And We made them leaders, guiding by Our command; and We inspired them to do good works, and to observe the prayer, and to give out charity. They were devoted servants to Us.

73.author: Maududi
73.text: And We made them into leaders to guide people in accordance with Our command, and We inspired them to good works, and to establish Prayers and to give Zakah. They worshipped Us alone.

73.author: Mubarakpuri
73.text: And We made them leaders, guiding by Our command, and We revealed to them the doing of good deeds, performing Salah, and the giving of Zakah, and of Us (Alone) they were the worshippers.

73.author: Pickthall
73.text: And We made them chiefs who guide by Our command, and We inspired in them the doing of good deeds and the right establishment of worship and the giving of alms, and they were worshippers of Us (alone).

73.author: Qarai
73.text: We made them imams, guiding by Our command, and We revealed to them [concerning] the performance of good deeds, the maintenance of prayers, and the giving of zakat, and they used to worship Us.

73.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
73.text: and appointed them leaders to guide by Our Command and We revealed to them to do good deeds, and to establish the prayer, and the giving of charity, and they were for Us worshipers.

73.author: Saheeh International
73.text: And We made them leaders guiding by Our command. And We inspired to them the doing of good deeds, establishment of prayer, and giving of zakah; and they were worshippers of Us.

73.author: Sarwar
73.text: We appointed them as leaders to guide the people through Our command and sent them revelation to strive for good deeds, worship their Lord, and pay religious tax. Both of them were Our worshipping servants.

73.author: Shakir
73.text: And We made them Imams who guided (people) by Our command, and We revealed to them the doing of good and the keeping up of prayer and the giving of the alms, and Us (alone) did they serve;

73.author: Wahiduddin Khan
73.text: We made them leaders who guided people by Our command. We revealed to them the doing of good, observance of prayer and the giving of alms and Us alone did they worship.

73.author: Yusuf Ali
73.text: And We made them leaders, guiding (men) by Our Command, and We sent them inspiration to do good deeds, to establish regular prayers, and to practise regular charity; and they constantly served Us (and Us only).

73.author: Transliteration
73.text: WajaAAalnahum aimmatan yahdoona biamrina waawhayna ilayhim fiAAla alkhayrati waiqama alssalati waeetaa alzzakati wakanoo lana AAabideena

74.author: Ahmed Ali
74.text: To Lot We gave wisdom and knowledge, and saved him from a people who acted villainously and were certainly wicked and disobedient.

74.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
74.text: And We gave Lut the kingdom and knowledge, and We rescued him from the township that committed vile deeds; indeed those evil people were disobedient.

74.author: Arberry
74.text: And Lot -- to him We gave judgment and knowledge; and we delivered him from the city that had been doing deeds of corruption; they were an evil people, truly ungodly;

74.author: Daryabadi
74.text: And Lut! We vouchsafed unto him judgment and knowledge, and We delivered him out of the city which had been working foul deeds; verily they were a people evil, wicked.

74.author: Hilali & Khan
74.text: And (remember) Lout (Lot), We gave him Hukman (right judgement of the affairs and Prophethood) and (religious) knowledge, and We saved him from the town (folk) who practised Al-Khaba'ith (evil, wicked and filthy deeds, etc.). Verily, they were a people given to evil, and were Fasiqun (rebellious, disobedient, to Allah).

74.author: Itani
74.text: And Lot—We gave him judgment and knowledge, and We delivered him from the town that practiced the abominations. They were wicked and perverted people.

74.author: Maududi
74.text: We bestowed upon Lot sound judgement and knowledge, and We delivered him from the city that was immersed in foul deeds. They were indeed a wicked people, exceedingly disobedient.

74.author: Mubarakpuri
74.text: And (remember) Lut, We gave him wisdom and knowledge, and We saved him from the town who practised Al-Khaba'ith. Verily, they were a people given to evil, and were rebellious.

74.author: Pickthall
74.text: And unto Lot we gave judgment and knowledge, and We delivered him from the community that did abominations. Lo! they were folk of evil, lewd.

74.author: Qarai
74.text: We gave judgement and knowledge to Lot, and We delivered him from the town which used to commit vicious acts. Indeed, they were an evil and depraved lot.

74.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
74.text: To Lot, We gave judgment and knowledge and saved him from the village that had been committing corrupt deeds; for they were an evil nation and were debauched.

74.author: Saheeh International
74.text: And to Lot We gave judgement and knowledge, and We saved him from the city that was committing wicked deeds. Indeed, they were a people of evil, defiantly disobedient.

74.author: Sarwar
74.text: To Lot We gave knowledge and wisdom and saved him from the people of the town who were committing indecent acts. They were certainly a bad and sinful people.

74.author: Shakir
74.text: And (as for) Lut, We gave him wisdom and knowledge, and We delivered him from the town which wrought abominations; surely they were an evil people, transgressors;

74.author: Wahiduddin Khan
74.text: To Lot We gave wisdom and knowledge and delivered him from the city which practiced abomination. They were indeed a wicked people.

74.author: Yusuf Ali
74.text: And to Lut, too, We gave Judgment and Knowledge, and We saved him from the town which practised abominations: truly they were a people given to Evil, a rebellious people.

74.author: Transliteration
74.text: Walootan ataynahu hukman waAAilman wanajjaynahu mina alqaryati allatee kanat taAAmalu alkhabaitha innahum kanoo qawma sawin fasiqeena

75.author: Ahmed Ali
75.text: Thus We admitted him to Our grace. He is surely one of the righteous.

75.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
75.text: And We admitted him into Our mercy; indeed he is among those who deserve Our proximity.

75.author: Arberry
75.text: and We admitted him into Our mercy; he was of the righteous.

75.author: Daryabadi
75.text: And We caused him to enter into Our mercy; verily he was of the righteous.

75.author: Hilali & Khan
75.text: And We admitted him to Our Mercy, truly, he was of the righteous.

75.author: Itani
75.text: And We admitted him into Our mercy; for He was one of the righteous.

75.author: Maududi
75.text: And We admitted him into Our mercy. Verily he was of the righteous.

75.author: Mubarakpuri
75.text: And We admitted him to Our mercy; truly, he was of the righteous.

75.author: Pickthall
75.text: And We brought him in unto Our mercy. Lo! he was of the righteous.

75.author: Qarai
75.text: And We admitted him into Our mercy. Indeed, he was one of the righteous.

75.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
75.text: We admitted him to Our Mercy, he was among the righteous.

75.author: Saheeh International
75.text: And We admitted him into Our mercy. Indeed, he was of the righteous.

75.author: Sarwar
75.text: We encompassed him in Our mercy; he was a righteous man.

75.author: Shakir
75.text: And We took him into Our mercy; surely he was of the good.

75.author: Wahiduddin Khan
75.text: We admitted him to Our mercy; he was a righteous man.

75.author: Yusuf Ali
75.text: And We admitted him to Our Mercy: for he was one of the Righteous.

75.author: Transliteration
75.text: Waadkhalnahu fee rahmatina innahu mina alssaliheena

76.author: Ahmed Ali
76.text: Remember Noah when he called to Us before this. We heard him and saved him and those with him from great distress;

76.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
76.text: And before this when Nooh called Us – We therefore heard his prayer and rescued him and his household from the great calamity.

76.author: Arberry
76.text: And Noah -- when he called before, and We answered him, and delivered him and his people from the great distress,

76.author: Daryabadi
76.text: And Nuh! recall what time he cried aforetime; so we answered him and delivered him and his house hold from a calamity mighty.

76.author: Hilali & Khan
76.text: And (remember) Nuh (Noah), when he cried (to Us) aforetime. We listened to his invocation and saved him and his family from great distress.

76.author: Itani
76.text: And Noah, when he called before. So We answered him, and delivered him and his family from the great disaster.

76.author: Maududi
76.text: We bestowed the same favour upon Noah. Recall, when he cried to Us before; We accepted his prayer and delivered him and his household from the great distress

76.author: Mubarakpuri
76.text: And (remember) Nuh, when he cried (to Us) aforetime. We answered to his invocation and saved him and his family from the great distress.

76.author: Pickthall
76.text: And Noah, when he cried of old, We heard his prayer and saved him and his household from the great affliction.

76.author: Qarai
76.text: And before that Noah, when he called out, We responded to him and delivered him and his family from the great agony.

76.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
76.text: And (remember) Noah, when he supplicated to Us, We answered him, and We saved him and his nation from great distress,

76.author: Saheeh International
76.text: And [mention] Noah, when he called [to Allah] before [that time], so We responded to him and saved him and his family from the great flood.

76.author: Sarwar
76.text: We answered the prayer of Noah who had prayed to Us before and saved him and his followers from the great disaster.

76.author: Shakir
76.text: And Nuh, when he cried aforetime, so We answered him, and delivered him and his followers from the great calamity.

76.author: Wahiduddin Khan
76.text: Before him Noah cried out to Us, and We heard his prayer. We saved him and all his household from a great distress.

76.author: Yusuf Ali
76.text: (Remember) Noah, when he cried (to Us) aforetime: We listened to his (prayer) and delivered him and his family from great distress.

76.author: Transliteration
76.text: Wanoohan ith nada min qablu faistajabna lahu fanajjaynahu waahlahu mina alkarbi alAAatheemi

77.author: Ahmed Ali
77.text: And We helped him against the people who rejected Our signs as lies. They were a wicked people indeed, so We drowned them one and all.

77.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
77.text: And We helped him against the people who denied Our signs; indeed they were evil people, We therefore drowned them all.

77.author: Arberry
77.text: and We helped him against the people who cried lies to Our signs; surely they were an evil people, so We drowned them all together.

77.author: Daryabadi
77.text: And We succoured him against the people who belied Our signs; verily they were a people evil, so we drowned them all.

77.author: Hilali & Khan
77.text: We helped him against people who denied Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.). Verily, they were a people given to evil. So We drowned them all.

77.author: Itani
77.text: And We supported him against the people who rejected Our signs. They were an evil people, so We drowned them all.

77.author: Maududi
77.text: and We helped him against a people who rejected Our signs as false. They were indeed an evil people and so We drowned them all.

77.author: Mubarakpuri
77.text: We helped him against the people who denied Our Ayat. Verily, they were a people given to evil. So We drowned them all.

77.author: Pickthall
77.text: And delivered him from the people who denied Our revelations. Lo! they were folk of evil, therefor did We drown them all.

77.author: Qarai
77.text: And We helped him against the people who denied Our signs. They were indeed an evil lot; so We drowned them all.

77.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
77.text: and helped him against the nation who had belied Our signs. They were an evil nation; We drowned them all.

77.author: Saheeh International
77.text: And We saved him from the people who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a people of evil, so We drowned them, all together.

77.author: Sarwar
77.text: We helped him against the people who said Our revelations were mere lies. They were a bad people and We drowned them all.

77.author: Shakir
77.text: And We helped him against the people who rejected Our communications; surely they were an evil people, so We drowned them all.

77.author: Wahiduddin Khan
77.text: We helped him against his people who rejected Our revelations. They were surely a wicked people, so We drowned them all.

77.author: Yusuf Ali
77.text: We helped him against people who rejected Our Signs: truly they were a people given to Evil: so We drowned them (in the Flood) all together.

77.author: Transliteration
77.text: Wanasarnahu mina alqawmi allatheena kaththaboo biayatina innahum kanoo qawma sawin faaghraqnahum ajmaAAeena

78.author: Ahmed Ali
78.text: (Remember) David and Solomon, when they pronounced judgement about the field which was eaten up at night by sheep belonging to certain people. We were witness to their judgement.

78.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
78.text: And remember Dawud and Sulaiman, when they were deciding the dispute of a field, when some people’s sheep had strayed into it at night; and We were Present at the time of their deciding.

78.author: Arberry
78.text: And David and Solomon -- when they gave judgment concerning the tillage, when the sheep of the people strayed there, and We bore witness to their judgment;

78.author: Daryabadi
78.text: And Daud and Sulaiman! recall what time they gave judgment regarding the tillage when certain people's sheep had pastured therein at night; and of the judgment concerning them We were the Witnesses.

78.author: Hilali & Khan
78.text: And (remember) Dawud (David) and Sulaiman (Solomon), when they gave judgement in the case of the field in which the sheep of certain people had pastured at night and We were witness to their judgement.

78.author: Itani
78.text: And David and Solomon, when they gave judgment in the case of the field, when some people’s sheep wandered therein by night; and We were witnesses to their judgment.

78.author: Maududi
78.text: We bestowed the same favour upon David and Solomon. Recall, when they gave judgement regarding a tillage into which the sheep of some people had strayed at night, and We were witnesses to their judgement.

78.author: Mubarakpuri
78.text: And (remember) Dawud and Sulayman, when they gave judgement in the case of the field in which the sheep of certain people had Nafashat; and We were witness to their judgement.

78.author: Pickthall
78.text: And David and Solomon, when they gave judgment concerning the field, when people's sheep had strayed and browsed therein by night; and We were witnesses to their judgment.

78.author: Qarai
78.text: And [remember] David and Solomon when they gave judgement concerning the tillage when the sheep of some people strayed into it by night, and We were witness to their judgement.

78.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
78.text: And David and Solomon they passed judgment upon the tilled land on which the people? s sheep had strayed. We bore witness to their judgment, and

78.author: Saheeh International
78.text: And [mention] David and Solomon, when they judged concerning the field - when the sheep of a people overran it [at night], and We were witness to their judgement.

78.author: Sarwar
78.text: David and Solomon were trying to settle the case of the people's sheep that grazed in a corn-field at night. We witnessed their decree in that matter.

78.author: Shakir
78.text: And Dawood and Sulaiman when they gave judgment concerning the field when the people's sheep pastured therein by night, and We were bearers of witness to their judgment.

78.author: Wahiduddin Khan
78.text: Tell of David and Solomon who both passed judgement on the field into which some people's sheep had strayed [and grazed] at night. We bore witness to their judgement.

78.author: Yusuf Ali
78.text: And remember David and Solomon, when they gave judgment in the matter of the field into which the sheep of certain people had strayed by night: We did witness their judgment.

78.author: Transliteration
78.text: Wadawooda wasulaymana ith yahkumani fee alharthi ith nafashat feehi ghanamu alqawmi wakunna lihukmihim shahideena

79.author: Ahmed Ali
79.text: We made Solomon understand the case, and bestowed on each wisdom and knowledge, We subdued the al-jibal (mountains) with David to sing Our praises, and at-tair (birds). It is We who did it.

79.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
79.text: And We explained the case to Sulaiman; and to both We gave the kingdom and knowledge; and subjected the hills to proclaim the Purity along with Dawud, and (also subjected) the birds; and these were Our works.

79.author: Arberry
79.text: and We made Solomon to understand it, and unto each gave We judgment and knowledge. And with David We subjected the mountains to give glory, and the birds, and We were doers.

79.author: Daryabadi
79.text: So We gave the understanding thereof unto Sulaiman; and unto each We vouchsafed judgment and knowledge. And We so subjected the mountains that they shall hallow us along with Daud, and also the birds; and We were the doers.

79.author: Hilali & Khan
79.text: And We made Sulaiman (Solomon) to understand (the case), and to each of them We gave Hukman (right judgement of the affairs and Prophethood) and knowledge. And We subjected the mountains and the birds to glorify Our Praises along with Dawud (David), And it was We Who were the doers (of all these things).

79.author: Itani
79.text: And so We made Solomon understand it, and to each We gave wisdom and knowledge. And We subjected the mountains along with David to sing Our praises, and the birds as well—surely We did.

79.author: Maududi
79.text: We guided Solomon to the right verdict, and We had granted each of them judgement and knowledge. We made the mountains and the birds celebrate the praise of Allah with David. It was We Who did all this.

79.author: Mubarakpuri
79.text: And We made Sulayman to understand (the case); and to each of them We gave wisdom (Hukm) and knowledge. And We subjected the mountains and the birds to glorify Our praises along with Dawud. And it was We Who were the doer (of all these things).

79.author: Pickthall
79.text: And We made Solomon to understand (the case); and unto each of them We gave judgment and knowledge. And we subdued the hills and the birds to hymn (His) praise along with David. We were the doers (thereof).

79.author: Qarai
79.text: We gave its understanding to Solomon, and to each We gave judgement and knowledge. We disposed the mountains and the birds to glorify [Us] with David, and We have been the doer [of these things].

79.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
79.text: We made Solomon to understand it, and to both We gave judgment and knowledge. And with David We subjected the mountains and birds to exalt (Allah). All this We have done.

79.author: Saheeh International
79.text: And We gave understanding of the case to Solomon, and to each [of them] We gave judgement and knowledge. And We subjected the mountains to exalt [Us], along with David and [also] the birds. And We were doing [that].

79.author: Sarwar
79.text: We made Solomon understand the law about the case and gave both David and Solomon knowledge and wisdom. We made the mountains and birds glorify the Lord along with David. We had also done to him such favors before.

79.author: Shakir
79.text: So We made Sulaiman to understand it; and to each one We gave wisdom and knowledge; and We made the mountains, and the birds to celebrate Our praise with Dawood; and We were the doers.

79.author: Wahiduddin Khan
79.text: We gave Solomon the right understanding of the matter, and We bestowed wisdom and knowledge on both of them. We caused the mountains and the birds to celebrate Our praises along with David. We had the power to do this --

79.author: Yusuf Ali
79.text: To Solomon We inspired the (right) understanding of the matter: to each (of them) We gave Judgment and Knowledge; it was Our power that made the hills and the birds celebrate Our praises, with David: it was We Who did (all these things).

79.author: Transliteration
79.text: Fafahhamnaha sulaymana wakullan atayna hukman waAAilman wasakhkharna maAAa dawooda aljibala yusabbihna waalttayra wakunna faAAileena

80.author: Ahmed Ali
80.text: And We taught him the art of making coats of mail to shield you from each other's violence. Will you not be grateful even then?

80.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
80.text: And We taught him to make a garment for you, to protect you from your hurt; so will you be thankful?

80.author: Arberry
80.text: And We taught him the fashioning of garments for you, to fortify you against your violence; then are you thankful?

80.author: Daryabadi
80.text: And We taught him the art of making the coats of mail for you that it may protect you from your violence; are ye then thankful?

80.author: Hilali & Khan
80.text: And We taught him the making of metal coats of mail (for battles), to protect you in your fighting. Are you then grateful?

80.author: Itani
80.text: And We taught him the making of shields for you, to protect you from your violence. Are you, then, appreciative?

80.author: Maududi
80.text: It was We Who taught him the art of making coats of mail for your benefit so that it may protect you from each other's violence. Do you, then, give thanks?

80.author: Mubarakpuri
80.text: And We taught him the making of metal coats of mail (for battles), to protect you in your fighting. Are you then grateful

80.author: Pickthall
80.text: And We taught him the art of making garments (of mail) to protect you in your daring. Are ye then thankful?

80.author: Qarai
80.text: We taught him the making of coats of mail for you, to protect you from your [own] violence. Will you then be grateful?

80.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
80.text: We taught him the craft of making garments that fortify you against your own violence. Are you thankful?

80.author: Saheeh International
80.text: And We taught him the fashioning of coats of armor to protect you from your [enemy in] battle. So will you then be grateful?

80.author: Sarwar
80.text: We taught him the art of making coats of mail so that you could protect yourselves during a war. Will you then give thanks?

80.author: Shakir
80.text: And We taught him the making of coats of mail for you, that they might protect you in your wars; will you then be grateful?

80.author: Wahiduddin Khan
80.text: We taught him the art of making coats of mail for you, to protect you in battle. Will you then give thanks?

80.author: Yusuf Ali
80.text: It was We Who taught him the making of coats of mail for your benefit, to guard you from each other's violence: will ye then be grateful?

80.author: Transliteration
80.text: WaAAallamnahu sanAAata laboosin lakum lituhsinakum min basikum fahal antum shakiroona

81.author: Ahmed Ali
81.text: We made tempestuous winds obedient to Solomon which blew swiftly to sail at his bidding (with his ships) to the land We had blessed. We are cognisant of everything.

81.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
81.text: And We subjected the fast wind for Sulaiman, which moved by his command towards the land in which We have placed blessings; and We know everything.

81.author: Arberry
81.text: And to Solomon the wind, strongly blowing, that ran at his command unto the land that We had blessed; and We had knowledge of everything;

81.author: Daryabadi
81.text: And unto Sulaiman We subjected the wind, strongly raging, running at his command toward the land wherein We had placed Our blessing; and of evrything We are the knowers.

81.author: Hilali & Khan
81.text: And to Sulaiman (Solomon) (We subjected) the wind strongly raging, running by his command towards the land which We had blessed. And of everything We are the All-Knower.

81.author: Itani
81.text: And to Solomon the stormy wind, blowing at His command towards the land that We have blessed. We are aware of everything.

81.author: Maududi
81.text: And We subdued the strongly raging wind to Solomon which blew at his bidding towards the land We blessed. We know everything.

81.author: Mubarakpuri
81.text: And to Sulayman (We subjected) the wind strongly raging, running by his command towards the land which We had blessed. And of everything We are the All-Knower.

81.author: Pickthall
81.text: And unto Solomon (We subdued) the wind in its raging. It set by his command toward the land which We had blessed. And of everything We are Aware.

81.author: Qarai
81.text: And [We disposed] for Solomon the tempestuous wind which blew by his command toward the land which We have blessed, and We have knowledge of all things.

81.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
81.text: To Solomon the raging wind ran at his command to the land which We had blessed. We have knowledge of all things.

81.author: Saheeh International
81.text: And to Solomon [We subjected] the wind, blowing forcefully, proceeding by his command toward the land which We had blessed. And We are ever, of all things, Knowing.

81.author: Sarwar
81.text: We made subservient to Solomon the swift wind that blew on his command to the land in which We had sent blessings. We have the knowledge of all things.

81.author: Shakir
81.text: And (We made subservient) to Sulaiman the wind blowing violent, pursuing its course by his command to the land which We had blessed, and We are knower of all things.

81.author: Wahiduddin Khan
81.text: We subjected to Solomon the stormy wind, which blew at his behest towards the land which We had blessed. For it is We who have knowledge of all things --

81.author: Yusuf Ali
81.text: (It was Our power that made) the violent (unruly) wind flow (tamely) for Solomon, to his order, to the land which We had blessed: for We do know all things.

81.author: Transliteration
81.text: Walisulaymana alrreeha AAasifatan tajree biamrihi ila alardi allatee barakna feeha wakunna bikulli shayin AAalimeena

82.author: Ahmed Ali
82.text: And many of the devils (We also made obedient to him) who dived for him, and did many other things; and We kept watch over them.

82.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
82.text: And among the devils, were those who dived (in water) for him and did works other than this; and We had kept them restrained.

82.author: Arberry
82.text: and of the Satans some dived for him and did other work besides; and We were watching over them.

82.author: Daryabadi
82.text: And of the satans were some who dove for him, and worked a work beside that; and of them We were the Watchers.

82.author: Hilali & Khan
82.text: And of the Shayatin (devils) (from the jinns) were some who dived for him, and did other work besides that; and it was We Who guarded them.

82.author: Itani
82.text: And of the devils were some that dived for him, and performed other, lesser tasks. But We kept them restrained.

82.author: Maududi
82.text: And We subdued many devils who dived (into the sea) for him and carried out other jobs besides that. We kept watch over all of them.

82.author: Mubarakpuri
82.text: And of the Shayatin were some who dived for him, and did other work besides that; and it was We Who guarded them.

82.author: Pickthall
82.text: And of the evil ones (subdued We unto him) some who dived (for pearls) for him and did other work, and We were warders unto them.

82.author: Qarai
82.text: Among the devils were some who dived for him and performed tasks other than that, and We were watchful over them.

82.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
82.text: And some of the satans dived for him and others did work as well. We were watching over them.

82.author: Saheeh International
82.text: And of the devils were those who dived for him and did work other than that. And We were of them a guardian.

82.author: Sarwar
82.text: We subdued the devils who would dive into the sea for him and perform other tasks for Solomon. We kept them in his service.

82.author: Shakir
82.text: And of the rebellious people there were those who dived for him and did other work besides that, and We kept guard over them;

82.author: Wahiduddin Khan
82.text: We also subjected to him some of the jinn who dived for him in the sea and performed other tasks; We kept a watch over them.

82.author: Yusuf Ali
82.text: And of the evil ones, were some who dived for him, and did other work besides; and it was We Who guarded them.

82.author: Transliteration
82.text: Wamina alshshayateeni man yaghoosoona lahu wayaAAmaloona AAamalan doona thalika wakunna lahum hafitheena

83.author: Ahmed Ali
83.text: (Remember) Job when he called to his Lord: "I am afflicted with distress, and You are the most compassionate of all."

83.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
83.text: And remember Ayyub (Job), when he called his Lord that, “Hardship has afflicted me, and You are the Most Merciful of all those who have mercy.”

83.author: Arberry
83.text: And Job -- when he called unto his Lord, 'Behold, affliction has visited me, and Thou art the most merciful of the merciful.'

83.author: Daryabadi
83.text: And Ayyub! recall what time he cried unto his Lord: verily hurt hath touched me, and Thou art the Most Merciful of the mercifuls.

83.author: Hilali & Khan
83.text: And (remember) Ayub (Job), when he cried to his Lord: "Verily, distress has seized me, and You are the Most Merciful of all those who show mercy."

83.author: Itani
83.text: And Job, when he cried out to his Lord: “Great harm has afflicted me, and you are the Most Merciful of the merciful.”

83.author: Maududi
83.text: We bestowed (the same wisdom, judgement and knowledge) upon Job. Recall, when he cried to his Lord: "Behold, disease has struck me and You are the Most Merciful of those that are merciful."

83.author: Mubarakpuri
83.text: And (remember) Ayyub, when he cried to his Lord: "Verily, distress has seized me, and You are the Most Merciful of all those who show mercy."

83.author: Pickthall
83.text: And Job, when he cried unto his Lord, (saying): Lo! adversity afflicteth me, and Thou art Most Merciful of all who show mercy.

83.author: Qarai
83.text: And [remember] Job, when he called out to his Lord, ‘Indeed distress has befallen me, and You are the most merciful of the merciful.’

83.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
83.text: And Job when he called to his Lord: 'Affliction has befallen me, and You are the Most Merciful of the merciful'

83.author: Saheeh International
83.text: And [mention] Job, when he called to his Lord, "Indeed, adversity has touched me, and you are the Most Merciful of the merciful."

83.author: Sarwar
83.text: When Job prayed, "Lord, I have been afflicted with hardships. Have mercy on me; You are the Most Merciful of those who have mercy,

83.author: Shakir
83.text: And Ayub, when he cried to his Lord, (saying): Harm has afflicted me, and Thou art the most Merciful of the merciful.

83.author: Wahiduddin Khan
83.text: Remember Job when he called on his Lord saying, "I have been afflicted with great distress: but You are the most merciful of the merciful."

83.author: Yusuf Ali
83.text: And (remember) Job, when He cried to his Lord, "Truly distress has seized me, but Thou art the Most Merciful of those that are merciful."

83.author: Transliteration
83.text: Waayyooba ith nada rabbahu annee massaniya alddurru waanta arhamu alrrahimeena

84.author: Ahmed Ali
84.text: So We heard his cry and relieved him of the misery he was in. We restored his family to him, and along with them gave him others similar to them as a grace from Us and reminder for those who are obedient.

84.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
84.text: We therefore heard his prayer and removed the adversity that had afflicted him, and We gave him his family and in addition bestowed along with them a similar number, by mercy from Ourselves – and an advice for the people who worship.

84.author: Arberry
84.text: So We answered him, and removed the affliction that was upon him, and We gave his people, and the like of them with them, mercy from Us, and a Reminder to those who serve.

84.author: Daryabadi
84.text: So We answered him and We removed that which was with him of the hurt, and We vouchsafed unto him his household and the like thereof along with them, as a mercy from us and a remembrance unto the worshippers.

84.author: Hilali & Khan
84.text: So We answered his call, and We removed the distress that was on him, and We restored his family to him (that he had lost), and the like thereof along with them, as a mercy from Ourselves and a Reminder for all who worship Us.

84.author: Itani
84.text: So We answered him, lifted his suffering, and restored his family to him, and their like with them—a mercy from Us, and a reminder for the worshipers.

84.author: Maududi
84.text: We accepted his prayer and removed the affliction from him, and We not only restored to him his family but as many more with them as a mercy from Us and as a lesson to the worshippers.

84.author: Mubarakpuri
84.text: So We answered his call, and We removed the distress that was on him, and We restored his family to him, and the like thereof along with them as a mercy from Ourselves and a Reminder for all those who worship Us.

84.author: Pickthall
84.text: Then We heard his prayer and removed that adversity from which he suffered, and We gave him his household (that he had lost) and the like thereof along with them, a mercy from Our store, and a remembrance for the worshippers;

84.author: Qarai
84.text: So We answered his prayer and removed his distress, and We gave him [back] his family along with others like them, as a mercy from Us and an admonition for the devout.

84.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
84.text: We answered him and removed his affliction, and We gave his people, and those like them that were with them, mercy from Us, as a reminder to those who worship.

84.author: Saheeh International
84.text: So We responded to him and removed what afflicted him of adversity. And We gave him [back] his family and the like thereof with them as mercy from Us and a reminder for the worshippers [of Allah].

84.author: Sarwar
84.text: "We answered his prayer, relieved him from his hardships, brought his family (back to him) and gave him twice as much property as that (which was destroyed). It was a mercy from Us and a reminder for the worshippers.

84.author: Shakir
84.text: Therefore We responded to him and took off what harm he had, and We gave him his family and the like of them with them: a mercy from Us and a reminder to the worshippers.

84.author: Wahiduddin Khan
84.text: We heard his prayer and relieved his suffering, We restored to him his family, doubling their number as an act of Our grace, and as a reminder for the worshippers.

84.author: Yusuf Ali
84.text: So We listened to him: We removed the distress that was on him, and We restored his people to him, and doubled their number,- as a Grace from Ourselves, and a thing for commemoration, for all who serve Us.

84.author: Transliteration
84.text: Faistajabna lahu fakashafna ma bihi min durrin waataynahu ahlahu wamithlahum maAAahum rahmatan min AAindina wathikra lilAAabideena

85.author: Ahmed Ali
85.text: (Remember) Ishmael, Edris and Dhu'l-Kifl. They were men of fortitude,

85.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
85.text: And remember Ismail, and Idrees, and Zul-Kifl; they were all patiently enduring.

85.author: Arberry
85.text: And Ishmael, Idris, Dhul Kifl -- each was of the patient,

85.author: Daryabadi
85.text: And Isma'il and ldris and Zul-Kifl! each were of the patient.

85.author: Hilali & Khan
85.text: And (remember) Isma'il (Ishmael), and Idris (Enoch) and Dhul-Kifl (Isaiah), all were from among As-Sabirin (the patient ones, etc.).

85.author: Itani
85.text: And Ishmael, and Enoch, and Ezekiel; each was one of the steadfast.

85.author: Maududi
85.text: And (We bestowed the same favour) upon Ishmael, Idris and Dhu al-Kifl, for they were all steadfast.

85.author: Mubarakpuri
85.text: And (remember) Isma`il, Idris and Dhul-Kifl: All were from among the patient.

85.author: Pickthall
85.text: And (mention) Ishmael, and Idris, and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were of the steadfast.

85.author: Qarai
85.text: And [remember] Ishmael, Idris, and Dhul-Kifl—each of them was among the patient.

85.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
85.text: And Ishmael, Idris, and ThulKifl (the son of Job) each were patient.

85.author: Saheeh International
85.text: And [mention] Ishmael and Idrees and Dhul-Kifl; all were of the patient.

85.author: Sarwar
85.text: Ismael, Idris and Dhul Kifl all were people of great patience.

85.author: Shakir
85.text: And Ismail and Idris and Zulkifl; all were of the patient ones;

85.author: Wahiduddin Khan
85.text: Remember Ishmael and Idris and Dhul Kifl: they were all patient and steadfast.

85.author: Yusuf Ali
85.text: And (remember) Isma'il, Idris, and Zul-kifl, all (men) of constancy and patience;

85.author: Transliteration
85.text: WaismaAAeela waidreesa watha alkifli kullun mina alssabireena

86.author: Ahmed Ali
86.text: And they were admitted to Our grace. Verily they were among the doers of good.

86.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
86.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy; indeed they are among those who deserve Our proximity.

86.author: Arberry
86.text: and We admitted them into Our mercy; they were of the righteous.

86.author: Daryabadi
86.text: And We caused them to enter into our mercy, verily they are of the righteous.

86.author: Hilali & Khan
86.text: And We admitted them to Our Mercy. Verily, they were of the righteous.

86.author: Itani
86.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy. They were among the righteous.

86.author: Maududi
86.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy, for they were of the righteous.

86.author: Mubarakpuri
86.text: And We admitted them to Our mercy. Verily, they were of the righteous.

86.author: Pickthall
86.text: And We brought them in unto Our mercy. Lo! they are among the righteous.

86.author: Qarai
86.text: We admitted them into Our mercy. Indeed, they were among the righteous.

86.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
86.text: We admitted them to Our Mercy, for they were of the righteous.

86.author: Saheeh International
86.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy. Indeed, they were of the righteous.

86.author: Sarwar
86.text: We encompassed them in Our mercy; they were righteous people.

86.author: Shakir
86.text: And We caused them to enter into Our mercy, surely they were of the good ones.

86.author: Wahiduddin Khan
86.text: We admitted them to Our mercy. They were all righteous men.

86.author: Yusuf Ali
86.text: We admitted them to Our mercy: for they were of the righteous ones.

86.author: Transliteration
86.text: Waadkhalnahum fee rahmatina innahum mina alssaliheena

87.author: Ahmed Ali
87.text: And (remember) Dhu'n-Noon (Jonah of the fish), when he went away in anger and imagined We will not test him (with distress). Then he called out from the darkness: "There is no god other than You. All glory to You; surely I was a sinner.

87.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
87.text: And remember Zun-Noon,* when he left in anger, assuming that We would not restrict him – he therefore called out in the realms of darkness, saying, “There is no God except You, Purity is to You; I have indeed committed a lapse.” (* Prophet Yunus – peace and blessings upon him)

87.author: Arberry
87.text: And Dhul Nun -- when he went forth enraged and thought that We would have no power over him; then he called out in the darkness, 'There is no god but Thou. Glory be to Thee! I have done evil.'

87.author: Daryabadi
87.text: And Zun-nun! recall what time he departed in anger and imagined that We could not straiten him, then he cried in the darknesses, that: there is no god but Thou! hallowed be Thou! verily I have been of the wrong-doers.

87.author: Hilali & Khan
87.text: And (remember) Dhan-Nun (Jonah), when he went off in anger, and imagined that We shall not punish him (i.e. the calamites which had befallen him)! But he cried through the darkness (saying): La ilaha illa Anta [none has the right to be worshipped but You (O Allah)], Glorified (and Exalted) are You [above all that (evil) they associate with You]. Truly, I have been of the wrong-doers."

87.author: Itani
87.text: And Jonah, when he stormed out in fury, thinking We had no power over him. But then He cried out in the darkness, “There is no god but You! Glory to You! I was one of the wrongdoers!”

87.author: Maududi
87.text: And We bestowed Our favour upon Dhu al-Nun. Recall, when he went forth enraged, thinking We have no power to take him to task. Eventually he cried out in the darkness:" There is no god but You. Glory be to You! I have done wrong."

87.author: Mubarakpuri
87.text: And (remember) Dhun-Nun, when he went off in anger, and imagined that We shall not punish him! But he cried through the darknesses (Zulumat) (saying): "There is no God but You, Glorified be You! Truly, I have been of the Zalimin wrongdoers."

87.author: Pickthall
87.text: And (mention) Dhu'n-Nun, when he went off in anger and deemed that We had no power over him, but he cried out in the darkness, saying: There is no Allah save Thee. Be Thou Glorified! Lo! I have been a wrong-doer.

87.author: Qarai
87.text: And [remember] the Man of the Fish, when he left in a rage, thinking that We would not put him to hardship. Then he cried out in the darkness, ‘There is no god except You! You are immaculate! I have indeed been among the wrongdoers!’

87.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
87.text: And ThulNun (Prophet Jonah), he went away in anger thinking We had no power over him. But in the darkness he cried: 'There is no god except You. Exaltations to You! I was among the harmdoers'

87.author: Saheeh International
87.text: And [mention] the man of the fish, when he went off in anger and thought that We would not decree [anything] upon him. And he called out within the darknesses, "There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers."

87.author: Sarwar
87.text: Dhun Nun went away in anger and thought that We would never have power over him, but in darkness he cried, "Lord, You are the Only God whom I glorify. I have certainly done wrong to myself (so forgive me)".

87.author: Shakir
87.text: And Yunus, when he went away in wrath, so he thought that We would not straiten him, so he called out among afflictions: There is no god but Thou, glory be to Thee; surely I am of those who make themselves to suffer loss.

87.author: Wahiduddin Khan
87.text: Remember the man in the whale [Jonah] when he went away in anger, thinking We had no power over him. But he cried out in the darkness, "There is no deity but You. Glory be to You! I was indeed wrong."

87.author: Yusuf Ali
87.text: And remember Zun-nun, when he departed in wrath: He imagined that We had no power over him! But he cried through the deptHs of darkness, "There is no god but thou: glory to thee: I was indeed wrong!"

87.author: Transliteration
87.text: Watha alnnooni ith thahaba mughadiban fathanna an lan naqdira AAalayhi fanada fee alththulumati an la ilaha illa anta subhanaka innee kuntu mina alththalimeena

88.author: Ahmed Ali
88.text: We heard his cry, and saved him from the anguish. That is how We deliver those who believe.

88.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
88.text: We therefore heard his prayer and rescued him from grief; and similarly We shall rescue the Muslims.

88.author: Arberry
88.text: So We answered him, and delivered him out of grief; even so do, We deliver the believers.

88.author: Daryabadi
88.text: So We answered him, and We delivered him from the distress, and Thus do We deliver the believers.

88.author: Hilali & Khan
88.text: So We answered his call, and delivered him from the distress. And thus We do deliver the believers (who believe in the Oneness of Allah, abstain from evil and work righteousness).

88.author: Itani
88.text: So We answered him, and saved him from the affliction. Thus We save the faithful.

88.author: Maududi
88.text: Thereupon We accepted his prayer, and rescued him from grief. Thus do We rescue the believers.

88.author: Mubarakpuri
88.text: So We answered his call, and delivered him from the distress. And thus We do deliver the believers.

88.author: Pickthall
88.text: Then we heard his prayer and saved him from the anguish. Thus we save believers.

88.author: Qarai
88.text: So We answered his prayer and delivered him from the agony; and thus do We deliver the faithful.

88.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
88.text: We heard his prayer and saved him from grief. As such We shall save the believers.

88.author: Saheeh International
88.text: So We responded to him and saved him from the distress. And thus do We save the believers.

88.author: Sarwar
88.text: We answered his prayer and saved him from his grief. Thus We save the faithful ones.

88.author: Shakir
88.text: So We responded to him and delivered him from the grief and thus do We deliver the believers.

88.author: Wahiduddin Khan
88.text: So We heard his prayer and delivered him from sorrow. Thus shall We deliver the true believers.

88.author: Yusuf Ali
88.text: So We listened to him: and delivered him from distress: and thus do We deliver those who have faith.

88.author: Transliteration
88.text: Faistajabna lahu wanajjaynahu mina alghammi wakathalika nunjee almumineena

89.author: Ahmed Ali
89.text: (Remember) Zachariah when he called to his Lord: "Do not leave me alone (and childless), for you are the best of givers."

89.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
89.text: And remember Zakaria, when he prayed to his Lord, “O my Lord – do not leave me alone, and You are the Best Inheritor.

89.author: Arberry
89.text: And Zachariah -- when he called unto his Lord, 'O my Lord, leave me not solitary; though Thou art the best of inheritors.'

89.author: Daryabadi
89.text: And Zakariyya! recall what time he cried unto his Lord: my Lord! leave me not solitary, though Thou art the best of heirs.

89.author: Hilali & Khan
89.text: And (remember) Zakariya (Zachariah), when he cried to his Lord: "O My Lord! Leave me not single (childless), though You are the Best of the inheritors."

89.author: Itani
89.text: And Zechariah, when he called out to his Lord, “My Lord, do not leave me alone, even though you are the Best of heirs.”

89.author: Maududi
89.text: And We bestowed favour upon Zechariah, when he cried to his Lord: "Lord! Leave me not solitary (without any issue). You are the Best Inheritor."

89.author: Mubarakpuri
89.text: And (remember) Zakariyya, when he cried to his Lord: "O My Lord! Leave me not single (childless), though You are the Best of the inheritors."

89.author: Pickthall
89.text: And Zachariah, when he cried unto his Lord: My Lord! Leave me not childless, though Thou art the Best of inheritors.

89.author: Qarai
89.text: And [remember] Zechariah, when he cried out to his Lord, ‘My Lord! Do not leave me without an heir, and You are the best of inheritors.’

89.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
89.text: And Zachariah when he called to his Lord saying: 'Lord, do not let me remain by myself; You are the Best of inheritors'

89.author: Saheeh International
89.text: And [mention] Zechariah, when he called to his Lord, "My Lord, do not leave me alone [with no heir], while you are the best of inheritors."

89.author: Sarwar
89.text: Zachariah prayed, "Lord, do not leave me alone without offspring, although you are the best heir".

89.author: Shakir
89.text: And Zakariya, when he cried to his Lord: O my Lord leave me not alone; and Thou art the best of inheritors.

89.author: Wahiduddin Khan
89.text: Remember Zachariah, when he called out to his Lord, "Do not leave me heirless Lord, You are the best of heirs."

89.author: Yusuf Ali
89.text: And (remember) Zakariya, when he cried to his Lord: "O my Lord! leave me not without offspring, though thou art the best of inheritors."

89.author: Transliteration
89.text: Wazakariyya ith nada rabbahu rabbi la tatharnee fardan waanta khayru alwaritheena

90.author: Ahmed Ali
90.text: So We heard him and gave him John, and cured his wife (of barrenness). These were men who vied in good deeds with one another, and prayed to Us with love and awe, and were meek before Us.

90.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
90.text: We therefore heard his prayer; and bestowed him Yahya, and cured his wife for him; indeed they used to hasten to perform good deeds, and pray to Us with hope and fear; and used to weep before Us.

90.author: Arberry
90.text: So We answered him, and bestowed on him John, and We set his wife right for him; truly they vied with one another, hastening to good works, and called upon Us out of yearning and awe; and they were humble to Us.

90.author: Daryabadi
90.text: So We answered him, and We bestowed on him Yahya and We made sound for him his spouse. Verily they Were wont to vie with one anot her in good deeds and to call upon us with longing and dread, and they were ever before us meek.

90.author: Hilali & Khan
90.text: So We answered his call, and We bestowed upon him Yahya (John), and cured his wife (to bear a child) for him. Verily, they used to hasten on to do good deeds, and they used to call on Us with hope and fear, and used to humble themselves before Us.

90.author: Itani
90.text: So We answered him, and gave him John. And We cured his wife for him. They used to vie in doing righteous deeds, and used to call on Us in love and awe, and they used to humble themselves to Us.

90.author: Maududi
90.text: So We accepted his prayer and bestowed upon him John, and We made his wife fit (to bear a child). Verily they hastened in doing good works and called upon Us with longing and fear, and humbled themselves to Us.

90.author: Mubarakpuri
90.text: So We answered his call, and We bestowed upon him Yahya, and cured his wife for him. Verily, they used to hasten on to do good deeds, and they used to call on Us with hope and fear, and they were Khashi`in before Us.

90.author: Pickthall
90.text: Then We heard his prayer, and bestowed upon him John, and adjusted his wife (to bear a child) for him. Lo! they used to vie one with the other in good deeds, and they cried unto Us in longing and in fear, and were submissive unto Us.

90.author: Qarai
90.text: So We answered his prayer, and gave him John, and cured for him his wife [of infertility]. Indeed, they were active in [performing] good works, and they would supplicate Us with eagerness and awe and were humble before Us.

90.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
90.text: So We answered him, and gave him John, curing his wife (of sterility). They raced with each other in good works and called on Us out of yearning and awe, and they were humble to Us.

90.author: Saheeh International
90.text: So We responded to him, and We gave to him John, and amended for him his wife. Indeed, they used to hasten to good deeds and supplicate Us in hope and fear, and they were to Us humbly submissive.

90.author: Sarwar
90.text: We answered his prayer and granted him his son, John, by making his wife fruitful. They were people who would compete with each other in good deeds and pray to Us with love and reverence. With Us they were all humble people.

90.author: Shakir
90.text: So We responded to him and gave him Yahya and made his wife fit for him; surely they used to hasten, one with another In deeds of goodness and to call upon Us, hoping and fearing and they were humble before Us.

90.author: Wahiduddin Khan
90.text: So We heard his prayer and bestowed John upon him and made his wife fit to bear him a child. They used to hasten to do good and they called on Us in hope and fear, and they were always humble towards Us.

90.author: Yusuf Ali
90.text: So We listened to him: and We granted him Yahya: We cured his wife's (Barrenness) for him. These (three) were ever quick in emulation in good works; they used to call on Us with love and reverence, and humble themselves before Us.

90.author: Transliteration
90.text: Faistajabna lahu wawahabna lahu yahya waaslahna lahu zawjahu innahum kanoo yusariAAoona fee alkhayrati wayadAAoonana raghaban warahaban wakanoo lana khashiAAeena

91.author: Ahmed Ali
91.text: (Remember) her who preserved her chastity, into whom We breathed a new life from Us, and made her and her son a token for mankind.

91.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
91.text: And remember the woman who maintained her chastity, We therefore breathed Our Spirit into her and made her and her son a sign for the entire world.

91.author: Arberry
91.text: And she who guarded her virginity, so We breathed into her of Our spirit and appointed her and her son to be a sign unto all beings.

91.author: Daryabadi
91.text: And she who guarded her chastity! Then We breathed into her of Our spirit, and made her and her son a sign unto the worlds.

91.author: Hilali & Khan
91.text: And (remember) she who guarded her chastity [Virgin Maryam (Mary)], We breathed into (the sleeves of) her (shirt or garment) [through Our Ruh - Jibrael (Gabriel)], and We made her and her son ['Iesa (Jesus)] a sign for Al-'Alamin (the mankind and jinns).

91.author: Itani
91.text: And she who guarded her virginity. We breathed into her of Our spirit, and made her and her son a sign to the world.

91.author: Maududi
91.text: And also recall the woman who guarded her chastity: We breathed into her of Our spirit, and made her and her son a Sign to the whole world.

91.author: Mubarakpuri
91.text: And she who guarded her chastity, We breathed into her through Our Ruh, and We made her and her son a sign for nations.

91.author: Pickthall
91.text: And she who was chaste, therefor We breathed into her (something) of Our Spirit and made her and her son a token for (all) peoples.

91.author: Qarai
91.text: And [remember] her who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into her Our spirit, and made her and her son a sign for all the nations.

91.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
91.text: And she (Mary) who guarded her virginity. We breathed into her of Our spirit (Gabriel), and made her and her son a sign for the worlds.

91.author: Saheeh International
91.text: And [mention] the one who guarded her chastity, so We blew into her [garment] through Our angel [Gabriel], and We made her and her son a sign for the worlds.

91.author: Sarwar
91.text: Into the woman who maintained her chastity We breathed Our Spirit and made her and her son a miracle for all people.

91.author: Shakir
91.text: And she who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into her of Our inspiration and made her and her son a sign for the nations.

91.author: Wahiduddin Khan
91.text: Remember the one who guarded her chastity; so We breathed Our Spirit into her, and made her and her son a sign for all people.

91.author: Yusuf Ali
91.text: And (remember) her who guarded her chastity: We breathed into her of Our spirit, and We made her and her son a sign for all peoples.

91.author: Transliteration
91.text: Waallatee ahsanat farjaha fanafakhna feeha min roohina wajaAAalnaha waibnaha ayatan lilAAalameena

92.author: Ahmed Ali
92.text: Verily this your order is one order, and I am your Lord; so worship Me.

92.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
92.text: Indeed this religion of yours, is one religion; and I am your Lord, therefore worship Me.

92.author: Arberry
92.text: 'Surely this community of yours is one community, and I am your Lord; so serve Me.'

92.author: Daryabadi
92.text: Verily this community of yours is a single community, and I am your Lord; so worship Me.

92.author: Hilali & Khan
92.text: Truly! This, your Ummah [Sharia or religion (Islamic Monotheism)] is one religion, and I am your Lord, therefore worship Me (Alone). [Tafsir Ibn Kathir]

92.author: Itani
92.text: This community of yours is one community, and I am your Lord, so worship Me.

92.author: Maududi
92.text: Verily this community of yours is a single community, and I am your Lord; so worship Me.

92.author: Mubarakpuri
92.text: Truly, this, your Ummah is one, and I am your Lord, therefore worship Me.

92.author: Pickthall
92.text: Lo! this, your religion, is one religion, and I am your Lord, so worship Me.

92.author: Qarai
92.text: Indeed this community of yours is one community, and I am your Lord. So worship Me.

92.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
92.text: Indeed, this nation of yours is one nation, and I am Your Lord, therefore worship Me.

92.author: Saheeh International
92.text: Indeed this, your religion, is one religion, and I am your Lord, so worship Me.

92.author: Sarwar
92.text: People, you are one nation and I am your Lord. Worship Me.

92.author: Shakir
92.text: Surely this Islam is your religion, one religion (only), and I am your Lord, therefore serve Me.

92.author: Wahiduddin Khan
92.text: This community of yours is one community and I am your Lord, so worship Me.

92.author: Yusuf Ali
92.text: Verily, this brotherhood of yours is a single brotherhood, and I am your Lord and Cherisher: therefore serve Me (and no other).

92.author: Transliteration
92.text: Inna hathihi ommatukum ommatan wahidatan waana rabbukum faoAAbudooni

93.author: Ahmed Ali
93.text: But they split up the order among themselves; (yet) all of them have to come back to Me.

93.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
93.text: And others have shattered their works into pieces among themselves; all have to return to Us.

93.author: Arberry
93.text: But they split up their affair between them; all shall return to Us.

93.author: Daryabadi
93.text: And they cut up their affair among them:, all are unto Us returners.

93.author: Hilali & Khan
93.text: But they have broken up and differed as regards their religion among themselves. (And) they all shall return to Us.

93.author: Itani
93.text: But they splintered themselves into factions. They will all return to Us.

93.author: Maududi
93.text: But they tore asunder their faith into many parts. But to Us they are bound to return.

93.author: Mubarakpuri
93.text: But they have broken up and differed in their religion among themselves. (And) they all shall return to Us.

93.author: Pickthall
93.text: And they have broken their religion (into fragments) among them, (yet) all are returning unto Us.

93.author: Qarai
93.text: They have fragmented their religion among themselves, but everyone of them will return to Us.

93.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
93.text: But they (the Christians and Jews) split their affair between them all shall return to Us.

93.author: Saheeh International
93.text: And [yet] they divided their affair among themselves, [but] all to Us will return.

93.author: Sarwar
93.text: People have divided themselves into many sects, but all will return to Us.

93.author: Shakir
93.text: And they broke their religion (into sects) between them: to Us shall all come back.

93.author: Wahiduddin Khan
93.text: But they divided themselves into factions, but to Us they shall all return.

93.author: Yusuf Ali
93.text: But (later generations) cut off their affair (of unity), one from another: (yet) will they all return to Us.

93.author: Transliteration
93.text: WataqattaAAoo amrahum baynahum kullun ilayna rajiAAoona

94.author: Ahmed Ali
94.text: So he who does the right and is a believer, will not have his labour denied, for We are congnisant of it.

94.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
94.text: So whoever does some good deeds, and is a believer, then his efforts will not be ignored; and We are recording it.

94.author: Arberry
94.text: And whosoever does deeds of righteousness, being a believer, no unthankfulness shall befall his endeavour; We Ourselves write it down for him.

94.author: Daryabadi
94.text: Whosoever worketh righteous works, and he is a believer, there shall be no denial of his endeavour; and We are for him the Writers.

94.author: Hilali & Khan
94.text: So whoever does righteous good deeds while he is a believer (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism), his efforts will not be rejected. Verily! We record it in his Book of deeds.

94.author: Itani
94.text: Whoever does righteous deeds, and is a believer, his effort will not be denied. We are writing it down for him.

94.author: Maududi
94.text: Then whosoever does righteous works, while believing, his striving will not go unappreciated. We record them all for him.

94.author: Mubarakpuri
94.text: So whoever does righteous good deeds while he is a believer, his efforts will not be rejected. Verily, We record it for him (in his Book of deeds).

94.author: Pickthall
94.text: Then whoso doeth some good works and is a believer, there will be no rejection of his effort. Lo! We record (it) for him.

94.author: Qarai
94.text: Whoever is faithful and does righteous deeds, his endeavour shall not go unappreciated, and We will indeed record it for him.

94.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
94.text: The endeavors of he who believes and does good works shall not go unthanked. We write it down for him.

94.author: Saheeh International
94.text: So whoever does righteous deeds while he is a believer - no denial will there be for his effort, and indeed We, of it, are recorders.

94.author: Sarwar
94.text: The reward of the righteously striving believers will not be neglected. We are keeping the record of their good deeds.

94.author: Shakir
94.text: Therefore whoever shall do of good deeds and he is a believer, there shall be no denying of his exertion, and surely We will write (It) down for him.

94.author: Wahiduddin Khan
94.text: He who does good works while he is a believer, shall not see his efforts disregarded: We record them all.

94.author: Yusuf Ali
94.text: Whoever works any act of righteousness and has faith,- His endeavour will not be rejected: We shall record it in his favour.

94.author: Transliteration
94.text: Faman yaAAmal mina alssalihati wahuwa muminun fala kufrana lisaAAyihi wainna lahu katiboona

95.author: Ahmed Ali
95.text: It is imperative that a habitation We have destroyed will not desist (from unbelief)

95.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
95.text: And it is forbidden for any township which We have destroyed, that they may return. (Once the disbelievers face death, their return to earth is impossible.)

95.author: Arberry
95.text: There is a ban upon any city that We have destroyed; they shall not return

95.author: Daryabadi
95.text: And a ban is laid on every town which We have destroyed, that they Shall not return.

95.author: Hilali & Khan
95.text: And a ban is laid on every town (population) which We have destroyed that they shall not return (to this world again, nor repent to Us).

95.author: Itani
95.text: There is a ban on the town that We had destroyed—that they will not return.

95.author: Maududi
95.text: It has been ordained against every town that We ever destroyed that they shall not return (to enjoy a new lease of life)

95.author: Mubarakpuri
95.text: And a ban is laid on every town which We have destroyed that they shall not return.

95.author: Pickthall
95.text: And there is a ban upon any community which We have destroyed: that they shall not return.

95.author: Qarai
95.text: It is forbidden for [the people of] any town that We have destroyed [to return to the world]: they shall not return,

95.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
95.text: (It is ordained that) no village We have destroyed shall return

95.author: Saheeh International
95.text: And there is prohibition upon [the people of] a city which We have destroyed that they will [ever] return

95.author: Sarwar
95.text: The people whom We destroyed can never return to this world

95.author: Shakir
95.text: And it is binding on a town which We destroy that they shall not return.

95.author: Wahiduddin Khan
95.text: It is ordained that no nation We have destroyed shall ever rise again,

95.author: Yusuf Ali
95.text: But there is a ban on any population which We have destroyed: that they shall not return,

95.author: Transliteration
95.text: Waharamun AAala qaryatin ahlaknaha annahum la yarjiAAoona

96.author: Ahmed Ali
96.text: Until when the way is opened up for Gog and Magog and they press from every elevated place,

96.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
96.text: Till the time when Yajuj and Majuj are released, and they will be flowing down from every height.

96.author: Arberry
96.text: till, when Gog and Magog are unloosed, and they slide down out of every slope,

96.author: Daryabadi
96.text: Until when Ya'juj and Ma'juj are let out, and from every mound they are trickling down.

96.author: Hilali & Khan
96.text: Until, when Ya'juj and Ma'juj (Gog and Magog) are let loose (from their barrier), and they swiftly swarm from every mound.

96.author: Itani
96.text: Until, when Gog and Magog are let loose, and they swarm down from every mound.

96.author: Maududi
96.text: until Gog and Magog are let loose, and begin swooping from every mound,

96.author: Mubarakpuri
96.text: Until, when Ya'juj and Ma'juj (Gog and Magog people) are let loose, and they swoop down from every Hadab.

96.author: Pickthall
96.text: Until, when Gog and Magog are let loose, and they hasten out of every mound,

96.author: Qarai
96.text: until when Gog and Magog are let loose, and they race down from every slope,

96.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
96.text: till Gog and Magog are let loose and slide down out of every slope;

96.author: Saheeh International
96.text: Until when [the dam of] Gog and Magog has been opened and they, from every elevation, descend

96.author: Sarwar
96.text: until Gog and Magog are let loose to rush down from the hills.

96.author: Shakir
96.text: Even when Gog and Magog are let loose and they shall break forth from every elevated place.

96.author: Wahiduddin Khan
96.text: but when Gog and Magog are let loose and swarm down from every hillside and they spread out, [leaping across every barrier of land and sea],

96.author: Yusuf Ali
