96.text: Until the Gog and Magog (people) are let through (their barrier), and they swiftly swarm from every hill.

96.author: Transliteration
96.text: Hatta itha futihat yajooju wamajooju wahum min kulli hadabin yansiloona

97.author: Ahmed Ali
97.text: And the certain promise (of Doom) comes near. Then the eyes of unbelievers will be fixed in horror, (and they will cry:) "Ah, woe betide, we were indeed heedless of this, and were oppressors and unjust."

97.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
97.text: And the True Promise has come near – thereupon the eyes of the disbelievers will become fixed, staring wide; saying “Woe to us – we were in neglect of this, but in fact we were unjust.”

97.author: Arberry
97.text: and nigh has drawn the true promise, and behold, the eyes of the unbelievers staring: 'Alas for us! We were heedless of this; 'nay, we were evildoers.'

97.author: Daryabadi
97.text: And there shall approach the true promise; and lo! the eyes of those who disbelieved shall be staring: woe unto us! surely we have been in neglect thereof; aye! we have been the wrong-doers.

97.author: Hilali & Khan
97.text: And the true promise (Day of Resurrection) shall draw near (of fulfillment). Then (when mankind is resurrected from their graves), you shall see the eyes of the disbelievers fixedly stare in horror. (They will say): "Woe to us! We were indeed heedless of this; nay, but we were Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc.)."

97.author: Itani
97.text: The promise of truth has drawn near. The eyes of those who disbelieved will stare in horror: “Woe to us. We were oblivious to this. In fact, we were wrongdoers.”

97.author: Maududi
97.text: and the time for the fulfilment of the true promise of Allah draws near, whereupon the eyes of those who disbelieved will stare in fear, and they will say: "Woe to us, we were indeed heedless of this; nay, we were wrongdoers."

97.author: Mubarakpuri
97.text: And the true promise shall draw near. Then, you shall see the eyes of the disbelievers fixedly staring in horror. (They will say:) "Woe to us! We were indeed heedless of this -- nay, but we were wrongdoers."

97.author: Pickthall
97.text: And the True Promise draweth nigh; then behold them, staring wide (in terror), the eyes of those who disbelieve! (They say): Alas for us! We (lived) in forgetfulness of this. Ah, but we were wrong-doers!

97.author: Qarai
97.text: and the true promise draws near [to its fulfillment], behold, the faithless will look on with a fixed gaze: ‘Woe to us! We have certainly been oblivious of this! Indeed, we have been wrongdoers!’

97.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
97.text: when the true promise draws near; the eyes of the unbelievers will stare (and they will say): 'Alas for us! Of this we have been heedless. We have beenharmdoers'

97.author: Saheeh International
97.text: And [when] the true promise has approached; then suddenly the eyes of those who disbelieved will be staring [in horror, while they say], "O woe to us; we had been unmindful of this; rather, we were wrongdoers."

97.author: Sarwar
97.text: The Day of Judgment will then draw near and the unbelievers will stare amazedly and cry, "Woe to us! We had neglected this day. We have done wrong".

97.author: Shakir
97.text: And the true promise shall draw nigh, then lo! the eyes of those who disbelieved shall be fixedly open: O woe to us! surely we were in a state of heedlessness as to this; nay, we were unjust.

97.author: Wahiduddin Khan
97.text: when the true promise of God draws near, those who denied the truth will stare in amazement, crying, "Woe to us! We have been so heedless of this. Indeed, we were wrongdoers."

97.author: Yusuf Ali
97.text: Then will the true promise draw nigh (of fulfilment): then behold! the eyes of the Unbelievers will fixedly stare in horror: "Ah! Woe to us! we were indeed heedless of this; nay, we truly did wrong!"

97.author: Transliteration
97.text: Waiqtaraba alwaAAdu alhaqqu faitha hiya shakhisatun absaru allatheena kafaroo ya waylana qad kunna fee ghaflatin min hatha bal kunna thalimeena

98.author: Ahmed Ali
98.text: Verily you and those you worship other than God will be faggots for Hell; and come to it you will.

98.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
98.text: “Indeed you* and all that you worship** besides Allah, are the fuel of hell; in it you must go.” (* All disbelievers ** Idols and disbelievers who claimed to be Gods. The Prophets like Eisa and Uzair who were worshipped are exempt from this, and so are Maryam, and trees and the moon etc.)

98.author: Arberry
98.text: 'Surely you, and that you were serving apart from God, are fuel for Gehenna; you shall go down to it.'

98.author: Daryabadi
98.text: Verily ye and whatsoever ye worship beside Allah shall be firewood for Hell: thereunto ye shall go down.

98.author: Hilali & Khan
98.text: Certainly! You (disbelievers) and that which you are worshipping now besides Allah, are (but) fuel for Hell! (Surely), you will enter it.

98.author: Itani
98.text: You and what you worship besides God are fuel for Hell. You will descend into it.

98.author: Maududi
98.text: (They will be told): "Verily you and the gods you worshipped beside Allah are the fuel of Hell. All of you are bound to arrive there.

98.author: Mubarakpuri
98.text: Certainly you and that which you are worshipping now besides Allah, are (but) fuel (Hasab) for Hell! (Surely) you will enter it.

98.author: Pickthall
98.text: Lo! ye (idolaters) and that which ye worship beside Allah are fuel of hell. Thereunto ye will come.

98.author: Qarai
98.text: Indeed you and what [idols] you worship besides Allah shall be fuel for hell, and you will enter it.

98.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
98.text: You and all those who you were worshipping, other than Allah, shall be the fuel of Gehenna; there you shall all go down to it.

98.author: Saheeh International
98.text: Indeed, you [disbelievers] and what you worship other than Allah are the firewood of Hell. You will be coming to [enter] it.

98.author: Sarwar
98.text: They will be told, "You and what you had worshipped instead of God will be the fuel of hell".

98.author: Shakir
98.text: Surely you and what you worship besides Allah are the firewood of hell; to it you shall come.

98.author: Wahiduddin Khan
98.text: You and what you worship instead of God will be fuel for hell: to it you shall all come --

98.author: Yusuf Ali
98.text: Verily ye, (unbelievers), and the (false) gods that ye worship besides Allah, are (but) fuel for Hell! to it will ye (surely) come!

98.author: Transliteration
98.text: Innakum wama taAAbudoona min dooni Allahi hasabu jahannama antum laha waridoona

99.author: Ahmed Ali
99.text: Had they really been gods they would not have entered it: They will all abide in it for ever.

99.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
99.text: “Had these been Gods they would not go into hell; and they have to remain in it.”

99.author: Arberry
99.text: If those had been gods, they would never have gone down to it; yet every one of them shall therein abide forever;'

99.author: Daryabadi
99.text: Had these been gods, they would not have gone down thereunto; and all of them therein shall be abiders.

99.author: Hilali & Khan
99.text: Had these (idols, etc.) been aliha (gods), they would not have entered there (Hell), and all of them will abide therein.

99.author: Itani
99.text: Had these been gods, they would not have descended into it. All will abide in it.

99.author: Maududi
99.text: Had these indeed been gods they would not have gone there. But (as it is), all of you shall ever abide in it."

99.author: Mubarakpuri
99.text: Had these been gods, they would not have entered there (Hell), and all of them will abide therein forever.

99.author: Pickthall
99.text: If these had been gods they would not have come thither, but all will abide therein.

99.author: Qarai
99.text: Had they been gods, they would not have entered it, and they will all remain in it [forever].

99.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
99.text: If those had been gods, they would never have gone down to it, but in it they shall live for ever.

99.author: Saheeh International
99.text: Had these [false deities] been [actual] gods, they would not have come to it, but all are eternal therein.

99.author: Sarwar
99.text: Had the idols been true lords, they would not have gone to hell. "All of you will live therein forever".

99.author: Shakir
99.text: Had these been gods, they would not have come to it and all shall abide therein.

99.author: Wahiduddin Khan
99.text: if those had really been deities, they would not have been led there; but there they will remain forever.

99.author: Yusuf Ali
99.text: If these had been gods, they would not have got there! but each one will abide therein.

99.author: Transliteration
99.text: Law kana haolai alihatan ma waradooha wakullun feeha khalidoona

100.author: Ahmed Ali
100.text: There will only be groaning for them, and they will not hear any thing.

100.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
100.text: They will bray in it and not be able to hear anything in it.

100.author: Arberry
100.text: there shall be sighing for them therein, and naught they shall hear.

100.author: Daryabadi
100.text: Theirs therein shall be roaring, and therein they shall hear not.

100.author: Hilali & Khan
100.text: Therein breathing out with deep sighs and roaring will be their portion, and therein they will hear not.

100.author: Itani
100.text: In it they will wail. In it they will not hear.

100.author: Maududi
100.text: There they shall groan with anguish and the din and noise in Hell will not let them hear anything.

100.author: Mubarakpuri
100.text: Therein they will be breathing out with deep sighs and roaring and therein they will hear not.

100.author: Pickthall
100.text: Therein wailing is their portion, and therein they hear not.

100.author: Qarai
100.text: Their lot therein will be groaning, and they will not hear anything in it.

100.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
100.text: There is groaning for them therein, and they do not hear.

100.author: Saheeh International
100.text: For them therein is heavy sighing, and they therein will not hear.

100.author: Sarwar
100.text: They will groan in pain therein, but no one will listen to them.

100.author: Shakir
100.text: For them therein shall be groaning and therein they shall not hear.

100.author: Wahiduddin Khan
100.text: They shall groan. They will not hear therein anything else.

100.author: Yusuf Ali
100.text: There, sobbing will be their lot, nor will they there hear (aught else).

100.author: Transliteration
100.text: Lahum feeha zafeerun wahum feeha la yasmaAAoona

101.author: Ahmed Ali
101.text: But those for whom a good reward had been fore-ordained by Us, will be far removed from it,

101.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
101.text: Indeed those to whom Our promise of goodness has been made, have been kept far away from hell.

101.author: Arberry
101.text: But as for those unto whom already the reward most fair has gone forth from Us,

101.author: Daryabadi
101.text: Verily those for whom the good reward hath preceded from Us, they therefrom shall be kept far away.

101.author: Hilali & Khan
101.text: Verily those for whom the good has preceded from Us, they will be removed far therefrom (Hell) [e.g. 'Iesa (Jesus), son of Maryam (Mary); 'Uzair (Ezra), etc.].

101.author: Itani
101.text: As for those who deserved goodness from Us—these will be kept away from it.

101.author: Maududi
101.text: But for those whom We had decided to favour with good reward, they shall be kept far removed from Hell.

101.author: Mubarakpuri
101.text: Verily, those for whom the good has preceded from Us, they will be removed far therefrom (Hell).

101.author: Pickthall
101.text: Lo! those unto whom kindness hath gone forth before from Us, they will be far removed from thence.

101.author: Qarai
101.text: Indeed those to whom there has gone beforehand [the promise of] the best reward from Us will be kept away from it.

101.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
101.text: But those whom We have surpassed with the finest (rank) from Us shall be far removed from it,

101.author: Saheeh International
101.text: Indeed, those for whom the best [reward] has preceded from Us - they are from it far removed.

101.author: Sarwar
101.text: But those to whom We have already promised blessings will be far away from hell.

101.author: Shakir
101.text: Surely (as for) those for whom the good has already gone forth from Us, they shall be kept far off from it;

101.author: Wahiduddin Khan
101.text: But those who have been promised a good reward by Us will be kept far away from Hell --

101.author: Yusuf Ali
101.text: Those for whom the good (record) from Us has gone before, will be removed far therefrom.

101.author: Transliteration
101.text: Inna allatheena sabaqat lahum minna alhusna olaika AAanha mubAAadoona

102.author: Ahmed Ali
102.text: And will not hear its hissing, and will live for ever in the midst of what their hearts desire.

102.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
102.text: And they will not hear its faintest sound; and they will forever abide in whatever their hearts desire.

102.author: Arberry
102.text: they shall be, kept far from it neither shall they hear any whisper of it, and they shall dwell forever in that their souls desired;

102.author: Daryabadi
102.text: They shall not hear the least sound thereof, and in that felicity which their souls desire they shall be abiders.

102.author: Hilali & Khan
102.text: They shall not hear the slightest sound of it (Hell), while they abide in that which their ownselves desire.

102.author: Itani
102.text: They will not hear its hissing, and they will forever abide in what their hearts desire.

102.author: Maududi
102.text: They shall not hear even a whisper of it, and they shall live for ever in the delights which they had desired.

102.author: Mubarakpuri
102.text: They shall not hear the slightest sound of it (Hell), while they abide in that which their own selves desire.

102.author: Pickthall
102.text: They will not hear the slightest sound thereof, while they abide in that which their souls desire.

102.author: Qarai
102.text: They will not hear even its faint sound and they will remain [forever] in what their souls desire.

102.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
102.text: neither shall they hear any of its whisper, but shall live for ever in that their souls desired.

102.author: Saheeh International
102.text: They will not hear its sound, while they are, in that which their souls desire, abiding eternally.

102.author: Sarwar
102.text: They will not even hear the slightest sound from it while enjoying the best that they can wish for in their everlasting life.

102.author: Shakir
102.text: They will not hear its faintest sound, and they shall abide in that which their souls long for.

102.author: Wahiduddin Khan
102.text: they will not hear the slightest sound of it, and they shall forever abide in a state of bliss, among everything their souls longed for.

102.author: Yusuf Ali
102.text: Not the slightest sound will they hear of Hell: what their souls desired, in that will they dwell.

102.author: Transliteration
102.text: La yasmaAAoona haseesaha wahum fee ma ishtahat anfusuhum khalidoona

103.author: Ahmed Ali
103.text: They will have safety from the mighty terror, and angels will receive them, (saying:) "This is your day which had been promised you."

103.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
103.text: The greatest fear will not aggrieve them, and the angels will welcome them; saying “This is your day, which you were promised.”

103.author: Arberry
103.text: the greatest terror shall not grieve them, and the angels shall receive them: 'This is your day that you were promised.'

103.author: Daryabadi
103.text: The Great Terror shall grieve them not, and the angels will meet them: this is Your Day which ye were ever promised-

103.author: Hilali & Khan
103.text: The greatest terror (on the Day of Resurrection) will not grieve them, and the angels will meet them, (with the greeting): "This is your Day which you were promised."

103.author: Itani
103.text: The Supreme Fear will not worry them, and the angels will receive them: “This is your Day which you were promised.”

103.author: Maududi
103.text: The Hour of the Great Terror shall not grieve them, and the angels shall receive them saying: "This is your Day which you had been promised."

103.author: Mubarakpuri
103.text: The greatest terror will not grieve them, and the angels will meet them, (with the greeting:) "This is your Day which you were promised."

103.author: Pickthall
103.text: The Supreme Horror will not grieve them, and the angels will welcome them, (saying): This is your Day which ye were promised;

103.author: Qarai
103.text: The Great Terror will not upset them, and the angels will receive them [saying]: ‘This is your day which you were promised.’

103.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
103.text: The greatest terror shall not grieve them, and the angels will receive them: 'This is the Day you have been promised'

103.author: Saheeh International
103.text: They will not be grieved by the greatest terror, and the angels will meet them, [saying], "This is your Day which you have been promised" -

103.author: Sarwar
103.text: They will not be affected by the great terror. The angels will come to them with this glad news: "This is your day which was promised to you."

103.author: Shakir
103.text: The great fearful event shall not grieve them, and the angels shall meet them: This is your day which you were promised.

103.author: Wahiduddin Khan
103.text: The great Horror [of the Day of Judgement] shall not grieve them, and the angels will welcome them, saying, "This is your Day which you have been promised."

103.author: Yusuf Ali
103.text: The Great Terror will bring them no grief: but the angels will meet them (with mutual greetings): "This is your Day,- (the Day) that ye were promised."

103.author: Transliteration
103.text: La yahzunuhumu alfazaAAu alakbaru watatalaqqahumu almalaikatu hatha yawmukumu allathee kuntum tooAAadoona

104.author: Ahmed Ali
104.text: The day We shall roll up the heavens like a written scroll, We shall revert it (to nothing) as it was before We first created it. This is a promise incumbent on Us; We will certainly fulfil it.

104.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
104.text: The day when We shall roll up the heavens as the recording angel rolls up the register of deeds; We shall make him similar to Our making him the first time; this is a promise upon Us; We certainly have to do it.

104.author: Arberry
104.text: On the day when We shall roll up heaven as a scroll is rolled for the writings; as We originated the first creation, so We shall bring it back again -- a promise binding on Us; so We shall do.

104.author: Daryabadi
104.text: The Day whereon We shall roll up the heaven like as the rolling up of a scroll for books. Even as We began the first creation, We shall restore it: a promise binding upon Us; verily We have been the Doers.

104.author: Hilali & Khan
104.text: And (remember) the Day when We shall roll up the heavens like a scroll rolled up for books, as We began the first creation, We shall repeat it, (it is) a promise binding upon Us. Truly, We shall do it.

104.author: Itani
104.text: On the Day when We fold the heaven, like the folding of a book. Just as We began the first creation, We will repeat it—a promise binding on Us. We will act.

104.author: Maududi
104.text: On that Day We shall roll up the heavens like a scroll for writing. Even as We originated the creation first so We shall repeat it. This is a promise binding on Us; and so We shall do.

104.author: Mubarakpuri
104.text: And (remember) the Day when We shall roll up the heaven like a Sijill for books. As We began the first creation, We shall repeat it. (It is) a promise binding upon Us. Truly, We shall do it.

104.author: Pickthall
104.text: The Day when We shall roll up the heavens as a recorder rolleth up a written scroll. As We began the first creation, We shall repeat it. (It is) a promise (binding) upon Us. Lo! We are to perform it.

104.author: Qarai
104.text: The day We shall roll up the heaven, like rolling of the scrolls [meant] for writings. We will bring it back just as We began the first creation—a promise [binding] on Us. [That] indeed We will do.

104.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
104.text: On that Day, We shall roll up the heaven like a written scroll is rolled. As We originated the first creation, so will We bring it back again. This is a binding promise on Us which We shall assuredly fulfill.

104.author: Saheeh International
104.text: The Day when We will fold the heaven like the folding of a [written] sheet for the records. As We began the first creation, We will repeat it. [That is] a promise binding upon Us. Indeed, We will do it.

104.author: Sarwar
104.text: (This will happen) on the day when We roll up the heavens as if it were a written scroll and bring it back into existence just as though We had created it for the first time. This is what We have promised and We have always been true to Our promise.

104.author: Shakir
104.text: On the day when We will roll up heaven like the rolling up of the scroll for writings, as We originated the first creation, (so) We shall reproduce it; a promise (binding on Us); surely We will bring it about.

104.author: Wahiduddin Khan
104.text: On that Day We shall roll up the heavens like a scroll of parchment. As We originated the first creation, so shall We repeat it. This is a promise binding on Us. Truly, We shall fulfill it.

104.author: Yusuf Ali
104.text: The Day that We roll up the heavens like a scroll rolled up for books (completed),- even as We produced the first creation, so shall We produce a new one: a promise We have undertaken: truly shall We fulfil it.

104.author: Transliteration
104.text: Yawma natwee alssamaa katayyi alssijlli lilkutubi kama badana awwala khalqin nuAAeeduhu waAAdan AAalayna inna kunna faAAileena

105.author: Ahmed Ali
105.text: We had prescribed in the Book of Psalms after the reminder and admonition, that those of Our creatures who are good will in the end rule the earth.

105.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
105.text: And indeed We wrote, after the reminder in the Zaboor that, “My virtuous bondmen will inherit the earth.”

105.author: Arberry
105.text: For We have written in the Psalms, after the Remembrance, 'The earth shall be the inheritance of My righteous servants.'

105.author: Daryabadi
105.text: And assuredly We have prescribed in the Scripture after the admonition, that: the land! there shall inherit it My bondmen righteous.

105.author: Hilali & Khan
105.text: And indeed We have written in Zabur (Psalms) [i.e. all the revealed Holy Books the Taurat (Torah), the Injeel (Gospel), the Quran] after (We have already written in) Al-Lauh Al-Mahfuz (the Book, that is in the heaven with Allah), that My righteous slaves shall inherit the land (i.e. the land of Paradise).

105.author: Itani
105.text: We have written in the Psalms, after the Reminder, that the earth will be inherited by My righteous servants.

105.author: Maududi
105.text: Surely We wrote in the Psalms, after the exhortation, that the earth shall be inherited by My righteous servants.

105.author: Mubarakpuri
105.text: And indeed We have written in Az-Zabur after Adh-Dhikr that My righteous servants shall inherit the land.

105.author: Pickthall
105.text: And verily we have written in the Scripture, after the Reminder: My righteous slaves will inherit the earth:

105.author: Qarai
105.text: Certainly We wrote in the Psalms, after the Torah: ‘Indeed My righteous servants shall inherit the earth.’

105.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
105.text: We have written in the Psalms, after the Remembrance: 'The righteous among My worshipers shall inherit the earth'

105.author: Saheeh International
105.text: And We have already written in the book [of Psalms] after the [previous] mention that the land [of Paradise] is inherited by My righteous servants.

105.author: Sarwar
105.text: We have written in the psalms which We had revealed after the Torah that the earth will be given to Our righteous servants as their inheritance.

105.author: Shakir
105.text: And certainly We wrote in the Book after the reminder that (as for) the land, My righteous servants shall inherit it.

105.author: Wahiduddin Khan
105.text: We have already written in the Psalms following the Reminder, "My righteous servants shall inherit the earth."

105.author: Yusuf Ali
105.text: Before this We wrote in the Psalms, after the Message (given to Moses): My servants the righteous, shall inherit the earth."

105.author: Transliteration
105.text: Walaqad katabna fee alzzaboori min baAAdi alththikri anna alarda yarithuha AAibadiya alssalihoona

106.author: Ahmed Ali
106.text: Verily there is a message in this for people who are devout.

106.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
106.text: This Qur’an is sufficient for people who are devout.

106.author: Arberry
106.text: Surely in this is a Message delivered unto a people who serve.

106.author: Daryabadi
106.text: Verily in this in a preaching for a people who are worshippers.

106.author: Hilali & Khan
106.text: Verily, in this (the Quran) there is a plain Message for people who worship Allah (i.e. the true, real believers of Islamic Monotheism who act practically on the Quran and the Sunnah legal ways of the Prophet SAW).

106.author: Itani
106.text: Indeed, in this is a message for people who worship.

106.author: Maududi
106.text: Herein, surely, is a message for those devoted to worship.

106.author: Mubarakpuri
106.text: Verily, in this there is a plain Message for people who worship Allah.

106.author: Pickthall
106.text: Lo! there is a plain statement for folk who are devout.

106.author: Qarai
106.text: There is indeed in this a proclamation for a devout people.

106.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
106.text: Surely, in this is a proclamation to a worshiping nation.

106.author: Saheeh International
106.text: Indeed, in this [Qur'an] is notification for a worshipping people.

106.author: Sarwar
106.text: This is a lesson for those who worship (God).

106.author: Shakir
106.text: Most surely in this is a message to a people who serve

106.author: Wahiduddin Khan
106.text: Herein, surely is a message for true worshippers.

106.author: Yusuf Ali
106.text: Verily in this (Qur'an) is a Message for people who would (truly) worship Allah.

106.author: Transliteration
106.text: Inna fee hatha labalaghan liqawmin AAabideena

107.author: Ahmed Ali
107.text: We have sent you as a benevolence to the creatures of the world.

107.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
107.text: And We did not send you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) except as a mercy for the entire world. (Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him – is the Prophet towards all mankind.)

107.author: Arberry
107.text: We have not sent thee, save as a mercy unto all beings.

107.author: Daryabadi
107.text: And We have not sent thee except as a mercy unto the Worlds.

107.author: Hilali & Khan
107.text: And We have sent you (O Muhammad SAW) not but as a mercy for the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

107.author: Itani
107.text: We did not send you except as mercy to mankind.

107.author: Maududi
107.text: We have sent you forth as nothing but mercy to people of the whole world.

107.author: Mubarakpuri
107.text: And We have sent you (O Muhammad) not but as a mercy for the `Alamin.

107.author: Pickthall
107.text: We sent thee not save as a mercy for the peoples.

107.author: Qarai
107.text: We did not send you but as a mercy to all the nations.

107.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
107.text: We have not sent you (Prophet Muhammad) except as a mercy to all the worlds.

107.author: Saheeh International
107.text: And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a mercy to the worlds.

107.author: Sarwar
107.text: (Muhammad), We have sent you for no other reason but to be a mercy for mankind.

107.author: Shakir
107.text: And We have not sent you but as a mercy to the worlds.

107.author: Wahiduddin Khan
107.text: We have sent you forth as a mercy to all mankind.

107.author: Yusuf Ali
107.text: We sent thee not, but as a Mercy for all creatures.

108.author: Ahmed Ali
108.text: Say: "This is what has been revealed to me: 'Your God is one and only God.' So will you bow in homage to Him?"

108.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
108.text: Proclaim, “It is divinely revealed to me that your God is the only One God – Allah; do you therefore become Muslims?”

108.author: Arberry
108.text: Say: 'It is revealed unto me only that your God is One God; do you then surrender?'

108.author: Daryabadi
108.text: Say thou: this only hath been revealed unto me, that your god is only One God. submit ye then?!

108.author: Hilali & Khan
108.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "It is revealed to me that your Ilah (God) is only one Ilah (God - Allah). Will you then submit to His Will (become Muslims and stop worshipping others besides Allah)?"

108.author: Itani
108.text: Say, “It is revealed to me that your God is One God. Are you going to submit?”

108.author: Maududi
108.text: Say: "It is revealed to me that your God is only One God. Will you, then, submit to Him?"

108.author: Mubarakpuri
108.text: Say: "It is revealed to me that your God is only one God. Will you then be Muslims"

108.author: Pickthall
108.text: Say: It is only inspired in me that your Allah is One Allah. Will ye then surrender (unto Him)?

108.author: Qarai
108.text: Say, ‘It has been revealed to me that your God is the One God. So will you submit?’

108.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
108.text: Say: 'It is revealed to me that your God is One God, do you then surrender'

108.author: Saheeh International
108.text: Say, "It is only revealed to me that your god is but one God; so will you be Muslims [in submission to Him]?"

108.author: Sarwar
108.text: Say, "It is revealed to me that there is only one Lord. Will you then submit yourselves to His will?"

108.author: Shakir
108.text: Say: It is only revealed to me that your Allah is one Allah; will you then submit?

108.author: Wahiduddin Khan
108.text: Say, "It has been revealed to me that your God is but One God. Will you then submit to Him?"

108.author: Yusuf Ali
108.text: Say: "What has come to me by inspiration is that your Allah is One Allah: will ye therefore bow to His Will (in Islam)?"

108.author: Transliteration
108.text: Qul innama yooha ilayya annama ilahukum ilahun wahidun fahal antum muslimoona

109.author: Ahmed Ali
109.text: If they turn away, tell them: "I have warned you all alike. I do not know if what has been promised you is near or far away.

109.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
109.text: Then if they turn away, proclaim, “I have proclaimed a war against you on equal terms; and what do I know whether the promise which is given to you, is close or far?”

109.author: Arberry
109.text: Then, if they should turn their backs, say: 'I have proclaimed to you all equally, even though I know not whether near or far is that you are promised.'

109.author: Daryabadi
109.text: Then if they turn away, say thou: I have proclaimed unto you all alike, and I know not whether nigh or far is that which ye are promised,

109.author: Hilali & Khan
109.text: But if they (disbelievers, idolaters, Jews, Christians, polytheists, etc.) turn away (from Islamic Monotheism) say (to them O Muhammad SAW): "I give you a notice (of war as) to be known to us all alike. And I know not whether that which you are promised (i.e. the torment or the Day of Resurrection) is near or far."

109.author: Itani
109.text: But if they turn away, say, “I have informed you sufficiently. Although I do not know whether what you are promised is near or far.”

109.author: Maududi
109.text: If they turn away, say to them: "I have warned you all alike; and I cannot say whether what you have been promised is near or distant.

109.author: Mubarakpuri
109.text: But if they turn away say: "I give you a notice to be known to us all alike. And I know not whether that which you are promised is near or far."

109.author: Pickthall
109.text: But if they are averse, then say: I have warned you all alike, although I know not whether nigh or far is that which ye are promised.

109.author: Qarai
109.text: But if they turn away, say, ‘I have proclaimed to you all alike, and I do not know whether what you have been promised is far or near.

109.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
109.text: If they turn back, say: 'I have warned you all alike, though I cannot tell whether what you are promised is imminent or distant.

109.author: Saheeh International
109.text: But if they turn away, then say, "I have announced to [all of] you equally. And I know not whether near or far is that which you are promised.

109.author: Sarwar
109.text: If they turn away, tell them, "I have warned every one of you equally. I do not know when the torment which you have to suffer will take place.

109.author: Shakir
109.text: But if they turn back, say: I have given you warning in fairness and I do not know whether what you are threatened with is near or far;

109.author: Wahiduddin Khan
109.text: If they turn away, say, "I have warned you all alike, though I do not know whether [the scourge] which you are promised is near at hand or far off.

109.author: Yusuf Ali
109.text: But if they turn back, Say: "I have proclaimed the Message to you all alike and in truth; but I know not whether that which ye are promised is near or far.

109.author: Transliteration
109.text: Fain tawallaw faqul athantukum AAala sawain wain adree aqareebun am baAAeedun ma tooAAadoona

110.author: Ahmed Ali
110.text: He knows surely what you say aloud, and what you hide within your breasts.

110.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
110.text: “Indeed Allah knows whatever is said, and knows all what you conceal.”

110.author: Arberry
110.text: Surely He knows what is spoken aloud and He knows what you hide.

110.author: Daryabadi
110.text: Verily He knoweth that which is public in speech, and He knoweth that which ye hide.

110.author: Hilali & Khan
110.text: (Say O Muhammad SAW) Verily, He (Allah) knows that which is spoken aloud (openly) and that which you conceal.

110.author: Itani
110.text: He knows what is said openly, and He knows what you conceal.

110.author: Maududi
110.text: Indeed He knows what you say loudly and what you hide.

110.author: Mubarakpuri
110.text: "Verily, He knows that which is spoken aloud and He knows that which you conceal."

110.author: Pickthall
110.text: Lo! He knoweth that which is said openly, and that which ye conceal.

110.author: Qarai
110.text: Indeed He knows whatever is spoken aloud, and He knows whatever you conceal.

110.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
110.text: He knows your spoken words and what you hide.

110.author: Saheeh International
110.text: Indeed, He knows what is declared of speech, and He knows what you conceal.

110.author: Sarwar
110.text: God knows well all that is spoken aloud and all that you hide.

110.author: Shakir
110.text: Surely He knows what is spoken openly and He knows what you hide;

110.author: Wahiduddin Khan
110.text: God surely knows what you say openly and also knows what you conceal.

110.author: Yusuf Ali
110.text: "It is He Who knows what is open in speech and what ye hide (in your hearts).

110.author: Transliteration
110.text: Innahu yaAAlamu aljahra mina alqawli wayaAAlamu ma taktumoona

111.author: Ahmed Ali
111.text: I do not know if this be a trial for you, or a little advantage for a while."

111.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
111.text: “And what do I know – it may be a trial for you, and an enjoyment for a time.”

111.author: Arberry
111.text: I know not; haply it is a trial for you and an enjoyment for a time.

111.author: Daryabadi
111.text: And know not; haply it may be a trial for you, and an enjoyment for a season.

111.author: Hilali & Khan
111.text: And I know not, perhaps it may be a trial for you, and an enjoyment for a while.

111.author: Itani
111.text: “And I do not know whether it is perhaps a trial for you, and an enjoyment for a while.”

111.author: Maududi
111.text: I think that this [reprieve] is possibly a trial for you, an opportunity to enjoy yourselves until an appointed time."

111.author: Mubarakpuri
111.text: "And I know not, perhaps it may be a trial for you, and an enjoyment for a while."

111.author: Pickthall
111.text: And I know not but that this may be a trial for you, and enjoyment for a while.

111.author: Qarai
111.text: I do not know—maybe it is a trial for you and an enjoyment for a while.’

111.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
111.text: And I do not know if this is a trial for you and an enjoyment for a time'

111.author: Saheeh International
111.text: And I know not; perhaps it is a trial for you and enjoyment for a time."

111.author: Sarwar
111.text: I do not know (why God has commanded me to warn you of the torment). Perhaps it is a trial for you and a respite for an appointed time".

111.author: Shakir
111.text: And I do not know if this may be a trial for you and a provision till a time.

111.author: Wahiduddin Khan
111.text: Nor do I know whether it may mean a trial for you and a short reprieve."

111.author: Yusuf Ali
111.text: "I know not but that it may be a trial for you, and a grant of (worldly) livelihood (to you) for a time."

111.author: Transliteration
111.text: Wain adree laAAallahu fitnatun lakum wamataAAun ila heenin

112.author: Ahmed Ali
112.text: "Judge in truth (between us), O Lord," he said. "Our Lord is merciful, whose help we seek against what you attribute."

112.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
112.text: And the Prophet submitted, “My Lord – render the true judgement”; “And only the help of Our Lord, the Most Gracious, is sought against all what you fabricate.”

112.author: Arberry
112.text: He said: 'My Lord, judge Thou with truth! And our Lord is the All-merciful; His succour is ever to be sought against that you describe.'

112.author: Daryabadi
112.text: He saith: my Lord! judge Thou with truth. And our Lord is the Compassionate Whose help is to be sought against that which ye utter.

112.author: Hilali & Khan
112.text: He (Muhammad SAW) said:"My Lord! Judge You in truth! Our Lord is the Most Beneficent, Whose Help is to be sought against that which you attribute (unto Allah that He has offspring, and unto Muhammad SAW that he is a sorcerer, and unto the Quran that it is poetry, etc.)!"

112.author: Itani
112.text: He said, “My Lord, judge with justice.” And, “Our Lord is the Gracious, Whose help is sought against what you allege.”

112.author: Maududi
112.text: The Messenger said: "My Lord! Judge with truth. Our Compassionate Lord alone is our support against your (blasphemous) utterances."

112.author: Mubarakpuri
112.text: He said:"My Lord! Judge You in truth! Our Lord is the Most Gracious, Whose help is to be sought against that which you attribute!"

112.author: Pickthall
112.text: He saith: My Lord! Judge Thou with truth. Our Lord is the Beneficent, Whose help is to be implored against that which ye ascribe (unto Him).

112.author: Qarai
112.text: He said, ‘My Lord! Judge [between us and the polytheists] with justice.’ ‘Our Lord is the All-beneficent; [He is our] resort against what you allege.’

112.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
112.text: He said: 'My Lord, judge in truth. Our Lord is the Merciful whose help is ever to be sought against what you describe'

112.author: Saheeh International
112.text: [The Prophet] has said, "My Lord, judge [between us] in truth. And our Lord is the Most Merciful, the one whose help is sought against that which you describe."

112.author: Sarwar
112.text: He also said, "Lord, judge (us) with Truth. Our Lord is the Beneficent One whose help I ask against the blasphemies you say about Him".

112.author: Shakir
112.text: He said: O my Lord! judge Thou with truth; and our Lord is the Beneficent Allah, Whose help is sought against what you ascribe (to Him).

112.author: Wahiduddin Khan
112.text: Say, "My Lord, judge with truth. Our Lord is the Gracious One whose help we seek against what you utter."

112.author: Yusuf Ali
112.text: Say: "O my Lord! judge Thou in truth!" "Our Lord Most Gracious is the One Whose assistance should be sought against the blasphemies ye utter!"

112.author: Transliteration
112.text: Qala rabbi ohkum bialhaqqi warabbuna alrrahmanu almustaAAanu AAala ma tasifoona
